filterCols 
Frame.filterColValues 
filterColValues 
Frame.mapCols 
mapCols 
Frame.mapColValues 
mapColValues 
Frame.mapColValuesAs 
mapColValuesAs 
Frame.mapColKeys 
mapColKeys 
Frame.mapValues 
mapValues 
Frame.map 
map 
Frame.reduceValues 
reduceValues 
Frame.maxRowBy 
maxRowBy 
Frame.minRowBy 
minRowBy 
Frame.shift 
shift 
Frame.diff 
diff 
Frame.pctChange 
pctChange 
Frame.tryMapRows 
tryMapRows 
Frame.tryValues 
tryValues 
Frame.fillErrorsWith 
fillErrorsWith 
Frame.fillMissingWith 
fillMissingWith 
Frame.fillMissing 
fillMissing 
Frame.fillMissingUsing 
fillMissingUsing 
Frame.dropSparseRows 
dropSparseRows 
Frame.dropSparseRowsBy 
dropSparseRowsBy 
Frame.dropEmptyRows 
dropEmptyRows 
Frame.dropSparseCols 
dropSparseCols 
Frame.dropEmptyCols 
dropEmptyCols 
Frame.denseCols 
denseCols 
Frame.denseRows 
denseRows 
Frame.join 
join 
Frame.joinAlign 
joinAlign 
Frame.joinOn 
joinOn 
Frame.joinOnString 
joinOnString 
Frame.joinOnInt 
joinOnInt 
Frame.mergeAll 
mergeAll 
Frame.merge 
merge 
Frame.interleave 
interleave 
Frame.zipAlign 
zipAlign 
Frame.zip 
zip 
Frame.strConcat 
strConcat 
Frame.compare 
compare 
Frame.reduceLevel 
reduceLevel 
Frame.applyLevel 
applyLevel 
Frame.nest 
nest 
Frame.nestBy 
nestBy 
Frame.nestRowsBy 
nestRowsBy 
Frame.unnest 
unnest 
Frame.toJson 
toJson 
Frame.saveJson 
saveJson

### [Frame.empty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#empty)

Frame.empty 
empty 

 Returns an empty data frame with no rows or columns.

### [Frame.countRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#countRows)

Frame.countRows 
countRows 

 Returns the total number of row keys in the specified frame. This returns
 the total length of the row series, including keys for which there is no
 value available.

### [Frame.countCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#countCols)

Frame.countCols 
countCols 

 Returns the total number of column keys in the specified frame. This returns
 the total length of columns, including keys for which there is no
 data available.

### [Frame.countValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#countValues)

Frame.countValues 
countValues 

 Returns a series with the total number of values in each column. This counts
 the number of actual values, excluding the missing values or not available
 values (such as `nan`, `null`, etc.)

### [Frame.cols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#cols)

Frame.cols 
cols 

 Returns the columns of the data frame as a series (indexed by
 the column keys of the source frame) containing untyped series representing
 individual columns of the frame.

### [Frame.getCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#getCol)

Frame.getCol 
getCol 

 Returns a specified column from a data frame. This function uses exact matching
 semantics on the key. Use `lookupCol` if you want to use inexact
 matching (e.g. on dates)

### [Frame.getCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#getCols)

Frame.getCols 
getCols 

 Returns a series of columns of the data frame indexed by the column keys,
 which contains those series whose values are convertible to <code>'T</code>, and with
 missing values where the conversion fails.

 If you want to get numeric columns, you can use a simpler `numericCols` function
 instead. Note that this function typically requires a type annotation. This can
 be specified in various ways, for example by annotating the result value:

     let (res:<code>Series<_, Series<_, string>></code>) = frame |> getCols

 Here, the annotation on the values of the nested series specifies that we want
 to get columns containing `string` values.

### [Frame.getNumericCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#getNumericCols)

Frame.getNumericCols 
getNumericCols 

 Returns a series of columns of the data frame indexed by the column keys,
 which contains those series whose values are convertible to float, and with
 missing values where the conversion fails.

### [Frame.rows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rows)

Frame.rows 
rows 
 Returns the rows of the data frame as a series (indexed by
 the row keys of the source frame) containing untyped series representing
 individual row of the frame.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.getRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#getRow)

Frame.getRow 
getRow 
 Returns a specified row from a data frame. This function uses exact matching
 semantics on the key. Use `lookupRow` if you want to use inexact matching
 (e.g. on dates)

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.getRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#getRows)

Frame.getRows 
getRows 
 Returns a series of rows of the data frame indexed by the row keys,
 which contains those rows whose values are convertible to 'T, and with
 missing values where the conversion fails.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.lookupCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#lookupCol)

Frame.lookupCol 
lookupCol 
 Returns a specified series (column) from a data frame. If the data frame has
 ordered column index, the lookup semantics can be used to get series
 with nearest greater/smaller key. For exact semantics, you can use `getCol`.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.tryLookupCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryLookupCol)

Frame.tryLookupCol 
tryLookupCol 
 Returns a specified series (column) from a data frame, or missing value if
 column doesn't exist.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.tryLookupColObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryLookupColObservation)

Frame.tryLookupColObservation 
tryLookupColObservation 
 Returns a specified key and series (column) from a data frame, or missing value if
 doesn't exist.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.lookupRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#lookupRow)

Frame.lookupRow 
lookupRow 
 Returns a specified row from a data frame. If the data frame has
 ordered row index, the lookup semantics can be used to get row with
 nearest greater/smaller key. For exact semantics, you can use `getRow`.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.tryLookupRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryLookupRow)

Frame.tryLookupRow 
tryLookupRow 
 Returns a specified series (row) from a data frame, or missing value if
 row doesn't exit.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.tryLookupRowObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryLookupRowObservation)

Frame.tryLookupRowObservation 
tryLookupRowObservation 
 Returns a specified series (row) and key from a data frame, or missing value if
 row doesn't exit.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.sliceCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sliceCols)

Frame.sliceCols 
sliceCols 
 Returns a frame consisting of the specified columns from the original
 data frame. The function uses exact key matching semantics.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.sliceRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sliceRows)

Frame.sliceRows 
sliceRows 
 Returns a frame consisting of the specified rows from the original
 data frame. The function uses exact key matching semantics.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.slice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#slice)

Frame.slice 
slice 
 Returns a frame consisting of the specified columns and rows from the original
 data frame. The function uses exact key matching semantics.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.ilocRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#ilocRows)

Frame.ilocRows 
ilocRows 

 Returns a frame consisting of the rows at the specified integer (zero-based) positions
 from the original data frame. Equivalent to pandas <code>iloc</code> row selection.

### [Frame.ilocCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#ilocCols)

Frame.ilocCols 
ilocCols 

 Returns a frame consisting of the columns at the specified integer (zero-based) positions
 from the original data frame. Equivalent to pandas <code>iloc</code> column selection.

### [Frame.iloc](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#iloc)

Frame.iloc 
iloc 

 Returns a sub-frame containing the rows and columns at the specified integer (zero-based)
 positions from the original data frame. Equivalent to pandas <code>DataFrame.iloc[rows, cols]</code>.

### [Frame.rowsAfter](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rowsAfter)

Frame.rowsAfter 
rowsAfter 

 Returns a new frame containing all rows with row keys strictly greater than
 <code>lowerExclusive</code>.

### [Frame.rowsBefore](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rowsBefore)

Frame.rowsBefore 
rowsBefore 

 Returns a new frame containing all rows with row keys strictly less than
 <code>upperExclusive</code>.

### [Frame.rowsStartAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rowsStartAt)

Frame.rowsStartAt 
rowsStartAt 

 Returns a new frame containing all rows with row keys greater than or equal to
 <code>lowerInclusive</code>.

### [Frame.rowsEndAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rowsEndAt)

Frame.rowsEndAt 
rowsEndAt 

 Returns a new frame containing all rows with row keys less than or equal to
 <code>upperInclusive</code>.

### [Frame.rowsBetween](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rowsBetween)

Frame.rowsBetween 
rowsBetween 

 Returns a new frame containing all rows with row keys in the range
 [<code>lowerInclusive</code>, <code>upperInclusive</code>] (both bounds inclusive).

### [Frame.addCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#addCol)

Frame.addCol 
addCol 

 Creates a new data frame that contains all data from
 the original data frame, together with an additional series.
 The operation uses left join and aligns new series to the
 existing frame keys.

### [Frame.dropCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropCol)

Frame.dropCol 
dropCol 

 Creates a new data frame that contains all data from the original
 data frame without the specified series (column). The operation throws
 if the column key is not found.

### [Frame.replaceCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#replaceCol)

Frame.replaceCol 
replaceCol 

 Creates a new data frame where the specified column is replaced
 with a new series. (If the series does not exist, only the new
 series is added.)

### [Frame.toArray2D](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#toArray2D)

Frame.toArray2D 
toArray2D 
 Returns data of the data frame as a 2D array containing data as `float` values.
 Missing data are represented as `Double.NaN` in the returned array.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.toJaggedArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#toJaggedArray)

Frame.toJaggedArray 
toJaggedArray 
 Returns data of the data frame as a jagged array containing data as `float` values.
 Missing data are represented as `Double.NaN` in the returned array.

 <category>Accessing frame data and lookup</category>

### [Frame.groupRowsUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsUsing)

Frame.groupRowsUsing 
groupRowsUsing 

 Group rows of a data frame using the specified `selector`. The selector is called with
 a row key and object series representing the row and should return a new key. The result
 is a frame with multi-level index, where the first level is formed by the newly created
 keys.

### [Frame.groupRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsBy)

Frame.groupRowsBy 
groupRowsBy 

 Group rows of a data frame using the specified `column`. The type of the column is inferred
 from the usage of the resulting frame. The result is a frame with multi-level index, where
 the first level is formed by the newly created keys. Use `groupRowsBy[Int|String|...]` to
 explicitly specify the type of the column.

### [Frame.groupRowsByObj](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsByObj)

Frame.groupRowsByObj 
groupRowsByObj 
 Groups the rows of a frame by a specified column in the same way as `groupRowsBy`.
 This function assumes that the values of the specified column are of type `obj`.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.groupRowsByInt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsByInt)

Frame.groupRowsByInt 
groupRowsByInt 
 Groups the rows of a frame by a specified column in the same way as `groupRowsBy`.
 This function assumes that the values of the specified column are of type `int`.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.groupRowsByString](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsByString)

Frame.groupRowsByString 
groupRowsByString 
 Groups the rows of a frame by a specified column in the same way as `groupRowsBy`.
 This function assumes that the values of the specified column are of type `string`.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.groupRowsByBool](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsByBool)

Frame.groupRowsByBool 
groupRowsByBool 
 Groups the rows of a frame by a specified column in the same way as `groupRowsBy`.
 This function assumes that the values of the specified column are of type `bool`.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.groupRowsByIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#groupRowsByIndex)

Frame.groupRowsByIndex 
groupRowsByIndex 
 Group rows of a data frame using the specified `keySelector`. The selector is called with
 a key of each row and should return a new key. The result is a frame with multi-level index,
 here the first level is formed by the newly created keys.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.aggregateRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#aggregateRowsBy)

Frame.aggregateRowsBy 
aggregateRowsBy 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are grouped by `groupBy` and whose columns specified
 in `aggBy` are aggregated according to `aggFunc`.

### [Frame.pivotTable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#pivotTable)

Frame.pivotTable 
pivotTable 

 Creates a new data frame resulting from a 'pivot' operation. Consider a denormalized data
 frame representing a table: column labels are field names & table values are observations
 of those fields. pivotTable buckets the rows along two axes, according to the results of
 the functions `rowGrp` and `colGrp`; and then computes a value for the frame of rows that
 land in each bucket.

### [Frame.window](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#window)

Frame.window 
window 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size. The result is a series
 containing data frames that represent individual windows.
 This function skips incomplete chunks.

### [Frame.windowInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#windowInto)

Frame.windowInto 
windowInto 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size and then applies the provided
 value selector `f` on each window to produce the result which is returned as a new series.
 This function skips incomplete chunks.

### [Frame.melt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#melt)

Frame.melt 
melt 
 Returns a data frame with three columns named `Row`, `Column`
 and `Value` that contains the data of the original data frame
 in individual rows.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category>

### [Frame.meltBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#meltBy)

Frame.meltBy 
meltBy 
<pre> Similar to `melt`, but keeps the specified identity columns as-is and melts
 the remaining columns into `Column` and `Value` pairs. This is analogous to
 pandas `DataFrame.melt(id_vars=...)` or R's `data.table::melt`.

 ## Parameters
  - `idCols` - The columns to use as identity variables (kept in the output)
  - `frame` - The input data frame

 ## Returns
 A data frame with the identity columns followed by `Column` and `Value` columns.
 The row index is a sequential integer starting from 0.

 <category>Grouping, windowing and chunking</category></pre>

### [Frame.unmelt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#unmelt)

Frame.unmelt 
unmelt 

 This function is the opposite of <code>melt</code>. It takes a data frame
 with three columns named <code>Row</code>, <code>Column</code> and <code>Value</code> and reconstructs
 a data frame by using <code>Row</code> and <code>Column</code> as row and column index keys,
 respectively.

### [Frame.stack](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#stack)

Frame.stack 
stack 

 Converts a data frame to a long-format frame by combining the row index and the
 column index into a 2-tuple row index. Each cell of the original frame becomes a row
 in the result keyed by <code>(rowKey, columnKey)</code>, with a single column named
 <code>"Value"</code>. Missing values are dropped.
  

 This is the pandas-style <code>DataFrame.stack()</code> operation. The inverse operation
 is <code>Frame.unstack</code>. To keep row/column keys in separate named columns instead,
 use <code>Frame.melt</code>.

### [Frame.unstack](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#unstack)

Frame.unstack 
unstack 

 Converts a data frame with a 2-tuple row index into a wide-format frame by promoting
 the inner (second) element of each row key to the column index. The resulting frame has
 row keys equal to the unique first tuple elements and column keys of the form
 <code>(originalColumnKey, innerRowKey)</code>. Cells with no corresponding entry in the
 input are represented as missing values.
  

 This is the pandas-style <code>DataFrame.unstack()</code> operation and is the inverse of
 <code>Frame.stack</code> (modulo column-key wrapping when the input has more than one column).

### [Frame.realignRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#realignRows)

Frame.realignRows 
realignRows 

 Align the existing data to a specified collection of row keys. Values in the data frame
 that do not match any new key are dropped, new keys (that were not in the original data
 frame) are assigned missing values.

### [Frame.indexRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRows)

Frame.indexRows 
indexRows 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. The generic type parameter is specifies the type of the values in the required
 index column (and usually needs to be specified using a type annotation). The specified
 column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsObj](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsObj)

Frame.indexRowsObj 
indexRowsObj 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `obj`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsInt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsInt)

Frame.indexRowsInt 
indexRowsInt 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `int`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsDate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsDate)

Frame.indexRowsDate 
indexRowsDate 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `DateTime`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsDateTime](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsDateTime)

Frame.indexRowsDateTime 
indexRowsDateTime 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `DateTime`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsDateOffs](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsDateOffs)

Frame.indexRowsDateOffs 
indexRowsDateOffs 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `DateTimeOffset`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsDateTimeOffset](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsDateTimeOffset)

Frame.indexRowsDateTimeOffset 
indexRowsDateTimeOffset 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `DateTimeOffset`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsString](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsString)

Frame.indexRowsString 
indexRowsString 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. This function casts (or converts) the column key to values of type `string`
 (a generic variant that may require some type annotation is `Frame.indexRows`)
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.indexColsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexColsWith)

Frame.indexColsWith 
indexColsWith 

 Replace the column index of the frame with the provided sequence of column keys.
 The columns of the frame are assigned keys according to the provided order.
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.renameCol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#renameCol)

Frame.renameCol 
renameCol 

 Rename a single column of the data frame. Returns a new frame; does not mutate the
 original. If <code>oldKey</code> is not found, the frame is returned unchanged.

### [Frame.renameColsUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#renameColsUsing)

Frame.renameColsUsing 
renameColsUsing 

 Rename all columns of the data frame by applying the specified mapping function.
 Returns a new frame; does not mutate the original.

### [Frame.indexRowsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsWith)

Frame.indexRowsWith 
indexRowsWith 

 Replace the row index of the frame with the provided sequence of row keys.
 The rows of the frame are assigned keys according to the provided order.

### [Frame.indexRowsOrdinally](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsOrdinally)

Frame.indexRowsOrdinally 
indexRowsOrdinally 
 Replace the row index of the frame with ordinarilly generated integers starting from zero.
 The rows of the frame are assigned index according to the current order, or in a
 non-deterministic way, if the current row index is not ordered.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.indexRowsUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsUsing)

Frame.indexRowsUsing 
indexRowsUsing 

 Replace the row index of the frame with a sequence of row keys generated using
 a function invoked on each row.

### [Frame.transpose](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#transpose)

Frame.transpose 
transpose 

 Returns a transposed data frame. The rows of the original data frame are used as the
 columns of the new one (and vice versa). Use this operation if you have a data frame
 and you mostly need to access its rows as a series (because accessing columns as a
 series is more efficient).

### [Frame.sortRowsByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sortRowsByKey)

Frame.sortRowsByKey 
sortRowsByKey 
 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are an ordered series. This allows using operations that are
 only available on indexed series such as alignment and inexact lookup.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.sortRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sortRows)

Frame.sortRows 
sortRows 
 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are ordered on a particular column of the frame.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.sortRowsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sortRowsWith)

Frame.sortRowsWith 
sortRowsWith 
 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are ordered on a particular column of the frame.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.sortRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sortRowsBy)

Frame.sortRowsBy 
sortRowsBy 
 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are ordered on a particular column of the frame.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.rankRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#rankRowsBy)

Frame.rankRowsBy 
rankRowsBy 

 Returns a series containing the dense rank of each row in the data frame, ordered
 by the values in the specified column. The rank is 1-based: the row with the smallest
 column value receives rank 1. Rows with equal values receive the same rank. Rows where
 the specified column has a missing value receive a missing rank.

### [Frame.sortColsByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#sortColsByKey)

Frame.sortColsByKey 
sortColsByKey 
 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose columns are an ordered series. This allows using operations that are
 only available on indexed series such as alignment and inexact lookup.

 <category>Sorting and index manipulation</category>

### [Frame.expandAllCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#expandAllCols)

Frame.expandAllCols 
expandAllCols 

 Creates a new data frame where all columns are expanded based on runtime
 structure of the objects they store. The expansion is performed recrusively
 to the specified depth. A column can be expanded if it is <code>Series<string, T></code>
 or <code>IDictionary<K, V></code> or if it is any .NET object with readable
 properties.

### [Frame.expandCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#expandCols)

Frame.expandCols 
expandCols 

 Creates a new data frame where the specified columns are expanded based on runtime
 structure of the objects they store. A column can be expanded if it is
 <code>Series<string, T></code> or <code>IDictionary<K, V></code> or if it is any .NET object with readable
 properties.
  
<example>
 Given a data frame with a series that contains tuples, you can expand the
 tuple members and get a frame with columns `S.Item1` and `S.Item2`:
 <code>
 let df = frame [ "S" => series [ 1 => (1, "One"); 2 => (2, "Two") ] ]
 df |> Frame.expandCols ["S"]
 </code></example>

### [Frame.take](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#take)

Frame.take 
take 
 Returns a frame that contains the specified `count` of rows from the
 original frame; `count` must be smaller or equal to the original number of rows.

 <category>Frame transformations</category>

### [Frame.takeLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#takeLast)

Frame.takeLast 
takeLast 
 Returns a frame that contains the specified `count` of rows from the
 original frame. The rows are taken from the end of the frame; `count`
 must be smaller or equal to the original number of rows.

 <category>Frame transformations</category>

### [Frame.skip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#skip)

Frame.skip 
skip 
 Returns a frame that contains the data from the original frame,
 except for the first `count` rows; `count` must be smaller or equal
 to the original number of rows.

 <category>Frame transformations</category>

### [Frame.skipLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#skipLast)

Frame.skipLast 
skipLast 
 Returns a frame that contains the data from the original frame,
 except for the last `count` rows; `count` must be smaller or equal to the
 original number of rows.

 <category>Frame transformations</category>

### [Frame.filterRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterRows)

Frame.filterRows 
filterRows 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame
 for which the specified predicate returns `true`. The predicate is called
 with the row key and object series that represents the row data.

### [Frame.filterRowValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterRowValues)

Frame.filterRowValues 
filterRowValues 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame
 for which the specified predicate returns `true`. The predicate is called
 with an object series that represents the row data (use `filterRows`
 if you need to access the row key).

### [Frame.filterRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterRowsBy)

Frame.filterRowsBy 
filterRowsBy 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame
 for which the specified `column` has the specified `value`. The operation
 may be implemented via an index for virtualized Deedle frames.

### [Frame.filterRowsByMask](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterRowsByMask)

Frame.filterRowsByMask 
filterRowsByMask 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame whose
 corresponding value in the boolean mask series is <code>true</code>. Rows whose key
 is missing from the mask are excluded. This enables pandas-style boolean indexing.

### [Frame.filterColsByMask](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterColsByMask)

Frame.filterColsByMask 
filterColsByMask 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the columns of the input frame whose
 corresponding value in the boolean mask series is <code>true</code>. Columns whose key
 is missing from the mask are excluded. This enables pandas-style boolean indexing.

### [Frame.distinctRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#distinctRowsBy)

Frame.distinctRowsBy 
distinctRowsBy 
<pre> Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame that have
 distinct values in the specified columns. When multiple rows have the same values
 in those columns, only the first row (in index order) is preserved.

 ## Parameters
  - `columns` - A sequence of column keys used to determine row uniqueness
  - `frame` - Input data frame to be transformed

 ## Example

     // Keep only the first row for each unique combination of "Category" and "Region"
     df |> Frame.distinctRowsBy ["Category"; "Region"]

 [category:Frame transformations]</pre>

### [Frame.mapRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapRows)

Frame.mapRows 
mapRows 

 Builds a new data frame whose rows are the results of applying the specified
 function on the rows of the input data frame. The function is called
 with the row key and object series that represents the row data.

### [Frame.mapRowValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapRowValues)

Frame.mapRowValues 
mapRowValues 

 Builds a new data frame whose rows are the results of applying the specified
 function on the rows of the input data frame. The function is called
 with an object series that represents the row data (use `mapRows`
 if you need to access the row key).

### [Frame.mapRowKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapRowKeys)

Frame.mapRowKeys 
mapRowKeys 

 Builds a new data frame whose row keys are the results of applying the
 specified function on the row keys of the original data frame.

### [Frame.indexRowsApply](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#indexRowsApply)

Frame.indexRowsApply 
indexRowsApply 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame, with each index value transformed by the given function. This is useful when
 the column contains values that need to be converted or parsed into the desired key type
 (for example, parsing date strings from a CSV file).
 The specified column is removed from the resulting frame.

### [Frame.filterCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterCols)

Frame.filterCols 
filterCols 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the columns of the input frame
 for which the specified predicate returns `true`. The predicate is called
 with the column key and object series that represents the column data.

### [Frame.filterColValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#filterColValues)

Frame.filterColValues 
filterColValues 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the columns of the input frame
 for which the specified predicate returns `true`. The predicate is called
 with an object series that represents the column data (use `filterCols`
 if you need to access the column key).

### [Frame.mapCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapCols)

Frame.mapCols 
mapCols 

 Builds a new data frame whose columns are the results of applying the specified
 function on the columns of the input data frame. The function is called
 with the column key and object series that represents the column data.

### [Frame.mapColValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapColValues)

Frame.mapColValues 
mapColValues 

 Builds a new data frame whose columns are the results of applying the specified
 function on the columns of the input data frame. The function is called
 with an object series that represents the column data (use `mapCols`
 if you need to access the column key).

### [Frame.mapColValuesAs](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapColValuesAs)

Frame.mapColValuesAs 
mapColValuesAs 

 Builds a new data frame whose columns are the results of applying the specified
 function on the typed columns of the input data frame. Unlike <code>mapColValues</code>,
 this function avoids the boxing overhead of <code>ObjectSeries</code> by operating directly
 on columns converted to the specified type <code>'T</code>. Columns that cannot be converted
 to <code>'T</code> are silently dropped.

### [Frame.mapColKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapColKeys)

Frame.mapColKeys 
mapColKeys 

 Builds a new data frame whose column keys are the results of applying the
 specified function on the column keys of the original data frame.

### [Frame.mapValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mapValues)

Frame.mapValues 
mapValues 

 Builds a new data frame whose values are the results of applying the specified
 function on these values, but only for those columns which can be converted
 to the appropriate type for input to the mapping function (use `map` if you need
 to access the row and column keys).

### [Frame.map](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#map)

Frame.map 
map 

 Builds a new data frame whose values are the results of applying the specified
 function on these values, but only for those columns which can be converted
 to the appropriate type for input to the mapping function.

### [Frame.reduceValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#reduceValues)

Frame.reduceValues 
reduceValues 

 Returns a series that contains the results of aggregating each column
 to a single value. The function takes columns that can be converted to
 the type expected by the specified `op` function and reduces the values
 in each column using `Series.reduceValues`.

### [Frame.maxRowBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#maxRowBy)

Frame.maxRowBy 
maxRowBy 

 Returns a row of the data frame which has the greatest value of the
 specified `column`. The row is returned as an optional value (which is
 `None` for empty frame) and contains a key together with an object
 series representing the row.

### [Frame.minRowBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#minRowBy)

Frame.minRowBy 
minRowBy 

 Returns a row of the data frame which has the smallest value of the
 specified `column`. The row is returned as an optional value (which is
 `None` for empty frame) and contains a key together with an object
 series representing the row.

### [Frame.shift](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#shift)

Frame.shift 
shift 

 Returns a frame with columns shifted by the specified offset. When the offset is
 positive, the values are shifted forward and first `offset` keys are dropped. When the
 offset is negative, the values are shifted backwards and the last `offset` keys are dropped.
 Expressed in pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k - offset]
  

 If you want to calculate the difference, e.g. `df - (Frame.shift 1 df)`, you can
 use `Frame.diff` which will be a little bit faster.

### [Frame.diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#diff)

Frame.diff 
diff 

 Returns a frame with columns containing difference between an original value and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Frame.diff 1 s` returns a
 frame where previous column values is subtracted from the current ones. In pseudo-code, the
 function behaves as follows:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 Columns that cannot be converted to `float` are left without a change.

### [Frame.pctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#pctChange)

Frame.pctChange 
pctChange 

 Returns a frame where each value is the percentage change relative to the value at the
 specified offset. For example, calling <code>Frame.pctChange 1 df</code> returns a frame where
 each value represents the relative change from the previous row's value. In pseudo-code:

     result[k] = (frame[k] - frame[k - offset]) / frame[k - offset]

 Columns that cannot be converted to <code>float</code> are left without a change.
 This is commonly used in financial analysis to compute returns (e.g. daily stock returns).

### [Frame.tryMapRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryMapRows)

Frame.tryMapRows 
tryMapRows 
 Returns a series, obtained by applying the specified projection function `f` to all rows
 of the input frame. The resulting series wraps the results in `tryval<'V>`. When the projection
 function fails, the exception is wrapped using the `Error` case.

 <category>Processing frames with exceptions</category>

### [Frame.tryValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#tryValues)

Frame.tryValues 
tryValues 
 Given a data frame containing columns of type `tryval<'T>`, returns a new data frame
 that contains the underlying values of type `'T`. When the frame contains one or more
 failures, the operation throws `AggregateException`. Otherwise, it returns a frame containing values.

 <category>Processing frames with exceptions</category>

### [Frame.fillErrorsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#fillErrorsWith)

Frame.fillErrorsWith 
fillErrorsWith 
 Fills all error cases of a `tryval<'T>` value in a data frame with the specified
 `value`. The function takes all columns of type `tryval<'T>` and uses `Series.fillErrorsWith`
 to fill the error values with the specified default value.

 <category>Processing frames with exceptions</category>

### [Frame.fillMissingWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#fillMissingWith)

Frame.fillMissingWith 
fillMissingWith 

 Fill missing values of a given type in the frame with a constant value.
 The fill value is applied to columns whose element type matches the fill value type
 (using safe numeric widening). This includes both cases where the column type can be
 widened to the fill value type (e.g. decimal columns filled with a float value) and
 cases where the fill value can be widened to the column type (e.g. an integer fill
 value filling float columns).
 Columns whose element type is incompatible with the fill value type are left unchanged.

### [Frame.fillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#fillMissing)

Frame.fillMissing 
fillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the data frame with the nearest available value
 (using the specified direction). Note that the frame may still contain
 missing values after call to this function (e.g. if the first value is not available
 and we attempt to fill series with previous values). This operation can only be
 used on ordered frames.

### [Frame.fillMissingUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#fillMissingUsing)

Frame.fillMissingUsing 
fillMissingUsing 

 Fill missing values in the frame using the specified function. The specified
 function is called with all series and keys for which the frame does not
 contain value and the result of the call is used in place of the missing value.

 The operation is only applied to columns (series) that contain values of the
 same type as the return type of the provided filling function. The operation
 does not attempt to convert between numeric values (so a series containing
 `float` will not be converted to a series of `int`).

### [Frame.dropSparseRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropSparseRows)

Frame.dropSparseRows 
dropSparseRows 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those rows of the original
 data frame that are _dense_, meaning that they have a value for each column.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of columns, but may have
 fewer rows (or no rows at all).

### [Frame.dropSparseRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropSparseRowsBy)

Frame.dropSparseRowsBy 
dropSparseRowsBy 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those rows of the original
 data frame for which the given column has a value (i.e., is not missing).
 The resulting data frame has the same number of columns, but may have
 fewer rows (or no rows at all).

### [Frame.dropEmptyRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropEmptyRows)

Frame.dropEmptyRows 
dropEmptyRows 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those rows that are empty for each column.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of columns, but may have
 fewer rows (or no rows at all).

### [Frame.dropSparseCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropSparseCols)

Frame.dropSparseCols 
dropSparseCols 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those columns of the original
 data frame that are _dense_, meaning that they have a value for each row.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of rows, but may have
 fewer columns (or no columns at all).

### [Frame.dropEmptyCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#dropEmptyCols)

Frame.dropEmptyCols 
dropEmptyCols 

 Creates a new data frame that drops those columns that are empty for each row.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of rows, but may have
 fewer columns (or no columns at all).

### [Frame.denseCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#denseCols)

Frame.denseCols 
denseCols 

 Returns the columns of the data frame that do not have any missing values.
 The operation returns a series (indexed by the column keys of the source frame)
 containing _series_ representing individual columns of the frame. This is similar
 to `Columns`, but it skips columns that contain missing value in _any_ row.

### [Frame.denseRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#denseRows)

Frame.denseRows 
denseRows 

 Returns the rows of the data frame that do not have any missing values.
 The operation returns a series (indexed by the row keys of the source frame)
 containing _series_ representing individual row of the frame. This is similar
 to `Rows`, but it skips rows that contain missing value in _any_ column.

### [Frame.join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#join)

Frame.join 
join 

 Join two data frames. The columns of the joined frames must not overlap and their
 rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.
 For more alignment options on ordered frames, see `joinAlign`.

### [Frame.joinAlign](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#joinAlign)

Frame.joinAlign 
joinAlign 

 Join two data frames. The columns of the joined frames must not overlap and their
 rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.
 When the index of both frames is ordered, it is possible to specify `lookup`
 in order to align indices from other frame to the indices of the main frame
 (typically, to find the nearest key with available value for a key).

### [Frame.joinOn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#joinOn)

Frame.joinOn 
joinOn 

 Join two data frames on a shared column, using that column's values as the row index for
 alignment. Both frames must have unique values in the specified column (the unique-key case).
 The join column is removed from the data columns of the result and becomes the row index.
 This is a convenience wrapper around <code>Frame.indexRows</code> followed by <code>Frame.join</code>.

### [Frame.joinOnString](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#joinOnString)

Frame.joinOnString 
joinOnString 

 Join two data frames on a shared string-valued column. Both frames must have unique values
 in the specified column (the unique-key case). The join column becomes the row index of the
 result frame. This is the string-specialised variant of <code>Frame.joinOn</code>.

### [Frame.joinOnInt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#joinOnInt)

Frame.joinOnInt 
joinOnInt 

 Join two data frames on a shared int-valued column. Both frames must have unique values
 in the specified column (the unique-key case). The join column becomes the row index of the
 result frame. This is the int-specialised variant of <code>Frame.joinOn</code>.

### [Frame.mergeAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#mergeAll)

Frame.mergeAll 
mergeAll 
 Append a sequence of data frames with non-overlapping values. The operation takes the union of
 columns and rows of the source data frames and then unions the values. An exception is thrown when
 both data frames define value for a column/row location, but the operation succeeds if one
 all frames but one has a missing value at the location.

 Note that the rows are *not* automatically reindexed to avoid overlaps. This means that when
 a frame has rows indexed with ordinal numbers, you may need to explicitly reindex the row
 keys before calling append.

 <category>Joining, merging and zipping</category>

### [Frame.merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#merge)

Frame.merge 
merge 

 Append two data frames with non-overlapping values. The operation takes the union of columns
 and rows of the source data frames and then unions the values. An exception is thrown when
 both data frames define value for a column/row location, but the operation succeeds if one
 frame has a missing value at the location.

 Note that the rows are *not* automatically reindexed to avoid overlaps. This means that when
 a frame has rows indexed with ordinal numbers, you may need to explicitly reindex the row
 keys before calling append.

### [Frame.interleave](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#interleave)

Frame.interleave 
interleave 

 Combines a list of data frames side-by-side by tagging each frame's column keys with
 its 1-based position in the list. The result has a tuple column index of type
 <code>('C * int)</code>, where the integer is the 1-based index of the source frame.

 For example, given two frames with columns <code>["a"; "b"]</code>, the result has columns
 <code>[("a",1); ("b",1); ("a",2); ("b",2)]</code>. Rows are aligned using an outer join.

 When only two frames are involved the result is equivalent to placing them side by side;
 when more than two are involved the frames are joined left-to-right.

### [Frame.zipAlign](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#zipAlign)

Frame.zipAlign 
zipAlign 

 Aligns two data frames using both column index and row index and apply the specified operation
 on values of a specified type that are available in both data frames. The parameters `columnKind`,
 and `rowKind` can be specified to determine how the alginment works (similarly to `Join`).
 Column keys are always matched using `Lookup.Exact`, but `lookup` determines lookup for rows.

 Once aligned, the call <code>df1.Zip<T>(df2, f)</code> applies the specifed function `f` on all `T` values
 that are available in corresponding locations in both frames. For values of other types, the
 value from `df1` is returned.

### [Frame.zip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#zip)

Frame.zip 
zip 

 Aligns two data frames using both column index and row index and apply the specified operation
 on values of a specified type that are available in both data frames. This overload uses
 `JoinKind.Outer` for both columns and rows.

 Once aligned, the call <code>df1.Zip<T>(df2, f)</code> applies the specifed function `f` on all `T` values
 that are available in corresponding locations in both frames. For values of other types, the
 value from `df1` is returned.

### [Frame.strConcat](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#strConcat)

Frame.strConcat 
strConcat 
 Piecewise concatenate two frames of string values

 <category>Joining, merging and zipping</category>

### [Frame.compare](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#compare)

Frame.compare 
compare 

 Compares two frames column-by-column and returns a new frame of <code>Diff</code> values.
 Each cell describes whether the value was added, removed, or changed between the two frames.
 Columns present only in <code>frame1</code> contribute <code>Diff.Remove</code> values;
 columns present only in <code>frame2</code> contribute <code>Diff.Add</code> values;
 columns present in both are compared element-wise.

### [Frame.reduceLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#reduceLevel)

Frame.reduceLevel 
reduceLevel 
 Reduce the values in each series according to the specified level of a hierarchical row key.
 <summary>
 For each group of rows as specified by `levelSel`, the function reduces the values in each series
 using the preovided function `op` by applying `Series.reduceLevel`. Columns that cannot be
 converted to a type required by `op` are skipped.
 </summary>
 <example>
 To sum the values in all numerical columns according to the first component of a two level
 row key `'K1 * 'K2`, you can use the following:
 <code>
 df |> Frame.reduceLevel fst (fun (a:float) b -> a + b)
 </code>
 </example>
 <remarks>
 This function reduces values using a function `'T -> 'T -> 'T`. If you want to process
 an entire group of values at once, you can use `applyLevel` instead.
 </remarks>
 <category>Hierarchical index operations</category>

### [Frame.applyLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#applyLevel)

Frame.applyLevel 
applyLevel 

 Apply a specified function to a group of values in each series according to the specified
 level of a hierarchical row key. For each group of rows as specified by `levelSel`, the function
 applies the specified function `op` to all columns. Columns that cannot be converted to a
 type required by `op` are skipped.
  

 This function reduces a series of values using a function <code>Series<'R, 'T> -> 'T</code>. If
 you want to reduce values using a simpler function <code>'T -> 'T -> 'T</code>, you can use
 `Frame.reduceLevel` instead.

### [Frame.nest](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#nest)

Frame.nest 
nest 

 Given a frame with two-level row index, returns a series indexed by the first
 part of the key, containing frames representing individual groups. This function
 can be used if you want to perform a transformation individually on each group
 (e.g. using `Series.mapValues` after calling `Frame.nest`).

### [Frame.nestBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#nestBy)

Frame.nestBy 
nestBy 
 Given a data frame, use the specified `keySelector` to generate a new, first-level
 of indices based on the current indices. Returns a series (indexed by the first-level)
 of frames (indexed by the second-level).

 <category>Hierarchical index operations</category>

### [Frame.nestRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#nestRowsBy)

Frame.nestRowsBy 
nestRowsBy 

 Given a data frame, uses <code>groupSel</code> to determine the group key for each row,
 and <code>rowSel</code> to determine the inner row key within each group's sub-frame.
 Returns a series (indexed by the group key) of frames (indexed by the inner row key).
  

 This is a generalisation of <code>Frame.nestBy</code> that lets you transform the inner row keys
 independently of the group keys.  A common use-case is grouping a frame whose rows are
 already keyed by tuples: <code>frame |> Frame.nestRowsBy fst snd</code> groups by the first
 element and re-indexes each sub-frame by the second element.

### [Frame.unnest](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#unnest)

Frame.unnest 
unnest 
 Given a series of frames, returns a new data frame with two-level hierarchical
 row index, using the series keys as the first component. This function is the
 dual of `Frame.nest`.

 <category>Hierarchical index operations</category>

### [Frame.toJson](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#toJson)

Frame.toJson 
toJson 

 Serialize the data frame to a JSON string.

### [Frame.saveJson](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framemodule.html#saveJson)

Frame.saveJson 
saveJson 

 Save the data frame as a JSON file at the specified path.

### [FrameBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder.html)

FrameBuilder 

 Type that can be used for creating frames using the C# collection initializer syntax.
 You can use <code>new FrameBuilder.Columns<...></code> to create a new frame from columns or you
 can use <code>new FrameBuilder.Rows<...></code> to create a new frame from rows.
  
FrameBuilder.Columns<'R, 'C> 
Columns<'R, 'C> 
FrameBuilder.Rows<'R, 'C> 
Rows<'R, 'C>

### [Columns<'R, 'C>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-columns-2.html)

Columns<'R, 'C> 
 
Columns<'R, 'C>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

Add 
Columns<'R, 'C>.Frame 
Frame

Columns<'R, 'C>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

Columns<'R, 'C>.Add 
Add

### [Columns<'R, 'C>.Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-columns-2.html#Frame)

Columns<'R, 'C>.Frame 
Frame

### [Rows<'R, 'C>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-rows-2.html)

Rows<'R, 'C> 
 
Rows<'R, 'C>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Rows<'R, 'C>.Add 
Add 
Rows<'R, 'C>.Frame 
Frame

### [Rows<'R, 'C>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-rows-2.html#``.ctor``)

Rows<'R, 'C>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Rows<'R, 'C>.Add](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-rows-2.html#Add)

Rows<'R, 'C>.Add 
Add

### [Rows<'R, 'C>.Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framebuilder-rows-2.html#Frame)

Rows<'R, 'C>.Frame 
Frame

### [MultiKeyExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html)

MultiKeyExtensions 

 F#-friendly functions for creating multi-level keys and lookups
  
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of2 
Lookup1Of2 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of2 
Lookup2Of2 
MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf2 
LookupAnyOf2 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of3 
Lookup1Of3 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of3 
Lookup2Of3 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of3 
Lookup3Of3 
MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf3 
LookupAnyOf3 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of4 
Lookup1Of4 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of4 
Lookup2Of4 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of4 
Lookup3Of4 
MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup4Of4 
Lookup4Of4 
MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf4 
LookupAnyOf4

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of2](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup1Of2)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of2 
Lookup1Of2 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 first element of a two-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of2](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup2Of2)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of2 
Lookup2Of2 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 second element of a two-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf2](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#LookupAnyOf2)

MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf2 
LookupAnyOf2 
 Creates an arbitrary lookup key that allows matching on elements
 of a two-level hierarchical index. Specify `None` to ignore a level
 or `Some k` to require match on a given level.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of3](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup1Of3)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of3 
Lookup1Of3 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 first element of a three-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of3](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup2Of3)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of3 
Lookup2Of3 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 second element of a three-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of3](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup3Of3)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of3 
Lookup3Of3 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 third element of a three-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf3](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#LookupAnyOf3)

MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf3 
LookupAnyOf3 
 Creates an arbitrary lookup key that allows matching on elements
 of a three-level hierarchical index. Specify `None` to ignore a level
 or `Some k` to require match on a given level.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup1Of4)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup1Of4 
Lookup1Of4 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 first element of a four-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup2Of4)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup2Of4 
Lookup2Of4 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 second element of a four-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup3Of4)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup3Of4 
Lookup3Of4 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 third element of a four-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup4Of4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#Lookup4Of4)

MultiKeyExtensions.Lookup4Of4 
Lookup4Of4 
 Creates a hierarchical key lookup that allows matching on the
 fourth element of a four-level hierarchical key.

### [MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-multikeyextensions.html#LookupAnyOf4)

MultiKeyExtensions.LookupAnyOf4 
LookupAnyOf4 
 Creates an arbitrary lookup key that allows matching on elements
 of a four-level hierarchical index. Specify `None` to ignore a level
 or `Some k` to require match on a given level.

### [OptionalValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html)

OptionalValue 

 Provides various helper functions for using the <code>OptionalValue<T></code> type from F#
 (The functions are similar to those in the standard <code>Option</code> module).
  
OptionalValue.bind 
bind 
OptionalValue.map 
map 
OptionalValue.map2 
map2 
OptionalValue.ofTuple 
ofTuple 
OptionalValue.ofNullable 
ofNullable 
OptionalValue.asOption 
asOption 
OptionalValue.ofOption 
ofOption 
OptionalValue.get 
get 
OptionalValue.defaultArg 
defaultArg 
OptionalValue.(|Missing|Present|) 
(|Missing|Present|)

### [OptionalValue.bind](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#bind)

OptionalValue.bind 
bind 
 If the <c>OptionalValue<T></c> does not contain a value, then returns a new
 <c>OptionalValue<R>.Empty</c>. Otherwise, returns the result of applying the
 function <c>f</c> to the value contained in the provided optional value.

### [OptionalValue.map](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#map)

OptionalValue.map 
map 
 If the `OptionalValue<T>` does not contain a value, then returns a new
 `OptionalValue<R>.Empty`. Otherwise, returns the result `OptionalValue<R>`
 containing the result of applying the function `f` to the value contained
 in the provided optional value.

### [OptionalValue.map2](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#map2)

OptionalValue.map2 
map2 
 If both of the arguments contain value, apply the specified function to their
 values and return `OptionalValue<R>` with the result. Otherwise return
 `OptionalValue.Missing`.

### [OptionalValue.ofTuple](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#ofTuple)

OptionalValue.ofTuple 
ofTuple 
<pre> Creates `OptionalValue<T>` from a tuple of type `bool * 'T`. This function
 can be used with .NET methods that use `out` arguments. For example:

     Int32.TryParse("42") |> OptionalValue.ofTuple</pre>

### [OptionalValue.ofNullable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#ofNullable)

OptionalValue.ofNullable 
ofNullable 
 Creates `OptionalValue<T>` from a .NET `Nullable<T>` type.

### [OptionalValue.asOption](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#asOption)

OptionalValue.asOption 
asOption 
 Turns the `OptionalValue<T>` into a corresponding standard F# `option<T>` value

### [OptionalValue.ofOption](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#ofOption)

OptionalValue.ofOption 
ofOption 
 Turns a standard F# `option<T>` value into a corresponding `OptionalValue<T>`

### [OptionalValue.get](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#get)

OptionalValue.get 
get 
 Get the value stored in the specified optional value. If a value is not
 available, throws an exception. (This is equivalent to the `Value` property)

### [OptionalValue.defaultArg](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#defaultArg)

OptionalValue.defaultArg 
defaultArg 
 Returns the value `def` when the argument is missing, otherwise returns its value

### [OptionalValue.(|Missing|Present|)](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvaluemodule.html#(|Missing|Present|))

OptionalValue.(|Missing|Present|) 
(|Missing|Present|) 
<pre> Complete active pattern that can be used to pattern match on `OptionalValue<T>`.
 For example:

     let optVal = OptionalValue(42)
     match optVal with
     | OptionalValue.Missing -> printfn "Empty"
     | OptionalValue.Present(v) -> printfn "Contains %d" v</pre>

RangeRestriction.map 
map 
 Transforms all absolute addresses in the specified range restriction
 using the provided function (this is useful for mapping between different
 address spaces).

### [Series](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html)

Series 

 The `Series` module provides an F#-friendly API for working with data and time series.
 The API follows the usual design for collection-processing in F#, so the functions work
 well with the pipelining (<code>|></code>) operator. For example, given a series with ages,
 we can use `Series.filterValues` to filter outliers and then `Stats.mean` to calculate
 the mean:

     ages
     |> Series.filterValues (fun v -> v > 0.0 && v < 120.0)
     |> Stats.mean

 The module provides comprehensive set of functions for working with series. The same
 API is also exposed using C#-friendly extension methods. In C#, the above snippet could
 be written as:

     [lang=csharp]
     ages
       .Where(kvp => kvp.Value > 0.0 && kvp.Value < 120.0)
       .Mean()

 For more information about similar frame-manipulation functions, see the `Frame` module.
 For more information about C#-friendly extensions, see `SeriesExtensions`. The functions
 in the `Series` module are grouped in a number of categories and documented below.

 Accessing series data and lookup
 --------------------------------

 Functions in this category provide access to the values in the series.

  - The term _observation_ is used for a key value pair in the series.
  - When working with a sorted series, it is possible to perform lookup using
    keys that are not present in the series - you can specify to search for the
    previous or next available value using _lookup behavior_.
  - Functions such as `get` and `getAll` have their counterparts `lookup` and
    `lookupAll` that let you specify lookup behavior.
  - For most of the functions that may fail, there is a `try[Foo]` variant that
    returns `None` instead of failing.
  - Functions with a name ending with `At` perform lookup based on the absolute
    integer offset (and ignore the keys of the series)

 Series transformations
 ----------------------

 Functions in this category perform standard transformations on series including
 projections, filtering, taking some sub-series of the series, aggregating values
 using scanning and so on.

 Projection and filtering functions generally skip over missing values, but there
 are variants `filterAll` and `mapAll` that let you handle missing values explicitly.
 Keys can be transformed using `mapKeys`. When you do not need to consider the keys,
 and only care about values, use `filterValues` and `mapValues` (which is also aliased
 as the `$` operator).

 Series supports standard set of folding functions including `reduce` and `fold` (to
 reduce series values into a single value) as well as the `scan[All]` function, which
 can be used to fold values of a series into a series of intermeidate folding results.

 The functions `take[Last]` and `skip[Last]` can be used to take a sub-series of the
 original source series by skipping a specified number of elements. Note that this
 does not require an ordered series and it ignores the index - for index-based lookup
 use slicing, such as `series.[lo .. hi]`, instead.

 Finally the `shift` function can be used to obtain a series with values shifted by
 the specified offset. This can be used e.g. to get previous value for each key using
 `Series.shift 1 ts`. The `diff` function calculates difference from previous value using
 `ts - (Series.shift offs ts)`.

 Processing series with exceptions
 ---------------------------------

 The functions in this group can be used to write computations over series that may fail.
 They use the type <code>tryval<'T></code> which is defined as a discriminated union
 with two cases: Success containing a value, or Error containing an exception.

 The function `tryMap` lets you create <code>Series<'K, tryval<'T>></code> by mapping over values
 of an original series. You can then extract values using `tryValues`, which throws
 `AggregateException` if there were any errors. Functions `tryErrors` and `trySuccesses`
 give series containing only errors and successes. You can fill failed values with
 a constant using `fillErrorsWith`.

 Hierarchical index operations
 -----------------------------

 When the key of a series is tuple, the elements of the tuple can be treated
 as multiple levels of a index. For example <code>Series<'K1 * 'K2, 'V></code> has two
 levels with keys of types <code>'K1</code> and <code>'K2</code> respectively.

 The functions in this cateogry provide a way for aggregating values in the
 series at one of the levels. For example, given a series `input` indexed by
 two-element tuple, you can calculate mean for different first-level values as
 follows:

     input |> applyLevel fst Stats.mean

 Note that the `Stats` module provides helpers for typical statistical operations,
 so the above could be written just as `input |> Stats.levelMean fst`.

 Grouping, windowing and chunking
 --------------------------------

 This category includes functions that group data from a series in some way. Two key
 concepts here are _window_ and _chunk_. Window refers to (overlapping) sliding windows
 over the input series while chunk refers to non-overlapping blocks of the series.

 The boundary behavior can be specified using the `Boundary` flags. The value
 `Skip` means that boundaries (incomplete windows or chunks) should be skipped. The value
 `AtBeginning` and `AtEnding` can be used to define at which side should the boundary be
 returned (or skipped). For chunking, `AtBeginning ||| Skip` makes sense and it means that
 the incomplete chunk at the beginning should be skipped (aligning the last chunk with the end).

 The behavior may be specified in a number of ways (which is reflected in the name):
  - `dist` - using an absolute distance between the keys
  - `while` - using a condition on the first and last key
  - `size` - by specifying the absolute size of the window/chunk

 The functions ending with `Into` take a function to be applied to the window/chunk.
 The functions `window`, `windowInto` and `chunk`, `chunkInto` are simplified versions
 that take a size. There is also `pairwise` function for sliding window of size two.

 Missing values
 --------------

 This group of functions provides a way of working with missing values in a series.
 The `dropMissing` function drops all keys for which there are no values in the series.
 The `withMissingFrom` function lets you copy missing values from another series.
 The remaining functions provide different mechanism for filling the missing values.

  * `fillMissingWith` fills missing values with a specified constant
  * `fillMissingUsing` calls a specified function for every missing value
  * `fillMissing` and variants propagates values from previous/later keys

 Sorting and index manipulation
 ------------------------------

 A series that is sorted by keys allows a number of additional operations (such as lookup
 using the `Lookp.ExactOrSmaller` lookup behavior). However, it is also possible to sort
 series based on the values - although the functions for manipulation with series do not
 guarantee that the order will be preserved.

 To sort series by keys, use `sortByKey`. Other sorting functions let you sort the series
 using a specified comparer function (`sortWith`), using a projection function (`sortBy`)
 and using the default comparison (`sort`).

 In addition, you can also replace the keys of a series with other keys using `indexWith`
 or with integers using `indexOrdinally`. To pick and reorder series values using to match
 a list of keys use `realign`.

 Sampling, resampling and advanced lookup
 ----------------------------------------

 Given a (typically) time series sampling or resampling makes it possible to
 get time series with representative values at lower or uniform frequency.
 We use the following terminology:

  - `lookup` and `sample` functions find values at specified key; if a key is not
    available, they can look for value associated with the nearest smaller or
    the nearest greater key.

  - `resample` function aggregate values values into chunks based
    on a specified collection of keys (e.g. explicitly provided times), or
    based on some relation between keys (e.g. date times having the same date).

  - `resampleUniform` is similar to resampling, but we specify keys by
    providing functions that generate a uniform sequence of keys (e.g. days),
    the operation also fills value for days that have no corresponding
    observations in the input sequence.

 Joining, merging and zipping
 ----------------------------

 Given two series, there are two ways to combine the values. If the keys in the series
 are not overlapping (or you want to throw away values from one or the other series),
 then you can use `merge` or `mergeUsing`. To merge more than 2 series efficiently, use
 the `mergeAll` function, which has been optimized for large number of series.

 If you want to align two series, you can use the _zipping_ operation. This aligns
 two series based on their keys and gives you tuples of values. The default behavior
 (`zip`) uses outer join and exact matching. For ordered series, you can specify
 other forms of key lookups (e.g. find the greatest smaller key) using `zipAlign`.
 functions ending with `Into` are generally easier to use as they call a specified
 function to turn the tuple (of possibly missing values) into a new value.

 For more complicated behaviors, it is often convenient to use joins on frames instead
 of working with series. Create two frames with single columns and then use the join
 operation. The result will be a frame with two columns (which is easier to use than
 series of tuples).
  
Series.empty
