### [Series.empty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#empty)

Series.empty 
empty 

 Returns an empty series with no keys or values.

### [Series.observations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#observations)

Series.observations 
observations 

 Return observations with available values. The operation skips over
 all keys with missing values (such as values created from `null`,
 `Double.NaN`, or those that are missing due to outer join etc.).

### [Series.observationsAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#observationsAll)

Series.observationsAll 
observationsAll 

 Returns all keys from the sequence, together with the associated (optional) values.

### [Series.lookupAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lookupAll)

Series.lookupAll 
lookupAll 

 Create a new series that contains values for all provided keys.
 Use the specified lookup semantics - for exact matching, use `getAll`

### [Series.sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sample)

Series.sample 
sample 

 Sample an (ordered) series by finding the value at the exact or closest prior key
 for some new sequence of keys.

### [Series.getAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#getAll)

Series.getAll 
getAll 

 Create a new series that contains values for all provided keys.
 Uses exact lookup semantics for key lookup - use `lookupAll` for more options

### [Series.lookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lookup)

Series.lookup 
lookup 

 Get the value for the specified key.
 Use the specified lookup semantics - for exact matching, use `get`

### [Series.get](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#get)

Series.get 
get 

 Get the value for the specified key.
 Uses exact lookup semantics for key lookup - use `lookup` for more options

### [Series.getAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#getAt)

Series.getAt 
getAt 

 Returns the value at the specified (integer) offset.

### [Series.iloc](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#iloc)

Series.iloc 
iloc 

 Returns a new series containing only the values at the specified integer (zero-based)
 positions from the original series. Equivalent to pandas <code>Series.iloc</code>.
 The resulting series preserves the original keys at those positions.

### [Series.tryLookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryLookup)

Series.tryLookup 
tryLookup 

 Attempts to get the value for the specified key. If the value is not
 available, `None` is returned.
 Use the specified lookup semantics - for exact matching, use `tryGet`.

### [Series.tryLookupObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryLookupObservation)

Series.tryLookupObservation 
tryLookupObservation 

 Attempts to get an observation (key value pair) based on the specified key.
 The search uses the specified lookup semantics and so the returned key
 may differ from the key searched for. If the value is not
 available, `None` is returned.

### [Series.tryGet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryGet)

Series.tryGet 
tryGet 

 Get the value for the specified key. Returns `None` when the key does not exist
 or the value is missing.
 Uses exact lookup semantics for key lookup - use `tryLookup` for more options

### [Series.tryGetAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryGetAt)

Series.tryGetAt 
tryGetAt 

 Returns the value at the specified (integer) offset, or `None` if the value is missing.

### [Series.countValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#countValues)

Series.countValues 
countValues 

 Returns the total number of values in the specified series. This excludes
 missing values or not available values (such as values created from `null`,
 `Double.NaN`, or those that are missing due to outer join etc.).

### [Series.countKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#countKeys)

Series.countKeys 
countKeys 

 Returns the total number of keys in the specified series. This returns
 the total length of the series, including keys for which there is no
 value available.

### [Series.hasAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#hasAll)

Series.hasAll 
hasAll 

 Returns true when the series contains value for all of the specified keys
 (This is useful for checking prior to performing a computation)

### [Series.hasSome](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#hasSome)

Series.hasSome 
hasSome 

 Returns true when the series contains value for some of the specified keys
 (This is useful for checking prior to performing a computation)

### [Series.hasNone](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#hasNone)

Series.hasNone 
hasNone 

 Returns true when the series does not contains value for any of the specified keys
 (This is useful for checking prior to performing a computation)

### [Series.has](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#has)

Series.has 
has 

 Returns true when the series contains value for the specified key
 (This is useful for checking prior to performing a computation)

### [Series.hasNot](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#hasNot)

Series.hasNot 
hasNot 

 Returns true when the series does not contains value for the specified key
 (This is useful for checking prior to performing a computation)

### [Series.lastKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lastKey)

Series.lastKey 
lastKey 

 Returns the last key of the series, or throws exception if one doesn't exist

### [Series.firstKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#firstKey)

Series.firstKey 
firstKey 

 Returns the first key of the series, or throws exception if one doesn't exist

### [Series.lastValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lastValue)

Series.lastValue 
lastValue 

 Returns the last value of the series. This fails if the last value is missing.

### [Series.tryLastValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryLastValue)

Series.tryLastValue 
tryLastValue 

 Returns the last value of the series if one exists.

### [Series.firstValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#firstValue)

Series.firstValue 
firstValue 

 Returns the first value of the series. This fails if the first value is missing.

### [Series.tryFirstValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryFirstValue)

Series.tryFirstValue 
tryFirstValue 

 Returns the first value of the series if one exists.

### [Series.values](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#values)

Series.values 
values 

 Returns the (non-missing) values of the series as a sequence

### [Series.valuesAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#valuesAll)

Series.valuesAll 
valuesAll 

 Returns the series values (both missing and present) as a sequence

### [Series.keys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#keys)

Series.keys 
keys 

 Returns the keys of the series as a sequence

### [Series.after](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#after)

Series.after 
after 

 Returns a new series containing all values with keys strictly greater than
 <code>lowerExclusive</code>. This is the functional equivalent of <code>series.After(k)</code>.

### [Series.before](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#before)

Series.before 
before 

 Returns a new series containing all values with keys strictly less than
 <code>upperExclusive</code>. This is the functional equivalent of <code>series.Before(k)</code>.

### [Series.startAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#startAt)

Series.startAt 
startAt 

 Returns a new series containing all values with keys greater than or equal to
 <code>lowerInclusive</code>. This is the functional equivalent of <code>series.StartAt(k)</code>.

### [Series.endAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#endAt)

Series.endAt 
endAt 

 Returns a new series containing all values with keys less than or equal to
 <code>upperInclusive</code>. This is the functional equivalent of <code>series.EndAt(k)</code>.

### [Series.between](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#between)

Series.between 
between 

 Returns a new series containing all values with keys in the range
 [<code>lowerInclusive</code>, <code>upperInclusive</code>] (both bounds inclusive).
 This is the functional equivalent of <code>series.Between(lo, hi)</code>.

### [Series.filter](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#filter)

Series.filter 
filter 

 Returns a new series containing only the elements for which the specified predicate
 returns `true`. The function skips over missing values. If you want to handle missing
 values, use `filterAll` instead.

### [Series.filterValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#filterValues)

Series.filterValues 
filterValues 

 Returns a new series containing only the elements for which the specified predicate
 returns `true`. The function skips over missing values and calls the predicate with
 just the value. See also `filterAll` and `filter` for more options.

### [Series.filterAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#filterAll)

Series.filterAll 
filterAll 

 Returns a new series containing only the elements for which the specified predicate
 returns `true`. The predicate is called for missing values as well.

### [Series.filterByMask](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#filterByMask)

Series.filterByMask 
filterByMask 

 Returns a new series containing only the elements of the input series whose key
 maps to <code>true</code> in the given boolean mask series. Keys that are missing from
 the mask are excluded. This enables pandas-style boolean indexing.

### [Series.map](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#map)

Series.map 
map 

 Returns a new series whose values are the results of applying the given function to
 values of the original series. This function skips over missing values and call the
 function with both keys and values.

### [Series.mapValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#mapValues)

Series.mapValues 
mapValues 

 Returns a new series whose values are the results of applying the given function to
 values of the original series. This function skips over missing values and call the
 function with just values. It is also aliased using the `$` operator so you can write
 `series $ func` for `series |> Series.mapValues func`.

### [Series.mapAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#mapAll)

Series.mapAll 
mapAll 

 Returns a new series whose values are the results of applying the given function to
 values of the original series. This specified function is called even when the value
 is missing. It returns <code>option<'T></code> so that it can create/eliminate missing values in
 the result.

### [Series.maskAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#maskAll)

Series.maskAll 
maskAll 

 Returns a new series with values replaced by missing where the predicate returns `true`.
 The predicate is called with both the key and an optional value (to handle missing values).
 This is the complement of `filterAll`: instead of dropping matching entries the values are
 replaced with missing so that the key alignment of the series is preserved.

### [Series.maskValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#maskValues)

Series.maskValues 
maskValues 

 Returns a new series with values replaced by missing where the predicate returns `true`.
 The predicate is called with the value only; missing values are never masked by this function.

### [Series.replaceValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#replaceValue)

Series.replaceValue 
replaceValue 

 Returns a new series where all occurrences of <code>oldValue</code> are replaced with
 <code>newValue</code>. Missing values in the series are left unchanged.

### [Series.mapKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#mapKeys)

Series.mapKeys 
mapKeys 

 Returns a new series whose keys are the results of applying the given function to
 keys of the original series.

### [Series.convert](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#convert)

Series.convert 
convert 

 Retruns a new series whose values are converted using the specified conversion function.
 This operation is like `mapValues`, but it requires a pair of function that converts
 the values in _both ways_.
  

 This operation is only interesting when working with virtualized data sources. Using the
 `convert` function makes it possible to perfom additional operations on the resulting
 series - for example lookup - by converting the new value back and using the lookup of
 the underlying virtualized source.

### [Series.flatten](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#flatten)

Series.flatten 
flatten 

 Given a series containing optional values, flatten the option values.
 That is, `None` values become missing values of the series and `Some` values
 become ordinary values in the resulting series.

### [Series.take](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#take)

Series.take 
take 

 Returns a series that contains the specified number of keys from the original series.

### [Series.takeLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#takeLast)

Series.takeLast 
takeLast 

 Returns a series that contains the specified number of keys from the
 original series. The keys are taken from the end of the series.

### [Series.skip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#skip)

Series.skip 
skip 

 Returns a series that contains the data from the original series,
 except for the first `count` keys.

### [Series.skipLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#skipLast)

Series.skipLast 
skipLast 

 Returns a series that contains the data from the original series,
 except for the last `count` keys.

### [Series.force](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#force)

Series.force 
force 

 Returns a new fully evaluated series. If the source series contains a lazy index or
 lazy vectors, these are forced to evaluate and the resulting series is fully loaded in memory.

### [Series.scanValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#scanValues)

Series.scanValues 
scanValues 

 Applies a folding function starting with some initial value and the first value of the series,
 and continues to "scan" along the series, saving all values produced from the first function
 application, and yielding a new series having the original index and newly produced values.
 Any application involving a missing value yields a missing value.

### [Series.scanAllValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#scanAllValues)

Series.scanAllValues 
scanAllValues 

 Applies a folding function starting with some initial optional value and the first optional value of
 the series, and continues to "scan" along the series, saving all values produced from the first function
 application, and yielding a new series having the original index and newly produced values.

### [Series.reduceValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#reduceValues)

Series.reduceValues 
reduceValues 

 Aggregates the values of the specified series using a function that can combine
 individual values. Fails if the series contains no values.

### [Series.foldValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#foldValues)

Series.foldValues 
foldValues 

 Aggregates the values of the specified series using a function that can combine
 individual values. The folding starts with the specified initial value.

### [Series.diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#diff)

Series.diff 
diff 

 Returns a series containing difference between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Series.diff 1 s` returns a
 series where previous value is subtracted from the current one. In pseudo-code, the
 function behaves as follows:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

### [Series.pctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#pctChange)

Series.pctChange 
pctChange 

 Returns a series containing the percentage change between a value in the series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling <code>Series.pctChange 1 s</code> returns
 a series where each value is the relative change from the previous value. In pseudo-code:

     result[k] = (series[k] - series[k - offset]) / series[k - offset]

 This is commonly used in financial analysis to compute returns.

### [Series.shift](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#shift)

Series.shift 
shift 

 Returns a series with values shifted by the specified offset. When the offset is
 positive, the values are shifted forward and first `offset` keys are dropped. When the
 offset is negative, the values are shifted backwards and the last `offset` keys are dropped.
 Expressed in pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k - offset]
  

 If you want to calculate the difference, e.g. `s - (Series.shift 1 s)`, you can
 use `Series.diff` which will be a little bit faster.

### [Series.tryMap](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryMap)

Series.tryMap 
tryMap 

 Returns a new series by applying the specified transformation to all values
 of the input series. The result contains `Error(e)` when the projection fails
 with an exception `e` or `Success(v)` containing a value `v` otherwise.

### [Series.tryValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryValues)

Series.tryValues 
tryValues 

 Obtains values from a series of <code>tryval<'T></code> values. When the series contains
 one or more failures, the operation throws `AggregateException`. Otherwise, it
 returns a series containing values.

### [Series.tryErrors](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#tryErrors)

Series.tryErrors 
tryErrors 

 Given a series of <code>tryval<'V></code> values, returns a series that contains all exceptions
 contained in the source series. The exceptions are returned as a series.

### [Series.trySuccesses](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#trySuccesses)

Series.trySuccesses 
trySuccesses 

 Given a series of <code>tryval<'V></code> values, returns a series that contains all values
 contained in the source series. The input elements containing exceptions are ignored.

### [Series.fillErrorsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillErrorsWith)

Series.fillErrorsWith 
fillErrorsWith 

 Givnen a series of <code>tryval<'V></code> values, returns a new series where all `Error`
 values are filled with the specified constant value.

### [Series.applyLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#applyLevel)

Series.applyLevel 
applyLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `level` and then aggregates series representing each group
 using the specified function `op`. The result is a new series containing
 the aggregates of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../frame.html#indexing).

### [Series.applyLevelOptional](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#applyLevelOptional)

Series.applyLevelOptional 
applyLevelOptional 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `level` and then aggregates series representing each group
 using the specified function `op`. The result is a new series containing
 the aggregates of each group. The result of a group may be None, in which
 case the group will have no representation in the resulting series.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../frame.html#indexing).

### [Series.reduceLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#reduceLevel)

Series.reduceLevel 
reduceLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `level` and then aggregates elements in each group
 using the specified function `op`. The result is a new series containing
 the aggregates of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../frame.html#indexing).

### [Series.aggregate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#aggregate)

Series.aggregate 
aggregate 

 Aggregates an ordered series using the method specified by <code>Aggregation<K></code> and
 returns the windows or chunks as nested series. A key for each window or chunk is
 selected using the specified `keySelector`.

### [Series.aggregateInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#aggregateInto)

Series.aggregateInto 
aggregateInto 

 Aggregates an ordered series using the method specified by <code>Aggregation<K></code> and then
 applies the provided value selector `f` on each window or chunk to produce the result
 which is returned as a new series. A key for each window or chunk is
 selected using the specified `keySelector`.

### [Series.windowSizeInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowSizeInto)

Series.windowSizeInto 
windowSizeInto 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size and boundary behavior and then
 applies the provided value selector `f` on each window to produce the result
 which is returned as a new series. The key is the last key of the window, unless
 boundary behavior is `Boundary.AtEnding` (in which case it is the first key).

### [Series.windowSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowSize)

Series.windowSize 
windowSize 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size and boundary behavior and returns
 the produced windows as a nested series. The key is the last key of the window, unless
 boundary behavior is `Boundary.AtEnding` (in which case it is the first key).

### [Series.windowDistInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowDistInto)

Series.windowDistInto 
windowDistInto 

 Creates a sliding window based on distance between keys. A window is started at each
 input element and ends once the distance between the first and the last key is greater
 than the specified `distance`. Each window is then aggregated into a value using the
 specified function `f`. The key of each window is the key of the first element in the window.

### [Series.windowDist](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowDist)

Series.windowDist 
windowDist 

 Creates a sliding window based on distance between keys. A window is started at each
 input element and ends once the distance between the first and the last key is greater
 than the specified `distance`. The windows are then returned as a nested series.
 The key of each window is the key of the first element in the window.

### [Series.windowWhileInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowWhileInto)

Series.windowWhileInto 
windowWhileInto 

 Creates a sliding window based on a condition on keys. A window is started at each
 input element and ends once the specified `cond` function returns `false` when called on
 the first and the last key of the window. Each window is then aggregated into a value using the
 specified function `f`. The key of each window is the key of the first element in the window.

### [Series.windowWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowWhile)

Series.windowWhile 
windowWhile 

 Creates a sliding window based on a condition on keys. A window is started at each
 input element and ends once the specified `cond` function returns `false` when called on
 the first and the last key of the window. The windows are then returned as a nested series.
 The key of each window is the key of the first element in the window.

### [Series.chunkSizeInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkSizeInto)

Series.chunkSizeInto 
chunkSizeInto 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks using the specified size and boundary behavior and then
 applies the provided value selector `f` on each chunk to produce the result
 which is returned as a new series. The key is the first key of the chunk, unless
 boundary behavior has `Boundary.AtBeginning` flag (in which case it is the last key).

### [Series.chunkSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkSize)

Series.chunkSize 
chunkSize 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks using the specified size and boundary behavior and returns
 the produced chunks as a nested series. The key is the first key of the chunk, unless
 boundary behavior has `Boundary.AtBeginning` flag (in which case it is the last key).

### [Series.chunkDistInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkDistInto)

Series.chunkDistInto 
chunkDistInto 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks. A chunk is started once
 the distance between the first and the last key of a previous chunk is greater
 than the specified `distance`. Each chunk is then aggregated into a value using the
 specified function `f`. The key of each chunk is the key of the first element in the chunk.

### [Series.chunkDist](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkDist)

Series.chunkDist 
chunkDist 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks. A chunk is started once
 the distance between the first and the last key of a previous chunk is greater
 than the specified `distance`. The chunks are then returned as a nested series.
 The key of each chunk is the key of the first element in the chunk.

### [Series.chunkWhileInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkWhileInto)

Series.chunkWhileInto 
chunkWhileInto 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks based on a condition on keys. A chunk is started
 once the specified `cond` function returns `false` when called on  the first and the last key of the
 previous chunk. Each chunk is then aggregated into a value using the
 specified function `f`. The key of each chunk is the key of the first element in the chunk.

### [Series.chunkWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkWhile)

Series.chunkWhile 
chunkWhile 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks based on a condition on keys. A chunk is started
 once the specified `cond` function returns `false` when called on  the first and the last key of the
 previous chunk. The chunks are then returned as a nested series.
 The key of each chunk is the key of the first element in the chunk.

### [Series.windowWhileIntoFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowWhileIntoFromEnd)

Series.windowWhileIntoFromEnd 
windowWhileIntoFromEnd 

 Creates a sliding window based on a condition on keys. A window ends at each
 input element and starts at the earliest key for which the specified `cond`
 function returns `true` when called on the start key and the end (current) key.
 Each window is then aggregated into a value using the specified function `f`.
 The key of each window is the key of the last element in the window.

### [Series.windowWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowWhileFromEnd)

Series.windowWhileFromEnd 
windowWhileFromEnd 

 Creates a sliding window based on a condition on keys. A window ends at each
 input element and starts at the earliest key for which the specified `cond`
 function returns `true` when called on the start key and the end (current) key.
 The windows are then returned as a nested series.
 The key of each window is the key of the last element in the window.

### [Series.chunkWhileIntoFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkWhileIntoFromEnd)

Series.chunkWhileIntoFromEnd 
chunkWhileIntoFromEnd 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adjacent chunks based on a condition on keys,
 using the same chunking as <code>chunkWhileInto</code>, but with the key of each chunk
 being the last key of the chunk rather than the first.
 Each chunk is then aggregated into a value using the specified function `f`.

### [Series.chunkWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkWhileFromEnd)

Series.chunkWhileFromEnd 
chunkWhileFromEnd 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adjacent chunks based on a condition on keys,
 using the same chunking as <code>chunkWhile</code>, but with the key of each chunk
 being the last key of the chunk rather than the first.
 The chunks are returned as a nested series.

### [Series.windowInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#windowInto)

Series.windowInto 
windowInto 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size and then applies the provided
 value selector `f` on each window to produce the result which is returned as a new series.
 This function skips incomplete chunks - you can use `Series.windowSizeInto` for more options.

### [Series.window](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#window)

Series.window 
window 

 Creates a sliding window using the specified size and returns the produced windows as
 a nested series. The key in the new series is the last key of the window. This function
 skips incomplete chunks - you can use `Series.windowSize` for more options.

### [Series.chunkInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunkInto)

Series.chunkInto 
chunkInto 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks and then applies the provided
 value selector `f` on each chunk to produce the result which is returned as a new series.
 The key in the new series is the last key of the chunk. This function
 skips incomplete chunks - you can use `Series.chunkSizeInto` for more options.

### [Series.chunk](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#chunk)

Series.chunk 
chunk 

 Aggregates the input into a series of adacent chunks and returns the produced chunks as
 a nested series. The key in the new series is the last key of the chunk. This function
 skips incomplete chunks - you can use `Series.chunkSize` for more options.

### [Series.pairwise](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#pairwise)

Series.pairwise 
pairwise 

 Returns a series containing the predecessor and an element for each input, except
 for the first one. The returned series is one key shorter (it does not contain a
 value for the first key).

### [Series.pairwiseWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#pairwiseWith)

Series.pairwiseWith 
pairwiseWith 

 Aggregates the input into pairs containing the predecessor and an element for each input, except
 for the first one. Then calls the specified aggregation function `f` with a tuple and a key.
 The returned series is one key shorter (it does not contain a  value for the first key).

### [Series.groupInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#groupInto)

Series.groupInto 
groupInto 

 Groups a series (ordered or unordered) using the specified key selector (`keySelector`)
 and then aggregates each group into a single value, returned in the resulting series,
 using the provided `f` function.

### [Series.groupBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#groupBy)

Series.groupBy 
groupBy 

 Groups a series (ordered or unordered) using the specified key selector (`keySelector`)
 and then returns a series of (nested) series as the result. The outer series is indexed by
 the newly produced keys, the nested series are indexed with the original keys.

### [Series.withMissingFrom](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#withMissingFrom)

Series.withMissingFrom 
withMissingFrom 

 Returns the current series with the same index but with values missing wherever the
 corresponding key exists in the other series index with an associated missing value.

### [Series.dropMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#dropMissing)

Series.dropMissing 
dropMissing 

 Drop missing values from the specified series. The returned series contains
 only those keys for which there is a value available in the original one.

### [Series.fillMissingUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillMissingUsing)

Series.fillMissingUsing 
fillMissingUsing 

 Fill missing values in the series using the specified function.
 The specified function is called with all keys for which the series
 does not contain value and the result of the call is used in place
 of the missing value.
  

 This function can be used to implement more complex interpolation.
 For example see [handling missing values in the tutorial](../frame.html#missing)

### [Series.fillMissingWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillMissingWith)

Series.fillMissingWith 
fillMissingWith 

 Fill missing values in the series with a constant value.

### [Series.fillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillMissing)

Series.fillMissing 
fillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the series with the nearest available value
 (using the specified direction). Note that the series may still contain
 missing values after call to this function. This operation can only be
 used on ordered series.

### [Series.fillMissingBetween](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillMissingBetween)

Series.fillMissingBetween 
fillMissingBetween 

 Fill missing values only between `startKey` and `endKey`, inclusive.

### [Series.fillMissingInside](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#fillMissingInside)

Series.fillMissingInside 
fillMissingInside 

 Fill missing values only between the first and last non-missing values.

### [Series.sortWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sortWith)

Series.sortWith 
sortWith 

 Returns a new series, containing the observations of the original series sorted using
 the specified comparison function.

### [Series.sortBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sortBy)

Series.sortBy 
sortBy 

 Returns a new series, containing the observations of the original series sorted by
 values returned by the specified projection function.

### [Series.sortByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sortByKey)

Series.sortByKey 
sortByKey 

 Returns a new series whose observations are sorted according to keys of the index.

### [Series.sort](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sort)

Series.sort 
sort 

 Returns a new series, containing the observations of the original series sorted based
 on the default ordering defined on the values of the series.

### [Series.rank](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#rank)

Series.rank 
rank 

 Returns a new series containing the dense rank of each value in the original series.
 The rank is 1-based: the smallest value receives rank 1, the next distinct value
 receives rank 2, and so on. Equal values (ties) receive the same rank. Missing values
 in the input are propagated as missing in the output.

### [Series.rankWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#rankWith)

Series.rankWith 
rankWith 

 Returns a new series containing the dense rank of each value in the original series,
 using a custom comparison function to determine the ordering. The rank is 1-based:
 the value that sorts first (lowest by the comparator) receives rank 1. Values that
 compare as equal by the comparator receive the same rank. Missing values in the input
 are propagated as missing in the output.

### [Series.ntile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#ntile)

Series.ntile 
ntile 

 Divides the values of the series into <span class="fsdocs-param-name">groups</span> equal-sized buckets
 numbered 0 to <code>groups - 1</code>. Bucket 0 contains the smallest values and bucket
 <code>groups - 1</code> contains the largest. When the series length is not evenly divisible
 by <span class="fsdocs-param-name">groups</span>, earlier buckets receive one extra element. Missing values
 in the input are propagated as missing in the output.

### [Series.rev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#rev)

Series.rev 
rev 

 Returns a new series, containing the observations of the original series in a reverse order.

### [Series.realign](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#realign)

Series.realign 
realign 

 Given an original series and a sequence of keys, returns a new series that contains
 the matching value for each of the specified keys. The `KeyCount` of the returned
 sequence is the length of `keys`. If there is no value for the specified keys in the
 input sequence, the returned series will contain a missing value.

### [Series.indexOrdinally](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#indexOrdinally)

Series.indexOrdinally 
indexOrdinally 

 Return a new series containing the same values as the original series, but with
 ordinal index formed by `int` values starting from 0.

### [Series.indexWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#indexWith)

Series.indexWith 
indexWith 

 Returns a new series containing the specified keys mapped to the original values of the series.
 When the sequence contains _fewer_ keys, the values from the series are dropped. When it
 contains _more_ keys, the values for additional keys are missing.

### [Series.resampleInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resampleInto)

Series.resampleInto 
resampleInto 

 Resample the series based on a provided collection of keys. The values of the series
 are aggregated into chunks based on the specified keys. Depending on `direction`, the
 specified key is either used as the smallest or as the greatest key of the chunk (with
 the exception of boundaries that are added to the first/last chunk).
 Such chunks are then aggregated using the provided function `f`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.resample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resample)

Series.resample 
resample 

 Resample the series based on a provided collection of keys. The values of the series
 are aggregated into chunks based on the specified keys. Depending on `direction`, the
 specified key is either used as the smallest or as the greatest key of the chunk (with
 the exception of boundaries that are added to the first/last chunk).
 Such chunks are then returned as nested series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.resampleEquivInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resampleEquivInto)

Series.resampleEquivInto 
resampleEquivInto 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys. A specified function
 `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and the observations for which the
 projected keys are equivalent are grouped into chunks. The chunks are then transformed
 to values using the provided function `f`.
  

 This function is similar to `Series.chunkBy`, with the exception that it transforms
 keys to a new space.

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered. For unordered
 series, similar functionality can be implemented using `Series.groupBy`.

### [Series.resampleEquiv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resampleEquiv)

Series.resampleEquiv 
resampleEquiv 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys. A specified function
 `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and the observations for which the
 projected keys are equivalent are grouped into chunks. The chunks are then returned
 as nested series.
  

 This function is similar to `Series.chunkBy`, with the exception that it transforms
 keys to a new space.

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered. For unordered
 series, similar functionality can be implemented using `Series.groupBy`.

### [Series.resampleUniformInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resampleUniformInto)

Series.resampleUniformInto 
resampleUniformInto 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys and also generate values
 for all keys of the target space that are between the minimal and maximal key of the
 specified series (e.g. generate value for all days in the range covered by the series).
 A specified function `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and `nextKey`
 is used to generate all keys in the range. The chunk is then aggregated using `f`.

 When there are no values for a (generated) key, then the function behaves according to
 `fillMode`. It can look at the greatest value of previous chunk or smallest value of the
 next chunk, or it produces an empty series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.resampleUniform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#resampleUniform)

Series.resampleUniform 
resampleUniform 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys and also generate values
 for all keys of the target space that are between the minimal and maximal key of the
 specified series (e.g. generate value for all days in the range covered by the series).
 A specified function `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and `nextKey`
 is used to generate all keys in the range. Then return the chunks as nested series.

 When there are no values for a (generated) key, then the function behaves according to
 `fillMode`. It can look at the greatest value of previous chunk or smallest value of the
 next chunk, or it produces an empty series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.sampleTimeInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sampleTimeInto)

Series.sampleTimeInto 
sampleTimeInto 

 Performs sampling by time and aggregates chunks obtained by time-sampling into a single
 value using a specified function. The operation generates keys starting at the first
 key in the source series, using the specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on
 these keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.sampleTimeAtInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sampleTimeAtInto)

Series.sampleTimeAtInto 
sampleTimeAtInto 

 Performs sampling by time and aggregates chunks obtained by time-sampling into a single
 value using a specified function. The operation generates keys starting at the given
 `start` time, using the specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on these
 keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.sampleTime](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sampleTime)

Series.sampleTime 
sampleTime 

 Performs sampling by time and returns chunks obtained by time-sampling as a nested
 series. The operation generates keys starting at the first key in the source series,
 using the specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on these
 keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.sampleTimeAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sampleTimeAt)

Series.sampleTimeAt 
sampleTimeAt 

 Performs sampling by time and returns chunks obtained by time-sampling as a nested
 series. The operation generates keys starting at the given `start` time, using the
 specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on these
 keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.lookupTime](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lookupTime)

Series.lookupTime 
lookupTime 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting from the smallest key of the original series, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds values close to such keys using the specified `lookup` and `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.lookupTimeAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#lookupTimeAt)

Series.lookupTimeAt 
lookupTimeAt 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting at the specified `start` time, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds values close to such keys using the specified `lookup` and `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series.merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#merge)

Series.merge 
merge 

 Merge two series with distinct keys. When the same key with a value occurs in both
 series, an exception is thrown. In that case, you can use `mergeUsing`, which allows
 specifying merging behavior.

### [Series.replace](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#replace)

Series.replace 
replace 

 Replace series value given in key with value.

### [Series.replaceArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#replaceArray)

Series.replaceArray 
replaceArray 

 Replace series values given in keys with value.

### [Series.intersect](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#intersect)

Series.intersect 
intersect 

 Returns a new series which is intersection of two series by (key, value) pair.

### [Series.compare](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#compare)

Series.compare 
compare 

 Compares two series and returns a new series of `Diff`s.

### [Series.mergeUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#mergeUsing)

Series.mergeUsing 
mergeUsing 

 Merge two series with possibly overlapping keys. The `behavior` parameter specifies
 how to handle situation when a value is definedin both series.

### [Series.mergeAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#mergeAll)

Series.mergeAll 
mergeAll 

 Merge multiple series with distinct keys. When the same key with a value occurs in two
 of the series, an exception is thrown. This function is efficient even when the number
 of series to be merged is large.

### [Series.zip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#zip)

Series.zip 
zip 

 Align and zip two series using outer join and exact key matching. The function returns
 a series of tuples where both elements may be missing. As a result, it is often easier
 to use join on frames instead.

### [Series.zipAlign](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#zipAlign)

Series.zipAlign 
zipAlign 

 Align and zip two series using the specified joining mechanism and key matching.
 The function returns a series of tuples where both elements may be missing. As a result,
 it is often easier to use join on frames instead.

### [Series.zipInner](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#zipInner)

Series.zipInner 
zipInner 

 Align and zip two series using inner join and exact key matching. The function returns
 a series of tuples with values from the two series.

### [Series.zipAlignInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#zipAlignInto)

Series.zipAlignInto 
zipAlignInto 

 Align and zip two series using the specified joining mechanism and key matching.
 The function calls the specified function `op` to combine values from the two series

### [Series.zipInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#zipInto)

Series.zipInto 
zipInto 

 Align and zip two series using inner join and exact key matching (use `zipAlignInto`
 for more options). The function calls the specified function `op` to combine values
 from the two series

### [Series.diffDate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#diffDate)

Series.diffDate 
diffDate 
<pre> Returns a series containing the difference (as <c>TimeSpan</c>) between a
 <c>DateTime</c> value in the original series and a <c>DateTime</c> value at the
 specified offset. For example, calling <c>Series.diffDate 1 s</c> returns a series
 where the previous timestamp is subtracted from the current one. In pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 Note: Unlike <c>Series.diff</c> for numeric types, this function handles the
 heterogeneous subtraction (DateTime - DateTime = TimeSpan) by using <c>shift</c>
 and <c>zipInto</c> internally.

 ## Parameters
  - `offset` - When positive, subtracts the past values from the current values;
    when negative, subtracts the future values from the current values.
  - `series` - The input series of <c>DateTime</c> values.

 [category:Series transformations]</pre>

### [Series.diffDateOffset](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#diffDateOffset)

Series.diffDateOffset 
diffDateOffset 
<pre> Returns a series containing the difference (as <c>TimeSpan</c>) between a
 <c>DateTimeOffset</c> value in the original series and a <c>DateTimeOffset</c> value
 at the specified offset. For example, calling <c>Series.diffDateOffset 1 s</c> returns
 a series where the previous timestamp is subtracted from the current one. In pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 Note: Unlike <c>Series.diff</c> for numeric types, this function handles the
 heterogeneous subtraction (DateTimeOffset - DateTimeOffset = TimeSpan) by using
 <c>shift</c> and <c>zipInto</c> internally.

 ## Parameters
  - `offset` - When positive, subtracts the past values from the current values;
    when negative, subtracts the future values from the current values.
  - `series` - The input series of <c>DateTimeOffset</c> values.

 [category:Series transformations]</pre>

### [Series.sweepLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series.html#sweepLevel)

Series.sweepLevel 
sweepLevel 

 For each element of the input series, computes a summary value for the group it belongs to
 (using <code>applyLevel</code>) and then combines each element with its group summary using
 <code>sweepOp</code>. This is analogous to the R <code>sweep</code> operation and is useful for
 normalising or relativising values within groups.

 For example, to express each value as a fraction of its group sum:
 <pre class="fssnip highlighted"><code lang="fsharp">   <span class="k">let</span> <span data-fsdocs-tip="fs1" data-fsdocs-tip-unique="1" class="id">s</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="id">series</span> <span class="pn">[</span> <span class="pn">(</span><span class="n">1</span><span class="pn">,</span><span class="s">"a"</span><span class="pn">)</span> <span class="o">=></span> <span class="n">10.0</span><span class="pn">;</span> <span class="pn">(</span><span class="n">1</span><span class="pn">,</span><span class="s">"b"</span><span class="pn">)</span> <span class="o">=></span> <span class="n">40.0</span><span class="pn">;</span> <span class="pn">(</span><span class="n">2</span><span class="pn">,</span><span class="s">"a"</span><span class="pn">)</span> <span class="o">=></span> <span class="n">3.0</span><span class="pn">;</span> <span class="pn">(</span><span class="n">2</span><span class="pn">,</span><span class="s">"b"</span><span class="pn">)</span> <span class="o">=></span> <span class="n">7.0</span> <span class="pn">]</span>
   <span data-fsdocs-tip="fs1" data-fsdocs-tip-unique="2" class="id">s</span> <span class="o">|></span> <span class="id">Series</span><span class="pn">.</span><span class="id">sweepLevel</span> <span data-fsdocs-tip="fs2" data-fsdocs-tip-unique="3" class="id">fst</span> <span class="id">Stats</span><span class="pn">.</span><span class="id">sum</span> <span class="pn">(</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="pn">)</span>
   <span class="c">// (1,"a") => 0.2   (10/50)</span>
   <span class="c">// (1,"b") => 0.8   (40/50)</span>
   <span class="c">// (2,"a") => 0.3   (3/10)</span>
   <span class="c">// (2,"b") => 0.7   (7/10)</span>
</code></pre>
<div popover class="fsdocs-tip" id="fs1">val s: obj</div>
<div popover class="fsdocs-tip" id="fs2">val fst: tuple: ('T1 * 'T2) -> 'T1</div>

### [StatsInternal.toFloat](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#toFloat)

StatsInternal.toFloat 
toFloat

### [StatsInternal.applySeriesProj](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#applySeriesProj)

StatsInternal.applySeriesProj 
applySeriesProj 
 Apply transformation on series elements. The projection function `proj` always
 returns `float`, but may return `nan` to indicate that the value is not available.
 The resulting sequence should have the same number of values as the input sequence

### [StatsInternal.applySeriesProjLazy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#applySeriesProjLazy)

StatsInternal.applySeriesProjLazy 
applySeriesProjLazy 
 Like `applySeriesProj`, but accepts a series of any value type and converts lazily
 to `float opt` before passing to `proj`, avoiding an intermediate `Series<'K, float>`.
 Must be non-inline so the internal `Series(...)` constructor is called from the library.

### [StatsInternal.movingWindowFn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#movingWindowFn)

StatsInternal.movingWindowFn 
movingWindowFn 
<pre> Helper for moving window calculations (adopted from `Seq.windowed` in F# code base)
 When calling `finit`, we do not copy the array - this is fine, because the function
 is internal (and the only use in `applyMovingSumsTransform` is correct)

 # Parameters
   - `winSize` - The size of the window to create
   - `finit` takes the first fully populated window array to an initial state
   - `fupdate` takes the current state, incoming observation,
      and out-going observation to the next state
   - `ftransf` takes the current state to the current output

 Returns a `float[]` of the same length as the source sequence.</pre>

### [StatsInternal.initSumsDense](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#initSumsDense)

StatsInternal.initSumsDense 
initSumsDense 
 Given an initial array of values, calculate the initial `Sums` value
 (only required elements of `Sums` are calculated based on `moment`)

### [StatsInternal.updateSumsDense](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#updateSumsDense)

StatsInternal.updateSumsDense 
updateSumsDense 
 Given an existing `state` of type `Sums`, new incoming element and
 an old outgoing element, update the sums value
 (only required elements of `Sums` are calculated based on `moment`)

### [StatsInternal.initSumsSparse](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#initSumsSparse)

StatsInternal.initSumsSparse 
initSumsSparse 
 Pick only available values from the input array and call `initSumsDense`
 (no need to handle `nan` values, because those are returned as Missing by Deedle)
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type
 is not convertible to floating point number.

### [StatsInternal.updateSumsSparse](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#updateSumsSparse)

StatsInternal.updateSumsSparse 
updateSumsSparse 
 Update `Sums` value using `updateSumsDense`, but handle the case
 when removing/adding value that is missing (`OptionalValue.Missing`)

### [StatsInternal.applyMovingSumsTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#applyMovingSumsTransform)

StatsInternal.applyMovingSumsTransform 
applyMovingSumsTransform 
 Apply moving window transformation based on `Sums` calculation. The `proj` function
 calculates the statistics from `Sums` value and the `moment` specifies which of the
 `Sums` properties are calculated during the processing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

### [StatsInternal.varianceSums](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#varianceSums)

StatsInternal.varianceSums 
varianceSums 
 Calculate variance from `Sums`; requires `moment=2`

### [StatsInternal.skewSums](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#skewSums)

StatsInternal.skewSums 
skewSums 
 Calculate skewness from `Sums`; requires `moment=3`

### [StatsInternal.kurtSums](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#kurtSums)

StatsInternal.kurtSums 
kurtSums 
 Calculate kurtosis from `Sums`; requires `moment=4`

### [StatsInternal.movingMinMaxHelper](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#movingMinMaxHelper)

StatsInternal.movingMinMaxHelper 
movingMinMaxHelper 
 O(n) moving min/max calculator

 Keeps double-ended queue of values sorted acording to the specified order,
 such that the front is the min/max value. During the iteration, new value is
 added to the end (and all values that are greater/smaller than the new value
 are removed before it is appended).

### [StatsInternal.movingMedianHelper](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#movingMedianHelper)

StatsInternal.movingMedianHelper 
movingMedianHelper 
 Moving median calculator using a circular buffer.
 Yields nan for the first winSize-1 elements (incomplete windows).

### [StatsInternal.expandingWindowFn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#expandingWindowFn)

StatsInternal.expandingWindowFn 
expandingWindowFn 
<pre> Helper for expanding window calculations.
 Returns a `float[]` of the same length as the source sequence.

 # Parameters
   - `initState` - the initial state of the computation
   - `fupdate` - takes the current state and incoming observation to the next state
   - `ftransf` - takes the current state to the current output
   - `source` - the input sequence to process</pre>

### [StatsInternal.updateMoments](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#updateMoments)

StatsInternal.updateMoments 
updateMoments 
 Updates the moments using the Knuth/Welford algorithm for online stats updating
 (See: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithms_for_calculating_variance#Online_algorithm)

### [StatsInternal.updateMomentsSparse](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#updateMomentsSparse)

StatsInternal.updateMomentsSparse 
updateMomentsSparse 
 Updates the moments using `updateMoments`, but skips over missing values

### [StatsInternal.applyExpandingMomentsTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#applyExpandingMomentsTransform)

StatsInternal.applyExpandingMomentsTransform 
applyExpandingMomentsTransform 
 Given a series, calculates expanding moments (using online `updateMoments`)
 The specified `proj` function is used to calculate the resulting value
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

### [StatsInternal.valuesAllOpt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#valuesAllOpt)

StatsInternal.valuesAllOpt 
valuesAllOpt 
 Returns all values of a series as an array of `OptionalValue`s

### [StatsInternal.trySeriesExtreme](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#trySeriesExtreme)

StatsInternal.trySeriesExtreme 
trySeriesExtreme 
 Calculates minimum or maximum using the specified function 'f'
 Returns None when there are no values or Some.

### [StatsInternal.quickSelectInplace](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal.html#quickSelectInplace)

StatsInternal.quickSelectInplace 
quickSelectInplace 
 Returns the nth smallest element from the specified array.
 (QuickSelect implementation based on: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quickselect)

### [Moments](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal-moments.html)

Moments 
 Represents the moments as calculated during online processing
 (`nobs` is the count, `sum` is the sum, `M1` to `M4` are moments)

### [Sums](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal-sums.html)

Sums 
 When calculating moments, this record is used to keep track of the
 count (`nobs`), sum of values (`sum`), sum of squares (`sum2`),
 sum of values to the power of 3 and 3 (`sum3` and `sum4`) 
Sums.nobs 
nobs 
Sums.sum 
sum 
Sums.sump2 
sump2 
Sums.sump3 
sump3 
Sums.sump4

### [Sums.sum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal-sums.html#sum)

Sums.sum

Sums.sump2 
sump2

Sums.sump3 
sump3

### [Sums.sump4](/Deedle/reference/deedle-statsinternal-sums.html#sump4)

Sums.sump4 
sump4

### [Aggregation<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html)

Aggregation<'K> 

 Represents a strategy for aggregating data in an ordered series into data segments.
 To create a value of this type from C#, use the non-generic `Aggregation` type.
 Data can be aggregate using floating windows or chunks of a specified size or
 by specifying a condition on two keys (i.e. end a window/chunk when the condition
 no longer holds).
  
Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkSize 
IsChunkSize 
Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhileFromEnd 
IsWindowWhileFromEnd 
Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkWhile 
IsChunkWhile 
Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhile 
IsWindowWhile 
Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowSize 
IsWindowSize 
Aggregation<'K>.WindowSize 
WindowSize 
Aggregation<'K>.ChunkSize 
ChunkSize 
Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhile 
WindowWhile 
Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhileFromEnd 
WindowWhileFromEnd 
Aggregation<'K>.ChunkWhile 
ChunkWhile

### [Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#IsChunkSize)

Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkSize 
IsChunkSize

### [Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#IsWindowWhileFromEnd)

Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhileFromEnd 
IsWindowWhileFromEnd

### [Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#IsChunkWhile)

Aggregation<'K>.IsChunkWhile 
IsChunkWhile

### [Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#IsWindowWhile)

Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowWhile 
IsWindowWhile

### [Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#IsWindowSize)

Aggregation<'K>.IsWindowSize 
IsWindowSize

### [Aggregation<'K>.WindowSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#WindowSize)

Aggregation<'K>.WindowSize 
WindowSize 
 Aggregate data into floating windows of a specified size
 and the provided handling of boundary elements.

### [Aggregation<'K>.ChunkSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#ChunkSize)

Aggregation<'K>.ChunkSize 
ChunkSize 
 Aggregate data into non-overlapping chunks of a specified size
 and the provided handling of boundary elements.

### [Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#WindowWhile)

Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhile 
WindowWhile 
 Aggregate data into floating windows where each window ends as soon
 as the specified function returns `false` when called with the
 first key and the current key as arguments.

### [Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#WindowWhileFromEnd)

Aggregation<'K>.WindowWhileFromEnd 
WindowWhileFromEnd 
 Aggregate data into floating windows where each window ends at the current
 element and starts at the earliest key such that the specified function
 returns `true` when called with the start key and the current (end) key.
 The key of each window is the last key of the window.

### [Aggregation<'K>.ChunkWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation-1.html#ChunkWhile)

Aggregation<'K>.ChunkWhile 
ChunkWhile 
 Aggregate data into non-overlapping chunks where each chunk ends as soon
 as the specified function returns `false` when called with the
 first key and the current key as arguments.

### [Aggregation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html)

Aggregation 

 A non-generic type that simplifies the construction of <code>Aggregation<K></code> values
 from C#. It provides methods for constructing different kinds of aggregation
 strategies for ordered series.
  
Aggregation.ChunkSize 
ChunkSize 
Aggregation.ChunkWhile 
ChunkWhile 
Aggregation.WindowSize 
WindowSize 
Aggregation.WindowWhile 
WindowWhile 
Aggregation.WindowWhileFromEnd 
WindowWhileFromEnd

### [Aggregation.ChunkSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html#ChunkSize)

Aggregation.ChunkSize 
ChunkSize 

 Aggregate data into non-overlapping chunks of a specified size
 and the provided handling of boundary elements.

### [Aggregation.ChunkWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html#ChunkWhile)

Aggregation.ChunkWhile 
ChunkWhile 

 Aggregate data into non-overlapping chunks where each chunk ends as soon
 as the specified function returns `false` when called with the
 first key and the current key as arguments.

### [Aggregation.WindowSize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html#WindowSize)

Aggregation.WindowSize 
WindowSize 

 Aggregate data into floating windows of a specified size
 and the provided handling of boundary elements.

### [Aggregation.WindowWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html#WindowWhile)

Aggregation.WindowWhile 
WindowWhile 

 Aggregate data into floating windows where each window ends as soon
 as the specified function returns `false` when called with the
 first key and the current key as arguments.

### [Aggregation.WindowWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-aggregation.html#WindowWhileFromEnd)

Aggregation.WindowWhileFromEnd 
WindowWhileFromEnd 

 Aggregate data into floating windows where each window ends at the current
 element. The window starts at the earliest key for which the specified function
 returns <code>true</code> when called with the start key and the current (end) key.
 The key of each produced window is the last (end) key of the window.

### [Boundary](/Deedle/reference/deedle-boundary.html)

Boundary 

 Represents boundary behaviour for operations such as floating window. The type
 specifies whether incomplete windows (of smaller than required length) should be
 produced at the beginning (`AtBeginning`) or at the end (`AtEnding`) or
 skipped (`Skip`). For chunking, combinations are allowed too - to skip incomplete
 chunk at the beginning, use `Boundary.Skip ||| Boundary.AtBeginning`.
  
Boundary.AtBeginning 
AtBeginning 
Boundary.AtEnding 
AtEnding 
Boundary.Skip 
Skip

### [Boundary.AtBeginning](/Deedle/reference/deedle-boundary.html#AtBeginning)

Boundary.AtBeginning 
AtBeginning

### [Boundary.AtEnding](/Deedle/reference/deedle-boundary.html#AtEnding)

Boundary.AtEnding 
AtEnding

### [Boundary.Skip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-boundary.html#Skip)

Boundary.Skip 
Skip

### [ColumnSeries<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-columnseries-2.html)

ColumnSeries<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey> 

 Represents a series of columns from a frame. The type inherits from a series of
 series representing individual columns (<code>Series<'TColumnKey, ObjectSeries<'TRowKey>></code>) but
 hides slicing operations with new versions that return frames.

### [ConversionKind](/Deedle/reference/deedle-conversionkind.html)

ConversionKind 

 Represents different kinds of type conversions that can be used by Deedle internally.
 This is used, for example, when converting <code>ObjectSeries<'K></code> to <code>Series<'K, 'T></code> -
 The conversion kind can be specified as an argument to allow certain conversions.
  
ConversionKind.Exact 
Exact 
ConversionKind.Safe 
Safe 
ConversionKind.Flexible 
Flexible

### [ConversionKind.Exact](/Deedle/reference/deedle-conversionkind.html#Exact)

ConversionKind.Exact 
Exact 
 Specifies that the type has to match exactly and no conversions are performed.

### [ConversionKind.Safe](/Deedle/reference/deedle-conversionkind.html#Safe)

ConversionKind.Safe 
Safe 
 Allows conversions that widen numeric types, but nothing else. This includes
 conversions on decimals `decimal -> float32 -> float` and also from integers
 to floating points (`int -> decimal` etc.)

### [ConversionKind.Flexible](/Deedle/reference/deedle-conversionkind.html#Flexible)

ConversionKind.Flexible 
Flexible 
 Allows the use of arbitrary .NET conversions. This uses `Convert.ChangeType`, which
 performs numerical conversions, parsing of strings, uses `IConvertable` and more.

### [DataSegment<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-datasegment-1.html)

DataSegment<'T> 

 Represents a segment of a series or sequence. The value is returned from
 various functions that aggregate data into chunks or floating windows. The
 `Complete` case represents complete segment (e.g. of the specified size) and
 `Boundary` represents segment at the boundary (e.g. smaller than the required
 size).
  
DataSegment<'T>.Kind 
Kind 
DataSegment<'T>.Data 
Data 
DataSegment<'T>.DataSegment 
DataSegment

### [DataSegment<'T>.Kind](/Deedle/reference/deedle-datasegment-1.html#Kind)

DataSegment<'T>.Kind 
Kind 
 Return the kind of this segment

### [DataSegment<'T>.Data](/Deedle/reference/deedle-datasegment-1.html#Data)

DataSegment<'T>.Data 
Data 
 Returns the data associated with the segment
 (for boundary segment, this may be smaller than the required window size)

### [DataSegment<'T>.DataSegment](/Deedle/reference/deedle-datasegment-1.html#DataSegment)

### [DelayedSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html)

DelayedSeries 

 This type exposes a single static method `DelayedSeries.Create` that can be used for
 constructing data series (of type <code>Series<K, V></code>) with lazily loaded data. You can
 use this functionality to create series that represents e.g. an entire price history
 in a database, but only loads data that are actually needed. For more information
 see the [lazy data loading tutorial](../lazysource.html).
  
DelayedSeries.Create 
Create 
DelayedSeries.Create 
Create 
DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader 
FromIndexVectorLoader 
DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader 
FromIndexVectorLoader 
DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader 
FromValueLoader 
DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader 
FromValueLoader

### [DelayedSeries.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#Create)

DelayedSeries.Create 
Create

### [DelayedSeries.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#Create)

DelayedSeries.Create 
Create

### [DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#FromIndexVectorLoader)

DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader 
FromIndexVectorLoader

### [DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#FromIndexVectorLoader)

DelayedSeries.FromIndexVectorLoader 
FromIndexVectorLoader

### [DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#FromValueLoader)

DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader 
FromValueLoader 

 An F#-friendly function that creates lazily loaded series. The method requires
 the overall range of the series (smallest and greatest key) and a function that
 loads the data. The function is called with two tuples that specify lower and upper
 boundary. It returns an asynchronous workflow that produces the data.
  

 For more information see the [lazy data loading tutorial](../lazysource.html).

### [DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-delayedseries.html#FromValueLoader)

DelayedSeries.FromValueLoader 
FromValueLoader 

 A C#-friendly function that creates lazily loaded series. The method requires
 the overall range of the series (smallest and greatest key) and a function that
 loads the data. In this overload, the function is a `Func` delegate taking
 information about the requested range and returning <code>Task<T></code> that produces the data.
  

 For more information see the [lazy data loading tutorial](../lazysource.html).
 The operation calls `loader` (and so creates the tasks) on the thread that is
 requesting the result.

### [Diff<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-diff-1.html)

Diff<'T> 

 Represents a difference operation applied to a value of type <code>'T</code>.
 A difference is either a <code>Change</code> (old and new value), a <code>Remove</code>, or an <code>Add</code>.

### [EnumerableExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-enumerableextensions.html)

EnumerableExtensions 

 Contains C#-friendly extension methods for various instances of `IEnumerable`
 that can be used for creating <code>Series<'K, 'V></code> from the `IEnumerable` value.
 You can create an ordinal series from <code>IEnumerable<'T></code> or an indexed series from
 <code>IEnumerable<KeyValuePair<'K, 'V>></code> or from <code>IEnumerable<KeyValuePair<'K, OptionalValue<'V>>></code>.
  
EnumerableExtensions.ToOrdinalSeries 
ToOrdinalSeries 
EnumerableExtensions.ToSeries 
ToSeries 
EnumerableExtensions.ToSparseSeries 
ToSparseSeries

### [EnumerableExtensions.ToOrdinalSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-enumerableextensions.html#ToOrdinalSeries)

EnumerableExtensions.ToOrdinalSeries 
ToOrdinalSeries 
 Convert the `IEnumerable` to a `Series`, using the seuqence as the values
 of the resulting series. The keys are generated ordinarilly, starting from 0.

### [EnumerableExtensions.ToSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-enumerableextensions.html#ToSeries)

EnumerableExtensions.ToSeries 
ToSeries 
 Convert the `IEnumerable` to a `Series`, using the keys and values of
 the `KeyValuePair` as keys and values of the resulting series.

### [EnumerableExtensions.ToSparseSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-enumerableextensions.html#ToSparseSeries)

EnumerableExtensions.ToSparseSeries 
ToSparseSeries 
 Convert the `IEnumerable` to a  `Series`, using the keys and values of
 the `KeyValuePair` as keys and values of the resulting series.
 `OptionalValue.Missing` can be used to denote missing values.

### [Extrapolation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html)

Extrapolation 

 The `Stats` type contains functions for fast calculation of statistics over
 series and frames as well as over a moving and an expanding window in a series.

 The resulting series has the same keys as the input series. When there are
 no values, or missing values, different functions behave in different ways.
 Statistics (e.g. mean) return missing value when any value is missing, while min/max
 functions return the minimal/maximal element (skipping over missing values).
 
 Specifies the extrapolation behavior used by <code>Stats.interpolateLinearWith</code>
 for keys that fall outside the range of the source series.
  
<p class='fsdocs-para'>Series statistics:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Functions such as `count`, `mean`, `kurt` etc. return the
 statistics calculated over all values of a series. The calculation skips
 over missing values (or `nan` values), so for example `mean` returns the
 average of all _present_ values.
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Frame statistics:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 The standard functions are exposed as static members and are
 overloaded. This means that they can be applied to both `Series<'K, float>` and
 to `Frame<'R, 'C>`. When applied to data frame, the functions apply the
 statistical calculation to all numerical columns of the frame.
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Moving windows:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Moving window means that the window has a fixed size and moves over the series.
 In this case, the result of the statisitcs is always attached to the last key
 of the window. The function names are prefixed with `moving`.
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Expanding windows:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Expanding window means that the window starts as a single-element sized window
 and expands as it moves over the series. In this case, statistics is calculated
 for all values up to the current key. This means that the result is attached
 to the key at the end of the window. The function names are prefixed
 with `expanding`.
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Multi-level statistics:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 For a series with multi-level (hierarchical) index, the
 functions prefixed with `level` provide a way to apply statistical operation on
 a single level of the index. (For example you can sum values along the `'K1` keys
 in a series `Series<'K1 * 'K2, float>` and get `Series<'K1, float>` as the result.)
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 The windowing functions in the `Stats` type support calculations over a fixed-size
 windows specified by the size of the window. If you need more complex windowing
 behavior (such as window based on the distance between keys), different handling
 of boundary, or chunking (calculation over adjacent chunks), you can use chunking and
 windowing functions from the `Series` module such as `Series.windowSizeInto` or
 `Series.chunkSizeInto`.
 </p> 
Extrapolation.IsClamp 
IsClamp 
Extrapolation.IsMissing 
IsMissing 
Extrapolation.IsLinear 
IsLinear 
Extrapolation.Clamp 
Clamp 
Extrapolation.Missing 
Missing 
Extrapolation.Linear 
Linear

### [Extrapolation.IsClamp](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#IsClamp)

Extrapolation.IsClamp 
IsClamp

### [Extrapolation.IsMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#IsMissing)

Extrapolation.IsMissing 
IsMissing

### [Extrapolation.IsLinear](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#IsLinear)

Extrapolation.IsLinear 
IsLinear

### [Extrapolation.Clamp](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#Clamp)

Extrapolation.Clamp 
Clamp 
 Retain the nearest boundary value. This is the same behavior as
 <c>Stats.interpolateLinear</c>.

### [Extrapolation.Missing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#Missing)

Extrapolation.Missing 
Missing 
 Return <c>nan</c> for keys that are outside the source series range.

### [Extrapolation.Linear](/Deedle/reference/deedle-extrapolation.html#Linear)

Extrapolation.Linear 
Linear 
 Continue the boundary slope linearly beyond the series boundaries.

### [Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html)

### [Frame.FromArray2D](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#FromArray2D)

Frame.FromArray2D 
FromArray2D 

 Create data frame from a 2D array of values. The first dimension of the array
 is used as rows and the second dimension is treated as columns. Rows and columns
 of the returned frame are indexed with the element's offset in the array.

### [Frame.FromRecords](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#FromRecords)

Frame.FromRecords 
FromRecords 

 Creates a data frame from a sequence of any .NET objects. The method uses reflection
 over the specified type parameter `'T` and turns its properties to columns. The
 rows of the resulting frame are automatically indexed by `int`.

Frame.FromRecords 
FromRecords 
 Creates a data frame from a sequence of any .NET objects. The method uses reflection
 over the specified type parameter `'T` and turns its properties to columns.
