FromValues 
 Create a data frame from a sequence of tuples containing row key, column key and a value

### [Frame.FromValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#FromValues)

Frame.FromValues 
FromValues 

 Create a data frame from a sequence of objects and functions that return
 row key, column key and value for each object in the input sequence.

### [Frame.ReadCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#ReadCsv)

Frame.ReadCsv 
ReadCsv 

 Load data frame from a CSV file. The operation automatically reads column names from the
 CSV file (if they are present) and infers the type of values for each column. Columns
 of primitive types (`int`, `float`, etc.) are converted to the right type. Columns of other
 types (such as dates) are not converted automatically.

### [Frame.ReadCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#ReadCsv)

Frame.ReadCsv 
ReadCsv 

 Load data frame from a CSV file. The operation automatically reads column names from the
 CSV file (if they are present) and infers the type of values for each column. Columns
 of primitive types (`int`, `float`, etc.) are converted to the right type. Columns of other
 types (such as dates) are not converted automatically.

### [Frame.ReadReader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#ReadReader)

Frame.ReadReader 
ReadReader 

 Read data from `IDataReader`. The method reads all rows from the data reader
 and for each row, gets all the columns. When a value is `DBNull`, it is treated
 as missing. The types of created vectors are determined by the field types reported
 by the data reader.

### [Frame.NonExpandableInterfaces](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#NonExpandableInterfaces)

Frame.NonExpandableInterfaces 
NonExpandableInterfaces 

 Configures how reflection-based expansion behaves - see also `df.ExpandColumns`.
 This (mutable, non-thread-safe) collection specifies interfaces whose implementations
 should not be expanded. By default, this includes collections such as IList.

### [Frame.NonExpandableTypes](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#NonExpandableTypes)

Frame.NonExpandableTypes 
NonExpandableTypes 

 Configures how reflection-based expansion behaves - see also `df.ExpandColumns`.
 This (mutable, non-thread-safe) collection specifies additional primitive (but reference)
 types that should not be expaneded. By default, this includes DateTime, string, etc.

### [Frame.CustomExpanders](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame.html#CustomExpanders)

Frame.CustomExpanders 
CustomExpanders 

 Configures how reflection-based expansion behaves - see also `df.ExpandColumns`.
 This (mutable, non-thread-safe) collection lets you specify custom expansion behavior
 for any type. This is a dictionary with types as keys and functions that implement the
 expansion as values.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey> 

 A frame is the key Deedle data structure (together with series). It represents a
 data table (think spreadsheet or CSV file) with multiple rows and columns. The frame
 consists of row index, column index and data. The indices are used for efficient
 lookup when accessing data by the row key `'TRowKey` or by the column key
 `'TColumnKey`. Deedle frames are optimized for the scenario when all values in a given
 column are of the same type (but types of different columns can differ).
  
<p class='fsdocs-para'>Joining, zipping and appending:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 More info
 </p> 
Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

AddColumn

ColumnApply

DropColumn

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetAllColumns

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#``.ctor``)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 

 Creates a new data frame from a sequence of column keys and a corresponding sequence of series.
 The row index is determined by the union of the row indices of all provided series.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#``.ctor``)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#AddColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn 
AddColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by adding an additional data series
 as a new column with the specified column key. The operation
 uses left join and aligns new series to the existing frame keys.
 A parameter `lookup` can be used to specify how to find a value in the
 added series (if an exact key is not available). The `lookup` parameter
 can only be used with ordered indices.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#AddColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn 
AddColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by adding an additional data series
 as a new column with the specified column key. The sequence is
 aligned to the data frame based on ordering. If it is longer, it is
 trimmed and if it is shorter, missing values will be added.
 A parameter `lookup` can be used to specify how to find a value in the
 added series (if the sequence contains invalid values like `null` or `NaN`).

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#AddColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn 
AddColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by adding an additional data series
 as a new column with the specified column key. The operation
 uses left join and aligns new series to the existing frame keys.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#AddColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AddColumn 
AddColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by adding an additional data series
 as a new column with the specified column key. The sequence is
 aligned to the data frame based on ordering. If it is longer, it is
 trimmed and if it is shorter, missing values will be added.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AggregateRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#AggregateRowsBy)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.AggregateRowsBy 
AggregateRowsBy 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are grouped by `groupBy` and whose columns specified
 in `aggBy` are aggregated according to `aggFunc`.

ColumnApply

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.DropColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#DropColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.DropColumn 
DropColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by removing the specified series from the
 frame columns. The operation throws if the column key is not found.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Format](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Format)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Format 
Format 

 Shows the data frame content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows all columns, but a limited number of rows.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Format](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Format)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Format 
Format 

 Shows the data frame content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows all columns, but a limited number of rows.

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Format 
Format 

 Shows the data frame content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows all columns, but a limited number of rows.

Format 

 Shows the data frame content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows all columns, but a limited number of rows.

 Shows the data frame content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows all columns, but a limited number of rows.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#GetRow)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRow 
GetRow 

 Returns a row with the specieifed key. This method is generic and returns the result
 as a series containing values of the specified type. To get heterogeneous series of
 type <code>ObjectSeries<'TCol></code>, use the `frame.Rows` property.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#GetRow)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRow 
GetRow 

 Returns a row with the specieifed key. This method is generic and returns the result
 as a series containing values of the specified type. To get heterogeneous series of
 type <code>ObjectSeries<'TCol></code>, use the `frame.Rows` property.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRowAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#GetRowAt)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRowAt 
GetRowAt 

 Returns a row of the data frame that is located at the specified int offset.
 This does not use the row key and directly accesses the frame data. This method
 is generic and returns the result as a series containing values of the specified type.
 To get heterogeneous series of type <code>ObjectSeries<'TCol></code>, use the `frame.Rows` property.
 If the index is invalid, `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` is thrown. You can get the
 matching key at a specified index using `GetRowKeyAt`.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRowKeyAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#GetRowKeyAt)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRowKeyAt 
GetRowKeyAt 

 Returns the row key that is located at the specified int offset.
 If the index is invalid, `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` is thrown.
 You can get the corresponding row using `GetRowAt`.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#GetRows)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GetRows 
GetRows

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.GroupRowsUsing

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.IndexRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#IndexRows)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.IndexRows 
IndexRows 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. The generic type parameter is (typically) needed to specify the type of the
 values in the required index column.

 The resulting frame will *not* contain the specified column. If you want to preserve the
 column, use the overload that takes `keepColumn` parameter.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.IndexRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#IndexRows)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.IndexRows 
IndexRows 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame. The generic type parameter is (typically) needed to specify the type of the
 values in the required index column.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Performs outer join on data frame and a series. The column key for the joined
 series must not occur in the current data frame. The rows are automatically aligned
 and unavailable values are marked as missing.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Join data frame and a series. The column key for the joined series must not occur in the
 current data frame. The rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Join data frame and a series. The column key for the joined series must not occur in the
 current data frame. The rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.
 When the index of both objects is ordered, it is possible to specify `lookup`
 in order to align indices from other frame to the indices of the main frame
 (typically, to find the nearest key with available value for a key).

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Performs outer join on two data frames. The columns of the joined frames must not
 overlap and their rows are aligned. The unavailable values are marked as missing.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Join two data frames. The columns of the joined frames must not overlap and their
 rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.
 For more alignment options on ordered frames, see overload taking `lookup`.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Join)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Join 
Join 

 Join two data frames. The columns of the joined frames must not overlap and their
 rows are aligned and transformed according to the specified join kind.
 When the index of both frames is ordered, it is possible to specify `lookup`
 in order to align indices from other frame to the indices of the main frame
 (typically, to find the nearest key with available value for a key).

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Merge)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge 
Merge 

 Merge multiple data frames with non-overlapping values. The operation takes the union of columns
 and rows of the source data frames and then unions the values. An exception is thrown when
 both data frames define value for a column/row location, but the operation succeeds if one
 frame has a missing value at the location.

 Note that the rows are *not* automatically reindexed to avoid overlaps. This means that when
 a frame has rows indexed with ordinal numbers, you may need to explicitly reindex the row
 keys before calling append.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Merge)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge 
Merge 

 Merge multiple data frames with non-overlapping values. The operation takes the union of columns
 and rows of the source data frames and then unions the values. An exception is thrown when
 both data frames define value for a column/row location, but the operation succeeds if one
 frame has a missing value at the location.

 Note that the rows are *not* automatically reindexed to avoid overlaps. This means that when
 a frame has rows indexed with ordinal numbers, you may need to explicitly reindex the row
 keys before calling append.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Merge)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Merge 
Merge 

 Merge two data frames with non-overlapping values. The operation takes the union of columns
 and rows of the source data frames and then unions the values. An exception is thrown when
 both data frames define value for a column/row location, but the operation succeeds if one
 frame has a missing value at the location.

 Note that the rows are *not* automatically reindexed to avoid overlaps. This means that when
 a frame has rows indexed with ordinal numbers, you may need to explicitly reindex the row
 keys before calling append.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.RenameColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#RenameColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.RenameColumn 
RenameColumn

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.RenameColumns

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.RenameColumns 
RenameColumns

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ReplaceColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn 
ReplaceColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by replacing the specified series with
 a new data sequence . (If the series does not exist, only the new
 series is added.)

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ReplaceColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn 
ReplaceColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by replacing the specified series with
 a new series. (If the series does not exist, only the new
 series is added.)

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ReplaceColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn 
ReplaceColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by replacing the specified series with
 a new data sequence. (If the series does not exist, only the new series is added.)
 When adding a series, the specified `lookup` parameter is used for filling
 missing values (e.g. `null` or `NaN`). The parameter can only be used for
 frame with ordered indices.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ReplaceColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ReplaceColumn 
ReplaceColumn 

 Mutates the data frame by replacing the specified series with
 a new series. (If the series does not exist, only the new series is added.)
 When adding a series, the specified `lookup` parameter is used for matching
 keys. The parameter can only be used for frame with ordered indices.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Select](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Select)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Select

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.SelectValues

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.StrConcat](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#StrConcat)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.StrConcat 
StrConcat 
Piecewise concatenate two frames of string values

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToArray2D](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ToArray2D)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToArray2D 
ToArray2D 

 Returns data of the data frame as a 2D array. The method attempts to convert
 all values to the specified type 'R. If the specified type is 'float' or 'double'
 then the method automatically uses NaN. For other values, the default value has to
 be explicitly specified using another overload.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToArray2D](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ToArray2D)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToArray2D 
ToArray2D 

 Returns data of the data frame as a 2D array. The method attempts to convert
 all values to the specified type 'R. When a value is missing, the specified
 `defaultValue` is used.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToJaggedArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ToJaggedArray)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToJaggedArray 
ToJaggedArray 

 Returns data of the data frame as a jagged array. The method attempts to convert
 all values to the specified type 'R. If the specified type is 'float' or 'double'
 then the method automatically uses NaN. For other values, the default value has to
 be explicitly specified using another overload.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToJaggedArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#ToJaggedArray)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.ToJaggedArray 
ToJaggedArray 

 Returns data of the data frame as a jagged array. The method attempts to convert
 all values to the specified type 'R. When a value is missing, the specified
 `defaultValue` is used.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#TryGetColumn)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetColumn 
TryGetColumn

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetColumnObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#TryGetColumnObservation)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetColumnObservation 
TryGetColumnObservation

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#TryGetRow)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRow 
TryGetRow 

 Returns a row with the specieifed key wrapped in `OptionalValue`. When the specified key
 is not found, the result is `OptionalValue.Missing`. This method is generic and returns the result
 as a series containing values of the specified type. To get heterogeneous series of
 type <code>ObjectSeries<'TCol></code>, use the `frame.Rows` property.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#TryGetRow)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRow 
TryGetRow 

 Returns a row with the specieifed key wrapped in `OptionalValue`. When the specified key
 is not found, the result is `OptionalValue.Missing`. This method is generic and returns the result
 as a series containing values of the specified type. To get heterogeneous series of
 type <code>ObjectSeries<'TCol></code>, use the `frame.Rows` property.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRowObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#TryGetRowObservation)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.TryGetRowObservation 
TryGetRowObservation 

 Try to find a row with the specified row key, or using the specified `lookup` parameter,
 and return the found row together with its actual key in case `lookup` was used. In case the
 row is not found, `OptionalValue.Missing` is returned.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Zip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Zip)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Zip 
Zip 

 Aligns two data frames using both column index and row index and apply the specified operation
 on values of a specified type that are available in both data frames. This overload uses
 `JoinKind.Outer` for both columns and rows.

 Once aligned, the call <code>df1.Zip<T>(df2, f)</code> applies the specifed function `f` on all `T` values
 that are available in corresponding locations in both frames. For values of other types, the
 value from `df1` is returned.

### [Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Zip](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frame-2.html#Zip)

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.Zip 
Zip 

 Aligns two data frames using both column index and row index and apply the specified operation
 on values of a specified type that are available in both data frames. The parameters `columnKind`,
 and `rowKind` can be specified to determine how the alginment works (similarly to `Join`).
 Column keys are always matched using `Lookup.Exact`, but `lookup` determines lookup for rows.
 The parameter `pointwise` can be specified to determine the outcome of unmatched column.

 Once aligned, the call <code>df1.Zip<T>(df2, f)</code> applies the specifed function `f` on all `T` values
 that are available in corresponding locations in both frames. For values of other types, the
 value from `df1` is returned.

Frame<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.($) 
($) 
 Custom operator that can be used for applying fuction to all elements of
 a frame. This provides a nicer syntactic sugar for the `Frame.mapValues`
 function.

### [FrameData](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framedata.html)

FrameData 

 Represents the underlying (raw) data of the frame in a format that can
 be used for exporting data frame to other formats etc. (DataTable, CSV, Excel)
  
FrameData.ColumnKeys 
ColumnKeys 
FrameData.RowKeys 
RowKeys 
FrameData.Columns 
Columns

### [FrameData.ColumnKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framedata.html#ColumnKeys)

FrameData.ColumnKeys 
ColumnKeys 
 A sequence of keys for all column. Individual key is an array
 which contains multiple values for hierarchical indices

### [FrameData.RowKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framedata.html#RowKeys)

FrameData.RowKeys 
RowKeys 
 A sequence of keys for all rows. Individual key is an array
 which contains multiple values for hierarchical indices

### [FrameData.Columns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framedata.html#Columns)

FrameData.Columns 
Columns 
 Represents the data of the frame as a sequence of columns containing
 type and array with column values. `OptionalValue.Missing` is used to
 represent missing data.

### [FrameExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html)

FrameExtensions 

 Contains C# and F# extension methods for the `Frame<'R, 'C>` type. The members are
 automatically available when you import the `Deedle` namespace. The type contains
 object-oriented counterparts to most of the functionality from the `Frame` module.
  
<p class='fsdocs-para'>Data structure manipulation:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Summary 1
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Input and output:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Summary 2
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Missing values:</p><p class='fsdocs-para'>
 Summary 3
 </p>

Window

### [FrameExtensions.Compare](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Compare)

FrameExtensions.Compare 
Compare 

 Compares two data frames column-by-column and returns a new frame of <code>Diff</code> values.
 Each cell describes whether the value was added, removed, or changed between the two frames.
 Columns present only in this frame contribute <code>Diff.Remove</code> values; columns present only
 in <code>other</code> contribute <code>Diff.Add</code> values; columns present in both are compared
 element-wise.

### [FrameExtensions.CountColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#CountColumns)

FrameExtensions.CountColumns 
CountColumns 
 Returns the total number of row keys in the specified frame. This returns
 the total length of the row series, including keys for which there is no
 value available.

### [FrameExtensions.CountRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#CountRows)

FrameExtensions.CountRows 
CountRows 
 Returns the total number of row keys in the specified frame. This returns
 the total length of the row series, including keys for which there is no
 value available.

### [FrameExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Diff)

FrameExtensions.Diff 
Diff 

 Returns a frame with columns containing difference between an original value and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Frame.diff 1 s` returns a
 frame where previous column values is subtracted from the current ones. In pseudo-code, the
 function behaves as follows:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 Columns that cannot be converted to `float` are left without a change.

### [FrameExtensions.DistinctRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#DistinctRowsBy)

FrameExtensions.DistinctRowsBy 
DistinctRowsBy 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows that have distinct values in the
 specified columns. When multiple rows have the same values in those columns, only
 the first row (in index order) is preserved.

### [FrameExtensions.DropEmptyColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#DropEmptyColumns)

FrameExtensions.DropEmptyColumns 
DropEmptyColumns 

 Creates a new data frame that drops those columns that are empty for each row.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of rows, but may have
 fewer columns (or no columns at all).

### [FrameExtensions.DropEmptyRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#DropEmptyRows)

FrameExtensions.DropEmptyRows 
DropEmptyRows 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those rows that are empty for each column.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of columns, but may have
 fewer rows (or no rows at all).

### [FrameExtensions.DropSparseColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#DropSparseColumns)

FrameExtensions.DropSparseColumns 
DropSparseColumns 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those columns of the original
 data frame that are _dense_, meaning that they have a value for each row.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of rows, but may have
 fewer columns (or no columns at all).

### [FrameExtensions.DropSparseRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#DropSparseRows)

FrameExtensions.DropSparseRows 
DropSparseRows 

 Creates a new data frame that contains only those rows of the original
 data frame that are _dense_, meaning that they have a value for each column.
 The resulting data frame has the same number of columns, but may have
 fewer rows (or no rows at all).

### [FrameExtensions.ExpandColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#ExpandColumns)

FrameExtensions.ExpandColumns 
ExpandColumns 

 Creates a new data frame where the specified columns are expanded based on runtime
 structure of the objects they store. A column can be expanded if it is
 <code>Series<string, T></code> or <code>IDictionary<K, V></code> or if it is any .NET object with readable
 properties.
  
<example>
 Given a data frame with a series that contains tuples, you can expand the
 tuple members and get a frame with columns `S.Item1` and `S.Item2`:
 <code>
 let df = frame [ "S" => series [ 1 => (1, "One"); 2 => (2, "Two") ] ]
 df.ExpandColumns ["S"]
 </code></example>

### [FrameExtensions.ExpandColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#ExpandColumns)

FrameExtensions.ExpandColumns 
ExpandColumns 

 Creates a new data frame where all columns are expanded based on runtime
 structure of the objects they store. The expansion is performed recrusively
 to the specified depth. A column can be expanded if it is <code>Series<string, T></code>
 or <code>IDictionary<K, V></code> or if it is any .NET object with readable
 properties.

### [FrameExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#FillMissing)

FrameExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the frame using the specified function. The specified
 function is called with all series and keys for which the frame does not
 contain value and the result of the call is used in place of the missing value.

 The operation is only applied to columns (series) that contain values of the
 same type as the return type of the provided filling function. The operation
 does not attempt to convert between numeric values (so a series containing
 `float` will not be converted to a series of `int`).

### [FrameExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#FillMissing)

FrameExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the data frame with the nearest available value
 (using the specified direction). Note that the frame may still contain
 missing values after call to this function (e.g. if the first value is not available
 and we attempt to fill series with previous values). This operation can only be
 used on ordered frames.

### [FrameExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#FillMissing)

FrameExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values of a given type in the frame with a constant value.
 The operation is only applied to columns (series) that contain values of the
 same type as the provided filling value. The operation does not attempt to
 convert between numeric values (so a series containing `float` will not be
 converted to a series of `int`).

### [FrameExtensions.FilterRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#FilterRowsBy)

FrameExtensions.FilterRowsBy 
FilterRowsBy

### [FrameExtensions.GetRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetRows)

FrameExtensions.GetRows 
GetRows

### [FrameExtensions.GetRowsAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetRowsAt)

FrameExtensions.GetRowsAt 
GetRowsAt

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#GetSlice)

FrameExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [FrameExtensions.IndexColumnsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#IndexColumnsWith)

FrameExtensions.IndexColumnsWith 
IndexColumnsWith 

 Replace the column index of the frame with the provided sequence of column keys.
 The columns of the frame are assigned keys according to the current order, or in a
 non-deterministic way, if the current column index is not ordered.

### [FrameExtensions.IndexRowsApply](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#IndexRowsApply)

FrameExtensions.IndexRowsApply 
IndexRowsApply 

 Returns a data frame whose rows are indexed based on the specified column of the original
 data frame, with each index value transformed by the given function. The specified column
 is removed from the resulting frame.

### [FrameExtensions.IndexRowsOrdinally](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#IndexRowsOrdinally)

FrameExtensions.IndexRowsOrdinally 
IndexRowsOrdinally 

 Replace the row index of the frame with ordinarilly generated integers starting from zero.
 The rows of the frame are assigned index according to the current order, or in a
 non-deterministic way, if the current row index is not ordered.

### [FrameExtensions.IndexRowsUsing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#IndexRowsUsing)

FrameExtensions.IndexRowsUsing 
IndexRowsUsing 

 Replace the row index of the frame with a sequence of row keys generated using
 a function invoked on each row.

### [FrameExtensions.IndexRowsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#IndexRowsWith)

FrameExtensions.IndexRowsWith 
IndexRowsWith 

 Replace the row index of the frame with the provided sequence of row keys.
 The rows of the frame are assigned keys according to the current order, or in a
 non-deterministic way, if the current row index is not ordered.

### [FrameExtensions.Interleave](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Interleave)

FrameExtensions.Interleave 
Interleave 

 Combines a sequence of data frames side-by-side by tagging each frame's column keys with
 its 1-based position in the sequence. The result has a tuple column index of type
 <code>Tuple<'TColumnKey, int></code>, where the integer is the 1-based source frame index.
 Rows are aligned using an outer join.

### [FrameExtensions.JoinOn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#JoinOn)

FrameExtensions.JoinOn 
JoinOn 

 Join two data frames on a shared column, using that column's values as the row index for
 alignment. Both frames must have unique values in the specified column. The join column is
 removed from the data columns of the result and becomes the row index.

### [FrameExtensions.JoinOnInt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#JoinOnInt)

FrameExtensions.JoinOnInt 
JoinOnInt 

 Join two data frames on a shared int-valued column. Both frames must have unique values
 in the specified column. The join column becomes the row index of the result frame.

### [FrameExtensions.JoinOnString](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#JoinOnString)

FrameExtensions.JoinOnString 
JoinOnString 

 Join two data frames on a shared string-valued column. Both frames must have unique values
 in the specified column. The join column becomes the row index of the result frame.

### [FrameExtensions.MapCols](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#MapCols)

FrameExtensions.MapCols 
MapCols 

 Builds a new data frame whose columns are the results of applying the specified
 function to each column of the input data frame. The function receives the column
 key and an <code>ObjectSeries</code> representing the column data and must return an
 <code>ObjectSeries</code> of the same row index type.

### [FrameExtensions.MapRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#MapRows)

FrameExtensions.MapRows 
MapRows 

 Builds a new series whose values are the results of applying the specified
 function to each row of the data frame. The function receives the row key and
 an <code>ObjectSeries</code> representing the row data.

### [FrameExtensions.MeltBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#MeltBy)

FrameExtensions.MeltBy 
MeltBy 

 Unpivots a data frame from wide format to long format, similar to pandas
 <code>DataFrame.melt</code>. The specified identity columns are kept as-is; all other
 columns are collapsed into two new columns: <code>"Column"</code> holding the original
 column key and <code>"Value"</code> holding the cell value. Rows with missing values in
 the value columns are dropped.

### [FrameExtensions.Merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Merge)

FrameExtensions.Merge 
Merge

### [FrameExtensions.Nest](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Nest)

FrameExtensions.Nest 
Nest 

 Given a data frame whose row index has two levels, create a series
 whose keys are the unique first level keys, and whose values are
 those corresponding frames selected from the original data.

### [FrameExtensions.NestBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#NestBy)

FrameExtensions.NestBy 
NestBy 

 Given a data frame whose row index has two levels, create a series
 whose keys are the unique results of the keyselector projection, and
 whose values are those corresponding frames selected from the original
 data.

### [FrameExtensions.NestRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#NestRowsBy)

FrameExtensions.NestRowsBy 
NestRowsBy 

 Given a data frame, groups rows by <code>groupSelector</code> and produces a series whose
 keys are the distinct group keys and whose values are sub-frames re-indexed by
 <code>rowSelector</code>.  This is a generalisation of <code>NestBy</code> that lets you transform
 the inner row keys independently of the group keys.

### [FrameExtensions.OrderColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#OrderColumns)

FrameExtensions.OrderColumns 
OrderColumns

### [FrameExtensions.OrderRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#OrderRows)

FrameExtensions.OrderRows 
OrderRows

### [FrameExtensions.PctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#PctChange)

FrameExtensions.PctChange 
PctChange 

 Returns a frame where each value is the percentage change relative to the value at the
 specified offset. For example, calling <code>PctChange(1)</code> returns a frame where each
 value represents the relative change from the previous row's value. In pseudo-code:

     result[k] = (frame[k] - frame[k - offset]) / frame[k - offset]

 Columns that cannot be converted to <code>float</code> are left without a change.
 This is commonly used in financial analysis to compute returns (e.g. daily stock returns).

### [FrameExtensions.PivotTable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#PivotTable)

FrameExtensions.PivotTable 
PivotTable 

 Creates a new data frame resulting from a 'pivot' operation. Consider a denormalized data
 frame representing a table: column labels are field names & table values are observations
 of those fields. pivotTable buckets the rows along two axes, according to the values of
 the columns `r` and `c`; and then computes a value for the frame of rows that land in each
 bucket.

### [FrameExtensions.Print](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Print)

FrameExtensions.Print 
Print

### [FrameExtensions.Print](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Print)

FrameExtensions.Print 
Print

### [FrameExtensions.RankRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#RankRowsBy)

FrameExtensions.RankRowsBy 
RankRowsBy 

 Returns a series containing the dense rank of each row in the data frame, based on
 values in the specified column. The rank is 1-based: the row with the smallest
 column value receives rank 1. Rows with equal values receive the same rank.
 Rows where the column has a missing value receive a missing rank.

### [FrameExtensions.RealignRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#RealignRows)

FrameExtensions.RealignRows 
RealignRows 

 Align the existing data to a specified collection of row keys. Values in the data frame
 that do not match any new key are dropped, new keys (that were not in the original data
 frame) are assigned missing values.

### [FrameExtensions.Reduce](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Reduce)

FrameExtensions.Reduce 
Reduce

### [FrameExtensions.RenameColumn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#RenameColumn)

FrameExtensions.RenameColumn 
RenameColumn 

 Returns a new data frame where the specified column has been renamed to the new key.
 If <code>oldKey</code> is not found, the frame is returned unchanged.

### [FrameExtensions.RenameColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#RenameColumns)

FrameExtensions.RenameColumns 
RenameColumns 

 Returns a new data frame where all column keys have been transformed by the
 specified mapping function.

### [FrameExtensions.SaveCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveCsv)

FrameExtensions.SaveCsv 
SaveCsv

### [FrameExtensions.SaveCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveCsv)

FrameExtensions.SaveCsv 
SaveCsv 

 Save data frame to a CSV file or to a `Stream`. When calling the operation,
 you can specify whether you want to save the row keys or not (and headers for the keys)
 and you can also specify the separator (use `\t` for writing TSV files). When specifying
 file name ending with `.tsv`, the `\t` separator is used automatically.

### [FrameExtensions.SaveCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveCsv)

FrameExtensions.SaveCsv 
SaveCsv 

 Save data frame to a CSV file or to a `Stream`. When calling the operation,
 you can specify whether you want to save the row keys or not (and headers for the keys)
 and you can also specify the separator (use `\t` for writing TSV files). When specifying
 file name ending with `.tsv`, the `\t` separator is used automatically.

### [FrameExtensions.SaveCsv](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveCsv)

FrameExtensions.SaveCsv 
SaveCsv 

 Save data frame to a CSV file or to a `Stream`. When calling the operation,
 you can specify whether you want to save the row keys or not (and headers for the keys)
 and you can also specify the separator (use `\t` for writing TSV files). When specifying
 file name ending with `.tsv`, the `\t` separator is used automatically.

### [FrameExtensions.SaveJson](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveJson)

FrameExtensions.SaveJson 
SaveJson 

 Save the data frame as a JSON file at the specified path.

### [FrameExtensions.SaveJson](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SaveJson)

FrameExtensions.SaveJson 
SaveJson 

 Save the data frame as JSON to the specified <code>TextWriter</code>.

### [FrameExtensions.Select](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Select)

FrameExtensions.Select 
Select

### [FrameExtensions.Select](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Select)

FrameExtensions.Select 
Select

### [FrameExtensions.SelectColumnKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SelectColumnKeys)

FrameExtensions.SelectColumnKeys 
SelectColumnKeys

### [FrameExtensions.SelectRowKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SelectRowKeys)

FrameExtensions.SelectRowKeys 
SelectRowKeys

### [FrameExtensions.Shift](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Shift)

FrameExtensions.Shift 
Shift 

 Returns a frame with columns shifted by the specified offset. When the offset is
 positive, the values are shifted forward and first `offset` keys are dropped. When the
 offset is negative, the values are shifted backwards and the last `offset` keys are dropped.
 Expressed in pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k - offset]
  

 If you want to calculate the difference, e.g. `df - (Frame.shift 1 df)`, you can
 use `Frame.diff` which will be a little bit faster.

### [FrameExtensions.SortByColKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortByColKey)

FrameExtensions.SortByColKey 
SortByColKey

### [FrameExtensions.SortByRowKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortByRowKey)

FrameExtensions.SortByRowKey 
SortByRowKey

### [FrameExtensions.SortColumnsByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortColumnsByKey)

FrameExtensions.SortColumnsByKey 
SortColumnsByKey 

 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose columns are an ordered series. This allows using operations that are
 only available on indexed series such as alignment and inexact lookup.

### [FrameExtensions.SortRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortRows)

FrameExtensions.SortRows 
SortRows 

 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are sorted by some column.

### [FrameExtensions.SortRowsBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortRowsBy)

FrameExtensions.SortRowsBy 
SortRowsBy 

 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are sorted by some column.

### [FrameExtensions.SortRowsByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortRowsByKey)

FrameExtensions.SortRowsByKey 
SortRowsByKey 

 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are an ordered series. This allows using operations that are
 only available on indexed series such as alignment and inexact lookup.

### [FrameExtensions.SortRowsWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#SortRowsWith)

FrameExtensions.SortRowsWith 
SortRowsWith 

 Returns a data frame that contains the same data as the input,
 but whose rows are sorted by some column.

### [FrameExtensions.Stack](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Stack)

FrameExtensions.Stack 
Stack 

 Converts a data frame to a long-format frame by combining the row index and the
 column index into a 2-tuple row index. Each cell of the original frame becomes a row
 in the result keyed by <code>(rowKey, columnKey)</code>, with a single column named
 <code>"Value"</code>. Missing values are dropped.
  

 This is the pandas-style <code>DataFrame.stack()</code> operation. The inverse operation
 is <code>Unstack</code>.

### [FrameExtensions.ToDataTable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#ToDataTable)

FrameExtensions.ToDataTable 
ToDataTable 

 Returns the data of the frame as a .NET `DataTable` object. The column keys are
 automatically converted to strings that are used as column names. The row index is
 turned into an additional column with the specified name (the function takes the name
 as a sequence to support hierarchical keys, but typically you can write just
 `frame.ToDataTable(["KeyName"])`.

### [FrameExtensions.ToJson](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#ToJson)

FrameExtensions.ToJson 
ToJson 

 Serialize the data frame to a JSON string.

### [FrameExtensions.Transpose](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Transpose)

FrameExtensions.Transpose 
Transpose 

 Returns a transposed data frame. The rows of the original data frame are used as the
 columns of the new one (and vice versa). Use this operation if you have a data frame
 and you mostly need to access its rows as a series (because accessing columns as a
 series is more efficient).

### [FrameExtensions.Unnest](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Unnest)

FrameExtensions.Unnest 
Unnest 

 Given a series whose values are frames, create a frame resulting
 from the concatenation of all the frames' rows, with the resulting
 keys having two levels. This is the inverse operation to nest.

### [FrameExtensions.Unstack](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Unstack)

FrameExtensions.Unstack 
Unstack 

 Converts a data frame with a 2-tuple row index into a wide-format frame by promoting
 the inner (second) element of each row key to the column index. The resulting frame has
 row keys equal to the unique first tuple elements and column keys of the form
 <code>(originalColumnKey, innerRowKey)</code>. Cells with no corresponding entry in the
 input are represented as missing values.
  

 This is the pandas-style <code>DataFrame.unstack()</code> operation and is the inverse of
 <code>Stack</code> (modulo column-key wrapping when the input has more than one column).

### [FrameExtensions.Where](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Where)

FrameExtensions.Where 
Where 

 Filters frame rows using the specified condtion. Returns a new data frame
 that contains rows for which the provided function returned false. The function
 is called with `KeyValuePair` containing the row key as the `Key` and `Value`
 gives access to the row series and a row index.

### [FrameExtensions.Where](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Where)

FrameExtensions.Where 
Where 

 Filters frame rows using the specified condition. Returns a new data frame
 that contains rows for which the provided function returned false. The function
 is called with `KeyValuePair` containing the row key as the `Key` and `Value`
 gives access to the row series.

### [FrameExtensions.WhereColsByMask](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#WhereColsByMask)

FrameExtensions.WhereColsByMask 
WhereColsByMask 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the columns of the input frame whose
 corresponding value in the boolean mask series is <code>true</code>. Columns whose key
 is missing from the mask are excluded. This enables pandas-style boolean indexing.

### [FrameExtensions.WhereRowsByMask](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#WhereRowsByMask)

FrameExtensions.WhereRowsByMask 
WhereRowsByMask 

 Returns a new data frame containing only the rows of the input frame whose
 corresponding value in the boolean mask series is <code>true</code>. Rows whose key
 is missing from the mask are excluded. This enables pandas-style boolean indexing.

### [FrameExtensions.Window](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Window)

FrameExtensions.Window 
Window

### [FrameExtensions.Window](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameextensions.html#Window)

FrameExtensions.Window 
Window

### [FrameStatsExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html)

FrameStatsExtensions 

 The type implements C# and F# extension methods that add numerical operations
 to Deedle series. With a few exceptions, the methods are only available for
 series containing floating-point values, that is <code>Series<'K, float></code>.
  
FrameStatsExtensions.Describe 
Describe 
FrameStatsExtensions.Kurtosis 
Kurtosis 
FrameStatsExtensions.Max 
Max 
FrameStatsExtensions.Mean 
Mean 
FrameStatsExtensions.Median 
Median 
FrameStatsExtensions.Min 
Min 
FrameStatsExtensions.Skewness 
Skewness 
FrameStatsExtensions.StdDev 
StdDev 
FrameStatsExtensions.Sum 
Sum 
FrameStatsExtensions.UniqueCount 
UniqueCount 
FrameStatsExtensions.Variance 
Variance

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Describe](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Describe)

FrameStatsExtensions.Describe 
Describe 

 Returns a data frame with summary statistics (unique count, mean, standard
 deviation, min, lower quartile, median, upper quartile, max) for each
 numerical column of the input frame. Only columns convertible to
 <code>float</code> are included; the row keys of the result are the statistic
 names ("unique", "mean", "std", "min", "0.25", "0.5", "0.75", "max").

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Kurtosis](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Kurtosis)

FrameStatsExtensions.Kurtosis 
Kurtosis 

 For each numerical column, returns the kurtosis of the values in a series.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 4 values,
 the result is NaN.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Max](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Max)

FrameStatsExtensions.Max 
Max 

 For each numerical column, returns the maximal values as a series.
 The function skips over missing and `NaN` values. When there are no values,
 the result is `NaN`.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Mean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Mean)

FrameStatsExtensions.Mean 
Mean 

 For each numerical column, returns the mean of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are
 no available values, the result is NaN.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Median](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Median)

FrameStatsExtensions.Median 
Median 

 For each numerical column, returns the median of the values in the column.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Min](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Min)

FrameStatsExtensions.Min 
Min 

 For each numerical column, returns the minimal values as a series.
 The function skips over missing and `NaN` values. When there are no values,
 the result is `NaN`.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Skewness](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Skewness)

FrameStatsExtensions.Skewness 
Skewness 

 For each numerical column, returns the skewness of the values in a series.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 3 values,
 the result is NaN.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.StdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#StdDev)

FrameStatsExtensions.StdDev 
StdDev 

 For each numerical column, returns the standard deviation of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values,
 the result is NaN.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Sum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Sum)

FrameStatsExtensions.Sum 
Sum 

 For each numerical column, returns the sum of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are no
 available values, the result is 0.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.UniqueCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#UniqueCount)

FrameStatsExtensions.UniqueCount 
UniqueCount 

 For each column, returns the number of unique values.

### [FrameStatsExtensions.Variance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-framestatsextensions.html#Variance)

FrameStatsExtensions.Variance 
Variance 

 For each numerical column, returns the variance of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less
 than 2 values, the result is NaN.

### [FrameUtils](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html)

FrameUtils 

 Module with helper functions and operations that are needed by <code>Frame<R, C></code>, but
 are easier to write in a separate type (having them inside generic type
 can confuse the type inference in various ways).
 
FrameUtils.fromColumns 
fromColumns 
FrameUtils.fromRows 
fromRows 
FrameUtils.fromRowsAndColumnKeys 
fromRowsAndColumnKeys 
FrameUtils.indexBuilder 
indexBuilder 
FrameUtils.vectorBuilder 
vectorBuilder

### [FrameUtils.fromColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html#fromColumns)

FrameUtils.fromColumns 
fromColumns 
 Create data frame from a series of columns

### [FrameUtils.fromRows](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html#fromRows)

FrameUtils.fromRows 
fromRows 
 Create data frame from a series of rows

### [FrameUtils.fromRowsAndColumnKeys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html#fromRowsAndColumnKeys)

FrameUtils.fromRowsAndColumnKeys 
fromRowsAndColumnKeys 
 Create data frame from a series of rows

### [FrameUtils.indexBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html#indexBuilder)

FrameUtils.indexBuilder 
indexBuilder

### [FrameUtils.vectorBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-frameutils.html#vectorBuilder)

FrameUtils.vectorBuilder 
vectorBuilder

### [ICustomLookup<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-icustomlookup-1.html)

ICustomLookup<'K> 

 Represents a special lookup. This can be used to support hierarchical or duplicate keys
 in an index. A key type `K` can come with associated <code>ICustomLookup<K></code> to provide
 customized pattern matching (equality testing)
  
ICustomLookup<'K>.Matches 
Matches

### [ICustomLookup<'K>.Matches](/Deedle/reference/deedle-icustomlookup-1.html#Matches)

ICustomLookup<'K>.Matches 
Matches 
 Tests whether a specified key matches the current key (for example, in hierarchical indexing
 based on tuples, if the current key represents a pair (1, _) then the value (1, 42) would match).

### [IFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iframe.html)

IFrame 

 An empty interface that is implemented by <code>Frame<'R, 'C></code>. The purpose of the
 interface is to allow writing code that works on arbitrary data frames (you
 need to provide an implementation of the <code>IFrameOperation<'V></code> which contains
 a generic method `Invoke` that will be called with the typed data frame).
  
IFrame.Apply 
Apply

### [IFrame.Apply](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iframe.html#Apply)

IFrame.Apply 
Apply 
 Calls the `Invoke` method of the specified interface `IFrameOperation<'V>`
 with the typed data frame as an argument

### [IFrameOperation<'V>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iframeoperation-1.html)

IFrameOperation<'V> 

 Represents an operation that can be invoked on <code>Frame<'R, 'C></code>. The operation
 is generic in the type of row and column keys.
  
IFrameOperation<'V>.Invoke 
Invoke

### [IFrameOperation<'V>.Invoke](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iframeoperation-1.html#Invoke)

IFrameOperation<'V>.Invoke 
Invoke

### [IRangeRestriction<'TAddress>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-irangerestriction-1.html)

IRangeRestriction<'TAddress> 

 A sequence of indicies together with the total number. Use `RangeRestriction.ofSeq` to
 create one from a sequence. This can be implemented by concrete vector/index
 builders to allow further optimizations (e.g. when the underlying source directly
 supports range operations).

 For example, if your source has an optimised way for getting every 10th address, you
 can create your own `IRangeRestriction` and then check for it in `LookupRange` and
 use optimised implementation rather than actually iterating over the sequence of indices.
  
IRangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Count 
Count

### [IRangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Count](/Deedle/reference/deedle-irangerestriction-1.html#Count)

IRangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Count 
Count

### [ISeries<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iseries-1.html)

ISeries<'K> 

 Represents an untyped series with keys of type `K` and values of some unknown type
 (This type should not generally be used directly, but it can be used when you need
 to write code that works on a sequence of series of heterogeneous types).
  
ISeries<'K>.TryGetObject 
TryGetObject 
ISeries<'K>.Vector 
Vector 
ISeries<'K>.Index 
Index 
ISeries<'K>.VectorBuilder 
VectorBuilder

### [ISeries<'K>.TryGetObject](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iseries-1.html#TryGetObject)

ISeries<'K>.TryGetObject 
TryGetObject 
 Attempts to get the value at a specified key and return it as `obj`

### [ISeries<'K>.Vector](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iseries-1.html#Vector)

ISeries<'K>.Vector 
Vector 
 Returns the vector containing data of the series (as an untyped vector)

### [ISeries<'K>.Index](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iseries-1.html#Index)

ISeries<'K>.Index 
Index 
 Returns the index containing keys of the series

### [ISeries<'K>.VectorBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-iseries-1.html#VectorBuilder)

ISeries<'K>.VectorBuilder 
VectorBuilder 
 Returns the vector builder associated with this series

### [IVector](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html)

IVector 

 Represents an (untyped) vector that stores some values and provides access
 to the values via a generic address. This type should be only used directly when
 extending the DataFrame library and adding a new way of storing or loading data.
 To allow invocation via Reflection, the vector exposes type of elements as <code>System.Type</code>.
  
IVector.GetObject 
GetObject 
IVector.Invoke 
Invoke 
IVector.SuppressPrinting 
SuppressPrinting 
IVector.AddressingScheme 
AddressingScheme 
IVector.ElementType 
ElementType 
IVector.ObjectSequence 
ObjectSequence 
IVector.Length 
Length

### [IVector.GetObject](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#GetObject)

IVector.GetObject 
GetObject 
 Return value stored in the vector at a specified address. This is simply an
 untyped version of `GetValue` method on a typed vector.

### [IVector.Invoke](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#Invoke)

IVector.Invoke 
Invoke 
 Invokes the specified generic function (vector call site) with the current
 instance of vector passed as a statically typed vector (ie. IVector<ElementType>)

### [IVector.SuppressPrinting](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#SuppressPrinting)

IVector.SuppressPrinting 
SuppressPrinting 
 When `true`, the formatter in F# Interactive will not attempt to evaluate the
 vector to print it. This is useful when the vector contains lazily loaded data.

### [IVector.AddressingScheme](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#AddressingScheme)

IVector.AddressingScheme 
AddressingScheme 
 Returns the addressing scheme of the index. When creating a series or a frame
 this is compared for equality with the addressing scheme of the vector(s).

### [IVector.ElementType](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#ElementType)

IVector.ElementType 
ElementType 
 Returns the type of elements stored in the current vector as `System.Type`.
 This member is mainly used for internal purposes (to invoke a generic function
 represented by `VectorCallSite1<R>` with the typed version of the current
 vector as an argument.

### [IVector.ObjectSequence](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#ObjectSequence)

IVector.ObjectSequence 
ObjectSequence 
 Returns all values of the vector as a sequence of optional objects

### [IVector.Length](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector.html#Length)

IVector.Length 
Length 
 Returns the number of elements in the vector

### [IVector<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html)

IVector<'T> 

 A generic, typed vector. Represents mapping from addresses to values of type <code>'T</code>.
 The vector provides a minimal interface that is required by series and can be
 implemented in a number of ways to provide vector backed by database or an
 alternative representation of data.
  
IVector<'T>.Convert 
Convert 
IVector<'T>.GetValue 
GetValue 
IVector<'T>.GetValueAtLocation 
GetValueAtLocation 
IVector<'T>.Select 
Select 
IVector<'T>.Data 
Data

### [IVector<'T>.Convert](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html#Convert)

IVector<'T>.Convert 
Convert 
 Create a vector whose values are converted using the specified function, but
 can be converted back using another specified function. For virtualized vectors,
 this enables e.g. efficient lookup on the returned vectors (by delegating the
 lookup to the original source)

### [IVector<'T>.GetValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html#GetValue)

IVector<'T>.GetValue 
GetValue 
 Returns value stored in the vector at a specified address.

### [IVector<'T>.GetValueAtLocation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html#GetValueAtLocation)

IVector<'T>.GetValueAtLocation 
GetValueAtLocation 
 Returns value stored in the vector at a specified location.
 This can typically just call 'GetValue(loc.Address)', but it can do something
 more clever using the fact that the caller provided us with the address & offset.

### [IVector<'T>.Select](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html#Select)

IVector<'T>.Select 
Select 
 Apply the specified function to all values stored in the vector and return
 a new vector (not necessarily of the same representation) with the results.
 The function handles missing values - it is called with optional values and
 may return a missing value as a result of the transformation.

### [IVector<'T>.Data](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivector-1.html#Data)

IVector<'T>.Data 
Data 
 Returns all data of the vector in one of the supported formats. Depending
 on the vector, data may be returned as a continuous block of memory using
 `ReadOnlyCollection<T>` or as a lazy sequence `seq<T>`.

### [IVectorLocation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivectorlocation.html)

IVectorLocation 

 Represents a location in a vector. In general, we always know the address, but
 sometimes (BigDeedle) it is hard to get the offset (requires some data lookups),
 so we use this interface to delay the calculation of the <code>Offset</code> (which is mainly
 needed in one of the <code>series.Select</code> overloads).
  
IVectorLocation.Address 
Address 
IVectorLocation.Offset 
Offset

### [IVectorLocation.Address](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivectorlocation.html#Address)

IVectorLocation.Address 
Address 
 Returns the address of the location (this should be immediate)

### [IVectorLocation.Offset](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ivectorlocation.html#Offset)

IVectorLocation.Offset 
Offset 
 Returns the offset of the location (this may involve some calculation)

### [Index](/Deedle/reference/deedle-index.html)

Index 

 Type that provides access to creating indices (represented as `LinearIndex` values)
  
Index.Create 
Create 
Index.CreateUnordered 
CreateUnordered

### [Index.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-index.html#Create)

Index.Create 
Create 
 Create an index from a sequence of keys and check if they are sorted or not

### [Index.CreateUnordered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-index.html#CreateUnordered)

Index.CreateUnordered 
CreateUnordered 
 Create an index from a sequence of keys and assume they are not sorted
 (the resulting index is also not sorted).

### [JoinKind](/Deedle/reference/deedle-joinkind.html)

JoinKind 

 This enumeration specifies joining behavior for `Join` method provided
 by `Series` and `Frame`. Outer join unions the keys (and may introduce
 missing values), inner join takes the intersection of keys; left and
 right joins take the keys of the first or the second series/frame.
  
JoinKind.Outer 
Outer 
JoinKind.Inner 
Inner 
JoinKind.Left 
Left 
JoinKind.Right 
Right

### [JoinKind.Outer](/Deedle/reference/deedle-joinkind.html#Outer)

JoinKind.Outer 
Outer 
 Combine the keys available in both structures, align the values that
 are available in both of them and mark the remaining values as missing.

### [JoinKind.Inner](/Deedle/reference/deedle-joinkind.html#Inner)

JoinKind.Inner 
Inner 
 Take the intersection of the keys available in both structures and align the
 values of the two structures. The resulting structure cannot contain missing values.

### [JoinKind.Left](/Deedle/reference/deedle-joinkind.html#Left)

JoinKind.Left 
Left 
 Take the keys of the left (first) structure and align values from the right (second)
 structure with the keys of the first one. Values for keys not available in the second
 structure will be missing.

### [JoinKind.Right](/Deedle/reference/deedle-joinkind.html#Right)

JoinKind.Right 
Right 
 Take the keys of the right (second) structure and align values from the left (first)
 structure with the keys of the second one. Values for keys not available in the first
 structure will be missing.

### [KeyValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keyvalue.html)

KeyValue 

 A type with extension method for <code>KeyValuePair<'K, 'V></code> that makes
 it possible to create values using just `KeyValue.Create`.
  
KeyValue.Create 
Create

### [KeyValue.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keyvalue.html#Create)

KeyValue.Create 
Create

### [Lookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html)

Lookup 

 Represents different behaviors of key lookup in series. For unordered series,
 the only available option is `Lookup.Exact` which finds the exact key - methods
 fail or return missing value if the key is not available in the index. For ordered
 series `Lookup.Greater` finds the first greater key (e.g. later date) with
 a value. `Lookup.Smaller` searches for the first smaller key. The options
 `Lookup.ExactOrGreater` and `Lookup.ExactOrSmaller` finds the exact key (if it is
 present) and otherwise search for the nearest larger or smaller key, respectively.
  
Lookup.Exact 
Exact 
Lookup.ExactOrGreater 
ExactOrGreater 
Lookup.ExactOrSmaller 
ExactOrSmaller 
Lookup.Greater 
Greater 
Lookup.Smaller 
Smaller

### [Lookup.Exact](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html#Exact)

Lookup.Exact 
Exact 
 Lookup a value associated with the exact specified key.
 If the key is not available, then fail or return missing value.

### [Lookup.ExactOrGreater](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html#ExactOrGreater)

Lookup.ExactOrGreater 
ExactOrGreater 
 Lookup a value associated with the specified key or with the nearest
 greater key that has a value available. Fails (or returns missing value)
 only when the specified key is greater than all available keys.

### [Lookup.ExactOrSmaller](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html#ExactOrSmaller)

Lookup.ExactOrSmaller 
ExactOrSmaller 
 Lookup a value associated with the specified key or with the nearest
 smaller key that has a value available. Fails (or returns missing value)
 only when the specified key is smaller than all available keys.

### [Lookup.Greater](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html#Greater)

Lookup.Greater 
Greater 
 Lookup a value associated with a key that is greater than the specified one.
 Fails (or returns missing value) when the specified key is greater or equal
 to the greatest available key.

### [Lookup.Smaller](/Deedle/reference/deedle-lookup.html#Smaller)

Lookup.Smaller 
Smaller 
 Lookup a value associated with a key that is smaller than the specified one.
 Fails (or returns missing value) when the specified key is smaller or equal
 to the smallest available key.

### [MissingValueException](/Deedle/reference/deedle-missingvalueexception.html)

MissingValueException 

 Thrown when a value at the specified index does not exist in the data frame or series.
 This exception is thrown only when the key is defined, but the value is not available,
 in other situations `KeyNotFoundException` is thrown
  
MissingValueException.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
MissingValueException.Key 
Key

### [MissingValueException.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-missingvalueexception.html#``.ctor``)

MissingValueException.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [MissingValueException.Key](/Deedle/reference/deedle-missingvalueexception.html#Key)

MissingValueException.Key 
Key 
 The key that has been accessed

### [ObjectSeries<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-objectseries-1.html)

ObjectSeries<'K> 

 Represents a series containing boxed values. This type is inherited from <code>Series<'K, obj></code>
 and it adds additional operations for accessing values with unboxing. This includes operations
 such as <code>os.GetAs<'T></code>, <code>os.TryGetAs<'T></code> and <code>os.TryAs<'T></code> which (attempt to) convert
 values to the specified type `'T`.
  
ObjectSeries<'K>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

GetAtAs

ObjectSeries<'K>.GetValues 
GetValues 
ObjectSeries<'K>.GetValues 
GetValues 
ObjectSeries<'K>.TryAs 
TryAs 
ObjectSeries<'K>.TryAs

### [OptionalValue<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html)

OptionalValue<'T> 

 Value type that represents a potentially missing value. This is similar to
 <code>System.Nullable<T></code>, but does not restrict the contained value to be a value
 type, so it can be used for storing values of any types. When obtained from
 <code>DataFrame<R, C></code> or <code>Series<K, T></code>, the <code>Value</code> will never be <code>Double.NaN</code> or <code>null</code>
 (but this is not, in general, checked when constructing the value).

 The type is only used in C#-friendly API. F# operations generally use expose
 standard F# <code>option<T></code> type instead. However, there the <code>OptionalValue</code> module
 contains helper functions for using this type from F# as well as <code>Missing</code> and
 <code>Present</code> active patterns.
  
OptionalValue<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
OptionalValue<'T>.HasValue 
HasValue 
OptionalValue<'T>.Value 
Value 
OptionalValue<'T>.ValueOrDefault 
ValueOrDefault 
OptionalValue<'T>.Missing 
Missing

### [OptionalValue<'T>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html#``.ctor``)

OptionalValue<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
 Creates a new instance of `OptionalValue<T>` that contains
 the specified `T` value .

### [OptionalValue<'T>.HasValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html#HasValue)

OptionalValue<'T>.HasValue 
HasValue 
 Gets a value indicating whether the current `OptionalValue<T>` has a value

### [OptionalValue<'T>.Value](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html#Value)

OptionalValue<'T>.Value 
Value 
<pre> Returns the value stored in the current `OptionalValue<T>`.
 Exceptions:
   `InvalidOperationException` - Thrown when `HasValue` is `false`.</pre>

### [OptionalValue<'T>.ValueOrDefault](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html#ValueOrDefault)

OptionalValue<'T>.ValueOrDefault 
ValueOrDefault 
 Returns the value stored in the current `OptionalValue<T>` or
 the default value of the type `T` when a value is not present.

### [OptionalValue<'T>.Missing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue-1.html#Missing)

OptionalValue<'T>.Missing 
Missing 
 Returns a new instance of `OptionalValue<T>` that does not contain a value.

### [OptionalValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue.html)

OptionalValue 

 Non-generic type that makes it easier to create <code>OptionalValue<T></code> values
 from C# by benefiting the type inference for generic method invocations.
  
OptionalValue.Create 
Create 
OptionalValue.Empty 
Empty 
OptionalValue.OfNullable 
OfNullable

### [OptionalValue.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue.html#Create)

OptionalValue.Create 
Create 
 Creates an <c>OptionalValue<'T></c> that contains a value <c>v</c>

### [OptionalValue.Empty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue.html#Empty)

OptionalValue.Empty 
Empty 
 Creates an `OptionalValue<'T>` that does not contain a value

### [OptionalValue.OfNullable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalue.html#OfNullable)

OptionalValue.OfNullable 
OfNullable 
 Creates an <c>OptionalValue<T></c> from a nullable value of type <c>T?</c>

### [OptionalValueExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalueextensions.html)

OptionalValueExtensions 

 Extension methods for working with optional values from C#. These make
 it easier to provide default values and convert optional values to
 `Nullable` (when the contained value is value type)
  
OptionalValueExtensions.AsNullable 
AsNullable 
OptionalValueExtensions.OrDefault 
OrDefault

### [OptionalValueExtensions.AsNullable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalueextensions.html#AsNullable)

OptionalValueExtensions.AsNullable 
AsNullable 
 Extension method that converts optional value containing a value type
 to a C# friendly `Nullable<T>` or `T?` type.

### [OptionalValueExtensions.OrDefault](/Deedle/reference/deedle-optionalvalueextensions.html#OrDefault)

OptionalValueExtensions.OrDefault 
OrDefault 
 Extension method that returns value in the specified optional value
 or the provided default value (the second argument).

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress> 

 Specifies a sub-range within index that can be accessed via slicing
 (see the <code>GetAddressRange</code> method). For in-memory data structures, accessing
 range via known addresses is typically sufficient, but for virtual Big Deedle
 sources, <code>Start</code> and <code>End</code> let us avoid fully evaluating addresses.
 <code>Custom</code> range can be used for optimizations.
  
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Select 
Select 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsEnd 
IsEnd 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsStart 
IsStart 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsFixed 
IsFixed 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsCustom 
IsCustom 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Fixed 
Fixed 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Start 
Start 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.End 
End 
RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Custom 
Custom

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Select](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#Select)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Select 
Select

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#IsEnd)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsEnd 
IsEnd

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsStart](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#IsStart)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsStart 
IsStart

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsFixed](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#IsFixed)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsFixed 
IsFixed

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsCustom](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#IsCustom)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.IsCustom 
IsCustom

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Fixed](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#Fixed)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Fixed 
Fixed 
 Range specified as a pair of (inclusive) lower and upper addresses

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Start](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#Start)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Start 
Start 
 Range referring to the specified number of elements from the start

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.End](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#End)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.End 
End 
 Range referring to the specified number of elements from the end

### [RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Custom](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rangerestriction-1.html#Custom)

RangeRestriction<'TAddress>.Custom 
Custom 
 Custom range, which is a sequence of indices, or other representation of it

### [RowSeries<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-rowseries-2.html)

RowSeries<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey> 

 Represents a series of rows from a frame. The type inherits from a series of
 series representing individual rows (<code>Series<'TRowKey, ObjectSeries<'TColumnKey>></code>) but
 hides slicing operations with new versions that return frames.

RowSeries<'TRowKey, 'TColumnKey>.FromSeries

 (and implements slicing in terms of the specified series)

### [Series<'K, 'V>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html)

Series<'K, 'V> 

 The type <code>Series<K, V></code> represents a data series consisting of values `V` indexed by
 keys `K`. The keys of a series may or may not be ordered
  
Series<'K, 'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Series<'K, 'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

After

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Aggregate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Aggregate)

Series<'K, 'V>.Aggregate 
Aggregate 

 Aggregates an ordered series using the method specified by <code>Aggregation<K></code> and then
 applies the provided `observationSelector` on each window or chunk to produce the result
 which is returned as a new series. The selector returns both the key and the value.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Aggregate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Aggregate)

Series<'K, 'V>.Aggregate 
Aggregate 

 Aggregates an ordered series using the method specified by <code>Aggregation<K></code> and then
 applies the provided `valueSelector` on each window or chunk to produce the result
 which is returned as a new series. A key for each window or chunk is
 selected using the specified `keySelector`.
