### [Series<'K, 'V>.Format](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Format)

Series<'K, 'V>.Format 
Format 

 Shows the series content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows a limited number of values from the series.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Format](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Format)

Series<'K, 'V>.Format 
Format

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Format](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Format)

Series<'K, 'V>.Format 
Format 
 Shows the series content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows a limited number of values from the series.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.FormatStrings](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#FormatStrings)

Series<'K, 'V>.FormatStrings 
FormatStrings 

 Shows the series content in a human-readable format. The resulting string
 shows a limited number of values from the series.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Get](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Get)

Series<'K, 'V>.Get 
Get 

 Gets the value at the specified key. Fails if not found.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Get](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Get)

Series<'K, 'V>.Get 
Get 

 Gets the value at the specified key using the specified lookup semantics. Fails if not found.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetAddressRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetAddressRange)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetAddressRange 
GetAddressRange 
 Internal helper used by `skip`, `take`, etc.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetAt)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetAt 
GetAt 

 Gets the value at the specified index position.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetByLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetByLevel)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetByLevel 
GetByLevel 

 Performs a hierarchical lookup by applying a custom lookup on the series index.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetItems](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetItems)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetItems 
GetItems 

 Returns a new series with an index containing the specified keys.
 When the key is not found in the current series, the newly returned
 series will contain a missing value. When the second parameter is not
 specified, the keys have to exactly match the keys in the current series
 (`Lookup.Exact`).

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetItems](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetItems)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetItems 
GetItems 

 Returns a new series with an index containing the specified keys.
 When the key is not found in the current series, the newly returned
 series will contain a missing value. When the second parameter is not
 specified, the keys have to exactly match the keys in the current series
 (`Lookup.Exact`).

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetKeyAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetKeyAt)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetKeyAt 
GetKeyAt 

 Gets the key at the specified index position.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetObservation)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetObservation 
GetObservation 

 Gets the value and key at the specified key. Fails if not found.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GetObservation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GetObservation)

Series<'K, 'V>.GetObservation 
GetObservation 

 Gets the value and key at the specified lookup semantics. Fails if not found.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.GroupBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#GroupBy)

Series<'K, 'V>.GroupBy 
GroupBy 

 Groups a series (ordered or unordered) using the specified key selector (`keySelector`)

### [Series<'K, 'V>.IndexOrdinally](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#IndexOrdinally)

Series<'K, 'V>.IndexOrdinally 
IndexOrdinally 

 Replace the index of the series with ordinally generated integers starting from zero.
 The elements of the series are assigned index according to the current order, or in a
 non-deterministic way, if the current index is not ordered.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.IndexWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#IndexWith)

Series<'K, 'V>.IndexWith 
IndexWith

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Interpolate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Interpolate)

Series<'K, 'V>.Interpolate 
Interpolate 

 Interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys. The function iterates through
 each new key, and invokes a function on the current key, the nearest smaller and larger valid
 observations from the series argument. The function must return a new valid float.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Intersect](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Intersect)

Series<'K, 'V>.Merge

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Pairwise](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Pairwise)

Series<'K, 'V>.Pairwise 
Pairwise 

 Returns a series containing the predecessor and an element for each input, except
 for the first one. The returned series is one key shorter (it does not contain a
 value for the first key).

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Pairwise](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Pairwise)

Series<'K, 'V>.Pairwise 
Pairwise 

 Returns a series containing an element and its neighbor for each input.
 The returned series is one key shorter (it does not contain a
 value for the first or last key depending on `boundary`). If `boundary` is
 other than `Boundary.Skip`, then the key is included in the returned series,
 but its value is missing.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Realign](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Realign)

Series<'K, 'V>.Realign 
Realign

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Replace](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Replace)

Series<'K, 'V>.Replace 
Replace 

 Replace series values given in keys with value.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Replace](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Replace)

Series<'K, 'V>.Replace 
Replace 

 Replace series values given in keys with value.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Resample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Resample)

Series<'K, 'V>.Resample 
Resample 

 Resample the series based on a provided collection of keys. The values of the series
 are aggregated into chunks based on the specified keys. Depending on `direction`, the
 specified key is either used as the smallest or as the greatest key of the chunk (with
 the exception of boundaries that are added to the first/last chunk). The chunks
 are then returned as a nested series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Resample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Resample)

Series<'K, 'V>.Resample 
Resample 

 Resample the series based on a provided collection of keys. The values of the series
 are aggregated into chunks based on the specified keys. Depending on `direction`, the
 specified key is either used as the smallest or as the greatest key of the chunk (with
 the exception of boundaries that are added to the first/last chunk).

 Such chunks are then aggregated using the provided `valueSelector` and `keySelector`
 (an overload that does not take `keySelector` just selects the explicitly provided key).
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Resample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Resample)

Series<'K, 'V>.Resample 
Resample 

 Resample the series based on a provided collection of keys. The values of the series
 are aggregated into chunks based on the specified keys. Depending on `direction`, the
 specified key is either used as the smallest or as the greatest key of the chunk (with
 the exception of boundaries that are added to the first/last chunk).

 Such chunks are then aggregated using the provided `valueSelector` and `keySelector`
 (an overload that does not take `keySelector` just selects the explicitly provided key).
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

Series<'K, 'V>.TryGet 
TryGet 

 Attempts to get the value at the specified key using the specified lookup semantics.

Series<'K, 'V>.TryGetAt

Series<'K, 'V>.TryGetObservation

Series<'K, 'V>.TryGetObservation 
TryGetObservation 

 Attempts to get the value and key at the specified lookup semantics.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.WithMissingFrom](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#WithMissingFrom)

Series<'K, 'V>.WithMissingFrom 
WithMissingFrom 

 Returns the current series with the same index but with values missing wherever the
 corresponding key exists in the other series index with an associated missing value.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.IsOrdered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#IsOrdered)

Series<'K, 'V>.IsOrdered 
IsOrdered 

 Gets a value indicating whether the series index is ordered.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Item](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Item)

Series<'K, 'V>.Item 
Item 

 Gets values by hierarchical level lookup.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Item](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Item)

Series<'K, 'V>.Item 
Item 

 Gets a series containing values at the specified keys.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Item](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Item)

Series<'K, 'V>.Item 
Item 

 Gets the value at the specified key.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Keys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Keys)

Series<'K, 'V>.Keys 
Keys 

 Returns a collection of keys that are defined by the index of this series.
 Note that the length of this sequence does not match the `Values` sequence
 if there are missing values. To get matching sequence, use the `Observations`
 property or `Series.observation`.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.ValuesAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#ValuesAll)

Series<'K, 'V>.ValuesAll 
ValuesAll 

 Returns a collection of values, including possibly missing values. Note that
 the length of this sequence matches the `Keys` sequence.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Reversed](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Reversed)

Series<'K, 'V>.Reversed 
Reversed

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Vector](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Vector)

Series<'K, 'V>.Vector 
Vector 

 Returns the vector associated with this series. This member should not generally
 be accessed directly, because all functionality is exposed through series operations.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.IsEmpty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#IsEmpty)

Series<'K, 'V>.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 

 Gets a value indicating whether the series is empty.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.ValueCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#ValueCount)

Series<'K, 'V>.ValueCount 
ValueCount 

 Returns the total number of values in the specified series. This excludes
 missing values or not available values (such as values created from `null`,
 `Double.NaN`, or those that are missing due to outer join etc.).

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Values](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Values)

Series<'K, 'V>.Values 
Values 

 Returns a collection of values that are available in the series data.
 Note that the length of this sequence does not match the `Keys` sequence
 if there are missing values. To get matching sequence, use the `Observations`
 property or `Series.observation`.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Index](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Index)

Series<'K, 'V>.Index 
Index 

 Returns the index associated with this series. This member should not generally
 be accessed directly, because all functionality is exposed through series operations.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.Observations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#Observations)

Series<'K, 'V>.Observations 
Observations 

 Returns a collection of observations that form this series. Note that this property
 skips over all missing (or NaN) values. Observations are returned as <code>KeyValuePair<K, V></code>
 objects. For an F# alternative that uses tuples, see `Series.observations`.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.KeyRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#KeyRange)

Series<'K, 'V>.KeyRange 
KeyRange 

 Gets the range of keys in the series.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.ObservationsAll](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#ObservationsAll)

Series<'K, 'V>.ObservationsAll 
ObservationsAll 

 Returns a collection of observations that form this series. Note that this property
 includes all missing (or NaN) values. Observations are returned as
 <code>KeyValuePair<K, OptionalValue<V>></code> objects. For an F# alternative that uses tuples,
 see `Series.observationsAll`.

### [Series<'K, 'V>.KeyCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#KeyCount)

Series<'K, 'V>.KeyCount 
KeyCount 

 Returns the total number of keys in the specified series. This returns
 the total length of the series, including keys for which there is no
 value available.

Series<'K, 'V>.(/) 
(/)

### [Series<'K, 'V>.($)](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#($))

Series<'K, 'V>.($) 
($) 
<pre> Custom operator that can be used for applying a function to all elements of
 a series. This provides a nicer syntactic sugar for the `Series.mapValues`
 function. For example:

     // Given a float series and a function on floats
     let s1 = Series.ofValues [ 1.0 .. 10.0 ]
     let adjust v = max 10.0 v

     // Apply "adjust (v + v)" to all elements
     adjust $ (s1 + s1)</pre>

### [Series<'K, 'V>.(?)](/Deedle/reference/deedle-series-2.html#(?))

Series<'K, 'V>.(?) 
(?) 

 Dynamic operator for accessing series values by key using the `?` syntax.

### [SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-2.html)

SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V> 

 The type can be used for creating series using mutation. You can add
 items using `Add` and get the resulting series using the <code>Series</code> property.
  

 Using from C#:

 The type supports the C# collection builder pattern:

    	var s = new <code>SeriesBuilder<string, double></code>
       { { "A", 1.0 }, { "B", 2.0 }, { "C", 3.0 } }.Series;

 The type also supports the `dynamic` operator:

     dynamic sb = new <code>SeriesBuilder<string, obj></code>();
     sb.ID = 1;
     sb.Value = 3.4;
  
SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Add 
Add 
SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Series 
Series 
SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.(?<-) 
(?<-)

### [SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-2.html#``.ctor``)

SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Add](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-2.html#Add)

SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Add 
Add 
 Add specified key and value to the series being build

### [SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Series](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-2.html#Series)

SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.Series 
Series 
 Returns the constructed series. The series is an immutable
 copy of the current values and so further additions will not
 change the returned series.

### [SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.(?<-)](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-2.html#(?<-))

SeriesBuilder<'K, 'V>.(?<-) 
(?<-)

### [SeriesBuilder<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-1.html)

SeriesBuilder<'K> 

 A simple class that inherits from <code>SeriesBuilder<'K, obj></code> and can be
 used instead of writing <code>SeriesBuilder<'K, obj></code> with two type arguments.
  
SeriesBuilder<'K>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [SeriesBuilder<'K>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesbuilder-1.html#``.ctor``)

SeriesBuilder<'K>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [SeriesExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html)

SeriesExtensions 

 The type implements C# and F# extension methods for the <code>Series<'K, 'V></code> type.
 The members are automatically available when you import the `Deedle` namespace.
 The type contains object-oriented counterparts to most of the functionality
 from the `Series` module.
  
SeriesExtensions.Chunk

### [SeriesExtensions.Chunk](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Chunk)

SeriesExtensions.Chunk 
Chunk

### [SeriesExtensions.ChunkInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ChunkInto)

SeriesExtensions.ChunkInto 
ChunkInto

### [SeriesExtensions.ChunkInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ChunkInto)

SeriesExtensions.ChunkInto 
ChunkInto

### [SeriesExtensions.ChunkWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ChunkWhile)

SeriesExtensions.ChunkWhile 
ChunkWhile 

 Splits a series into non-overlapping chunks. A new chunk is started when the condition
 on consecutive keys is no longer met. Returns a series of nested series, one per chunk.

### [SeriesExtensions.ChunkWhileInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ChunkWhileInto)

SeriesExtensions.ChunkWhileInto 
ChunkWhileInto 

 Splits a series into non-overlapping chunks. A new chunk is started when the condition
 on consecutive keys is no longer met. Each chunk is then aggregated into a single value
 using the provided <code>reduce</code> function.

### [SeriesExtensions.ContainsKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ContainsKey)

SeriesExtensions.ContainsKey 
ContainsKey

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 
<pre> Returns a series containing the difference (as <see cref="System.TimeSpan"/>) between
 a <see cref="System.DateTimeOffset"/> value in the original series and a value at the
 specified offset. For example, calling <c>Diff(1)</c> returns a series where the
 previous timestamp is subtracted from the current one:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 ## Parameters
  - `offset` - When positive, subtracts the past values from the current values;
    when negative, subtracts the future values from the current values.
  - `series` - The input series.</pre>

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 
<pre> Returns a series containing the difference (as <see cref="System.TimeSpan"/>) between
 a <see cref="System.DateTime"/> value in the original series and a value at the
 specified offset. For example, calling <c>Diff(1)</c> returns a series where the
 previous timestamp is subtracted from the current one:

     result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]

 ## Parameters
  - `offset` - When positive, subtracts the past values from the current values;
    when negative, subtracts the future values from the current values.
  - `series` - The input series.</pre>

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 

 Returns a series containing difference between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Series.diff 1 s` returns a
 series where previous value is subtracted from the current one.

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 

 Returns a series containing difference between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Series.diff 1 s` returns a
 series where previous value is subtracted from the current one.

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 

 Returns a series containing difference between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Series.diff 1 s` returns a
 series where previous value is subtracted from the current one.

### [SeriesExtensions.Diff](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Diff)

SeriesExtensions.Diff 
Diff 

 Returns a series containing difference between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling `Series.diff 1 s` returns a
 series where previous value is subtracted from the current one.

### [SeriesExtensions.DropMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#DropMissing)

SeriesExtensions.DropMissing 
DropMissing 

 Drop missing values from the specified series. The returned series contains
 only those keys for which there is a value available in the original one.

### [SeriesExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FillMissing)

SeriesExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the series using the specified function.
 The specified function is called with all keys for which the series
 does not contain value and the result of the call is used in place
 of the missing value.
  

 This function can be used to implement more complex interpolation.
 For example see [handling missing values in the tutorial](../frame.html#missing)

### [SeriesExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FillMissing)

SeriesExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing

### [SeriesExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FillMissing)

SeriesExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the series with the nearest available value
 (using the specified direction). The default direction is `Direction.Backward`.
 Note that the series may still contain missing values after call to this
 function. This operation can only be used on ordered series.

### [SeriesExtensions.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FillMissing)

SeriesExtensions.FillMissing 
FillMissing 

 Fill missing values in the series with a constant value.

### [SeriesExtensions.FirstKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FirstKey)

SeriesExtensions.FirstKey 
FirstKey

### [SeriesExtensions.FirstValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#FirstValue)

SeriesExtensions.FirstValue 
FirstValue

### [SeriesExtensions.Flatten](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Flatten)

SeriesExtensions.Flatten 
Flatten 
 Collapses a series of OptionalValue<'T> values to just 'T values

### [SeriesExtensions.GetAllObservations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetAllObservations)

SeriesExtensions.GetAllObservations 
GetAllObservations 
 Returns all keys from the sequence, together with the associated (optional)
 values. The values are returned using the `OptionalValue<T>` struct which
 provides `HasValue` for testing if the value is available.

### [SeriesExtensions.GetAllValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetAllValues)

SeriesExtensions.GetAllValues 
GetAllValues 
 Returns all (optional) values. The values are returned using the
 `OptionalValue<T>` struct which provides `HasValue` for testing
 if the value is available.

### [SeriesExtensions.GetObservations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetObservations)

SeriesExtensions.GetObservations 
GetObservations 
 Return observations with available values. The operation skips over
 all keys with missing values (such as values created from `null`,
 `Double.NaN`, or those that are missing due to outer join etc.).

### [SeriesExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetSlice)

SeriesExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [SeriesExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetSlice)

SeriesExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [SeriesExtensions.GetSlice](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#GetSlice)

SeriesExtensions.GetSlice 
GetSlice

### [SeriesExtensions.LastKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#LastKey)

SeriesExtensions.LastKey 
LastKey

### [SeriesExtensions.LastValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#LastValue)

SeriesExtensions.LastValue 
LastValue

### [SeriesExtensions.Log](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Log)

SeriesExtensions.Log 
Log

### [SeriesExtensions.Ntile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Ntile)

SeriesExtensions.Ntile 
Ntile 

 Divides the series values into the specified number of equal-sized buckets numbered
 0 to <span class="fsdocs-param-name">groups</span> - 1. Bucket 0 contains the smallest values.

### [SeriesExtensions.OrderByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#OrderByKey)

SeriesExtensions.OrderByKey 
OrderByKey

### [SeriesExtensions.PairwiseWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#PairwiseWith)

SeriesExtensions.PairwiseWith 
PairwiseWith 

 Returns a series containing the result of applying the specified function to each
 pair of consecutive values in the series. The function receives the key and both the
 preceding and the current value.

### [SeriesExtensions.PctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#PctChange)

SeriesExtensions.PctChange 
PctChange 

 Returns a series containing the percentage change between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling <code>PctChange(1)</code> returns a series
 where each value is the relative change from the previous value.

### [SeriesExtensions.PctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#PctChange)

SeriesExtensions.PctChange 
PctChange 

 Returns a series containing the percentage change between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling <code>PctChange(1)</code> returns a series
 where each value is the relative change from the previous value.

### [SeriesExtensions.PctChange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#PctChange)

SeriesExtensions.PctChange 
PctChange 

 Returns a series containing the percentage change between a value in the original series and
 a value at the specified offset. For example, calling <code>PctChange(1)</code> returns a series
 where each value is the relative change from the previous value:

     result[k] = (series[k] - series[k - offset]) / series[k - offset]

 This is commonly used in financial analysis to compute returns (e.g. daily stock returns).

### [SeriesExtensions.Print](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Print)

SeriesExtensions.Print 
Print

### [SeriesExtensions.Rank](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Rank)

SeriesExtensions.Rank 
Rank 
 Returns a new series containing the dense rank of each value. The rank is 1-based:
 the smallest value receives rank 1. Ties receive the same rank.

### [SeriesExtensions.RankWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#RankWith)

SeriesExtensions.RankWith 
RankWith 
 Returns a new series containing the dense rank of each value, using the specified
 comparer to determine ordering. The rank is 1-based. Ties receive the same rank.

### [SeriesExtensions.ReplaceValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ReplaceValue)

SeriesExtensions.ReplaceValue 
ReplaceValue 

 Returns a new series where all occurrences of <code>oldValue</code> are replaced with
 <code>newValue</code>. Missing values in the series are left unchanged.

### [SeriesExtensions.ResampleEquivalence](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ResampleEquivalence)

SeriesExtensions.ResampleEquivalence 
ResampleEquivalence 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys. A specified function
 `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and the observations for which the
 projected keys are equivalent are grouped into chunks. The chunks are then transformed
 to values using the provided function `f`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered. For unordered
 series, similar functionality can be implemented using `GroupBy`.

### [SeriesExtensions.ResampleEquivalence](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ResampleEquivalence)

SeriesExtensions.ResampleEquivalence 
ResampleEquivalence 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys. A specified function
 `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and the observations for which the
 projected keys are equivalent are grouped into chunks. The chunks are then returned
 as nested series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered. For unordered
 series, similar functionality can be implemented using `GroupBy`.

### [SeriesExtensions.ResampleUniform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ResampleUniform)

SeriesExtensions.ResampleUniform 
ResampleUniform 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys and also generate values
 for all keys of the target space that are between the minimal and maximal key of the
 specified series (e.g. generate value for all days in the range covered by the series).
 A specified function `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and `nextKey`
 is used to generate all keys in the range. The last value of each chunk is returned.

 When there are no values for a (generated) key, then the function attempts to get the
 greatest value from the previous smaller chunk (i.e. value for the previous date time).
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.ResampleUniform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ResampleUniform)

SeriesExtensions.ResampleUniform 
ResampleUniform 

 Resample the series based on equivalence class on the keys and also generate values
 for all keys of the target space that are between the minimal and maximal key of the
 specified series (e.g. generate value for all days in the range covered by the series).
 For each equivalence class (e.g. date), select the latest value (with greatest key).
 A specified function `keyProj` is used to project keys to another space and `nextKey`
 is used to generate all keys in the range.

 When there are no values for a (generated) key, then the function attempts to get the
 greatest value from the previous smaller chunk (i.e. value for the previous date time).
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting from the smallest key of the original series, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys. The function generates samples
 at, or just before the end of an interval and at, or after, the end of the series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting from the smallest key of the original series, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys. The function generates samples
 at, or just before the end of an interval and at, or after, the end of the series.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting from the smallest key of the original series, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys according to `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting from the smallest key of the original series, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys according to `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting at the specified `start` time, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys according to `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Finds values at, or near, the specified times in a given series. The operation generates
 keys starting at the specified `start` time, using the specified `interval`
 and then finds nearest smaller values close to such keys according to `dir`.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sample)

SeriesExtensions.Sample 
Sample 

 Sample an (ordered) series by finding the value at the exact or closest prior key
 for some new sequence of keys.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.SampleInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#SampleInto)

SeriesExtensions.SampleInto 
SampleInto 

 Performs sampling by time and aggregates chunks obtained by time-sampling into a single
 value using a specified function. The operation generates keys starting at the first
 key in the source series, using the specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on
 these keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.SampleInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#SampleInto)

SeriesExtensions.SampleInto 
SampleInto 

 Performs sampling by time and aggregates chunks obtained by time-sampling into a single
 value using a specified function. The operation generates keys starting at the first
 key in the source series, using the specified `interval` and then obtains chunks based on
 these keys in a fashion similar to the `Series.resample` function.
  

 This operation is only supported on ordered series. The method throws
 `InvalidOperationException` when the series is not ordered.

### [SeriesExtensions.Shift](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Shift)

SeriesExtensions.Shift 
Shift 

 Returns a series with values shifted by the specified offset. When the offset is
 positive, the values are shifted forward and first `offset` keys are dropped. When the
 offset is negative, the values are shifted backwards and the last `offset` keys are dropped.
 Expressed in pseudo-code:

     result[k] = series[k - offset]
  

 If you want to calculate the difference, e.g. `s - (Series.shift 1 s)`, you can
 use `Series.diff` which will be a little bit faster.

### [SeriesExtensions.Sort](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Sort)

SeriesExtensions.Sort 
Sort

### [SeriesExtensions.SortBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#SortBy)

SeriesExtensions.SortBy 
SortBy

### [SeriesExtensions.SortByKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#SortByKey)

SeriesExtensions.SortByKey 
SortByKey 

 Returns a new series whose entries are reordered according to index order

### [SeriesExtensions.SortWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#SortWith)

SeriesExtensions.SortWith 
SortWith

### [SeriesExtensions.TryFirstValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#TryFirstValue)

SeriesExtensions.TryFirstValue 
TryFirstValue

### [SeriesExtensions.TryLastValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#TryLastValue)

SeriesExtensions.TryLastValue 
TryLastValue

### [SeriesExtensions.Window](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#Window)

SeriesExtensions.Window 
Window

### [SeriesExtensions.WindowInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#WindowInto)

SeriesExtensions.WindowInto 
WindowInto

### [SeriesExtensions.WindowInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#WindowInto)

SeriesExtensions.WindowInto 
WindowInto

### [SeriesExtensions.WindowWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#WindowWhile)

SeriesExtensions.WindowWhile 
WindowWhile 

 Creates a series of sliding windows. The windows are created based on the specified
 condition: a new window is started when the condition on consecutive keys is no longer met.
 Returns a series of nested series, one per window.

### [SeriesExtensions.WindowWhileInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#WindowWhileInto)

SeriesExtensions.WindowWhileInto 
WindowWhileInto 

 Creates a series of sliding windows. The windows are created based on the specified
 condition: a new window is started when the condition on consecutive keys is no longer met.
 Each window is then aggregated into a single value using the provided <code>reduce</code>
 function.

### [SeriesExtensions.ZipAlignInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ZipAlignInto)

SeriesExtensions.ZipAlignInto 
ZipAlignInto 

 Align two series using the specified join kind and key lookup, and combine
 matching values using the provided function. When either value is missing
 (outer-join key present in only one series), the corresponding
 <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/optionalvalue{t}">OptionalValue{T}</a> will have <code>HasValue = false</code>. Return
 an <code>OptionalValue</code> with <code>HasValue = false</code> to produce a missing
 value in the result.

### [SeriesExtensions.ZipInner](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ZipInner)

SeriesExtensions.ZipInner 
ZipInner 

 Align two series using inner join and exact key matching. The result is a
 series of tuples containing the values from both series for each shared key.
 Keys that are present in only one of the series are dropped.

### [SeriesExtensions.ZipInto](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesextensions.html#ZipInto)

SeriesExtensions.ZipInto 
ZipInto 

 Align two series using inner join and exact key matching, and combine
 matching values using the provided function. Keys that are present in
 only one of the series are dropped (inner-join semantics). This is a
 convenience alternative to the arithmetic operators for series with custom
 element types that have their own operators defined.

### [SeriesStatsExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html)

SeriesStatsExtensions 

 The type implements C# and F# extension methods that add numerical operations
 to Deedle series.

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.InterpolateLinear](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#InterpolateLinear)

SeriesStatsExtensions.InterpolateLinear 
InterpolateLinear 

 Linearly interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys.

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.InterpolateLinearWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#InterpolateLinearWith)

SeriesStatsExtensions.InterpolateLinearWith 
InterpolateLinearWith 

 Linearly interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys, with
 configurable extrapolation behavior for keys that fall outside the range of
 the original series.

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Kurtosis](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Kurtosis)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Kurtosis 
Kurtosis 
 Returns the kurtosis of the elements of the series.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Max](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Max)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Max 
Max 
 Returns the maximum of the values in a series. The result is an float value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.MaxLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#MaxLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.MaxLevel 
MaxLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the greatest element of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Mean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Mean)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Mean 
Mean 
 Returns the mean of the elements of the series.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.MeanLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#MeanLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.MeanLevel 
MeanLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the mean of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Median](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Median)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Median 
Median 
 Returns the median of the elements of the series.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.MedianLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#MedianLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.MedianLevel 
MedianLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the median of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Min](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Min)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Min 
Min 
 Returns the minimum of the values in a series. The result is an float value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.MinLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#MinLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.MinLevel 
MinLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the smallest element of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.NumSum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#NumSum)

SeriesStatsExtensions.NumSum 
NumSum 
 Returns the sum of the elements of the series of numeric values.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.Skewness](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#Skewness)

SeriesStatsExtensions.Skewness 
Skewness 
 Returns the skewness of the elements of the series.
 <category>Statistics</category>

SeriesStatsExtensions.StandardDeviation

SeriesStatsExtensions.StdDev 
StdDev 
 Returns the standard deviation of the elements of the series.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.StdDevLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#StdDevLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.StdDevLevel 
StdDevLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the standard deviation of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

SeriesStatsExtensions.Sum 
Sum 
 Returns the sum of the elements of the series of float values.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.SumLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#SumLevel)

SeriesStatsExtensions.SumLevel 
SumLevel 

 Groups the elements of the input series in groups based on the keys
 produced by `groupSelector` and then returns a new series containing
 the sum of each group.

 This operation is designed to be used with [hierarchical indexing](../features.html#indexing).

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.TryMax](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#TryMax)

SeriesStatsExtensions.TryMax 
TryMax 
 Returns the maximum of the values in a series. The result is an option value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [SeriesStatsExtensions.TryMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-seriesstatsextensions.html#TryMin)

SeriesStatsExtensions.TryMin 
TryMin 
 Returns the minimum of the values in a series. The result is an option value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.
 <category>Statistics</category>

### [Stats.corr](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#corr)

Stats.corr 
corr 

 Returns the Pearson correlation coefficient of the values in two series.
 The series are aligned on their keys (inner join) and correlation is computed
 over all key-aligned, non-missing pairs. Returns `NaN` when fewer than 2 pairs
 are available or when either series has zero variance.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of either
 series is not convertible to floating point number.

### [Stats.corrFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#corrFrame)

Stats.corrFrame 
corrFrame 

 Returns the pairwise Pearson correlation matrix of the numerical columns
 in the input frame. Only columns convertible to <code>float</code> are included.
 The result is a square <code>Frame<'C, 'C></code> where entry <code>(c1, c2)</code>
 is the correlation coefficient of columns <code>c1</code> and <code>c2</code>.
 Diagonal entries are 1.0; off-diagonal entries are NaN when either column
 has fewer than 2 non-missing values or zero variance.

### [Stats.count](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#count)

Stats.count 
count 
 For each column, returns the number of the values in the column.
 This excludes missing values and values created from `Double.NaN` etc.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.count](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#count)

Stats.count 
count 
 Returns the number of the values in a series. This excludes missing values
 and values created from `Double.NaN` etc.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.cov](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#cov)

Stats.cov 
cov 

 Returns the sample covariance of the values in two series. The series are aligned
 on their keys (inner join) and covariance is computed over all key-aligned,
 non-missing pairs. Returns `NaN` when fewer than 2 pairs are available.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of either
 series is not convertible to floating point number.

### [Stats.covFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#covFrame)

Stats.covFrame 
covFrame 

 Returns the pairwise sample covariance matrix of the numerical columns
 in the input frame. Only columns convertible to <code>float</code> are included.
 The result is a square <code>Frame<'C, 'C></code> where entry <code>(c1, c2)</code>
 is the sample covariance of columns <code>c1</code> and <code>c2</code>.
 Diagonal entries are the sample variance of each column; off-diagonal entries
 are NaN when either column has fewer than 2 non-missing values.

### [Stats.describe](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#describe)

Stats.describe 
describe 
 Returns a frame with summary statistics (unique count, mean, standard
 deviation, min, lower quartile, median, upper quartile, max) for each
 numerical column of the input frame. Only columns convertible to
 <c>float</c> are included; the row keys of the result are the statistic
 names ("unique", "mean", "std", "min", "0.25", "0.5", "0.75", "max").

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.describe](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#describe)

Stats.describe 
describe 
 Returns the series of main statistic values of the series.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.expandingCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingCount)

Stats.expandingCount 
expandingCount 
 Returns a series that contains counts over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys).

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingKurt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingKurt)

Stats.expandingKurt 
expandingKurt 
 Returns a series that contains kurtosis over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains fewer than 4 values, the result is missing.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingMax](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingMax)

Stats.expandingMax 
expandingMax 
 Returns a series that contains maximum over an expanding window. The value
 for a key _k_ in the returned series is the maximum from all elements with
 smaller keys.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingMean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingMean)

Stats.expandingMean 
expandingMean 
 Returns a series that contains means over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains no values, the result is missing.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingMedian](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingMedian)

Stats.expandingMedian 
expandingMedian 
 Returns a series that contains median over an expanding window. The value
 for a key _k_ in the returned series is the median from all elements with
 smaller keys.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingMin)

Stats.expandingMin 
expandingMin 
 Returns a series that contains minimum over an expanding window. The value
 for a key _k_ in the returned series is the minimum from all elements with
 smaller keys.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingSkew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingSkew)

Stats.expandingSkew 
expandingSkew 
 Returns a series that contains skewness over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains fewer than 3 values, the result is missing.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingStdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingStdDev)

Stats.expandingStdDev 
expandingStdDev 
 Returns a series that contains standard deviation over expanding windows (the
 value for a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains fewer than 2 values, the result is missing.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingSum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingSum)

Stats.expandingSum 
expandingSum 
 Returns a series that contains sums over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains no values, the result is 0.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.expandingVariance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#expandingVariance)

Stats.expandingVariance 
expandingVariance 
 Returns a series that contains variance over expanding windows (the value for
 a given key is calculated from all elements with smaller keys); If the
 entire window contains fewer than 2 values, the result is missing.

 <category>Expanding windows</category>

### [Stats.interpolate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#interpolate)

Stats.interpolate 
interpolate 

 Interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys. The function iterates through
 each new key, and invokes a function on the current key, the nearest smaller and larger valid
 observations from the series argument. The function must return a new valid float.

### [Stats.interpolateLinear](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#interpolateLinear)

Stats.interpolateLinear 
interpolateLinear 

 Linearly interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

### [Stats.interpolateLinearWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#interpolateLinearWith)

Stats.interpolateLinearWith 
interpolateLinearWith 

 Linearly interpolates an ordered series given a new sequence of keys, with
 configurable extrapolation behavior for keys that fall outside the range of the
 original series. Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the
 value type of the series is not convertible to a floating point number.

### [Stats.kurt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#kurt)

Stats.kurt 
kurt 
 For each numerical column, returns the kurtosis of the values in a series.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 4 values,
 the result is NaN.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.kurt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#kurt)

Stats.kurt 
kurt 
 Returns the kurtosis of the values in a series. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 4 values, the result is NaN.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelCount)

Stats.levelCount 
levelCount 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the number of the values in the group. This excludes missing
 values and values created from `Double.NaN` etc.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelKurt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelKurt)

Stats.levelKurt 
levelKurt 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the kurtosis of the values in a series. The function skips over missing values
 and `NaN` values. When there are less than 4 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelMax](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelMax)

Stats.levelMax 
levelMax 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the maximum of the values in the group. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When the group contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelMean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelMean)

Stats.levelMean 
levelMean 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the mean of the values in the group. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When there are no available values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelMedian](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelMedian)

Stats.levelMedian 
levelMedian 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the median of the values in the group.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelMin)

Stats.levelMin 
levelMin 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the minimum of the values in the group. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When the group contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelSkew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelSkew)

Stats.levelSkew 
levelSkew 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the skewness of the values in a series. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 3 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelStdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelStdDev)

Stats.levelStdDev 
levelStdDev 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the standard deviation of the values in the group. The function skips over
 missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelSum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelSum)

Stats.levelSum 
levelSum 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the sum of the values in the group. The function skips over missing values
 and `NaN` values. When there are no available values, the result is 0.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.levelVariance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#levelVariance)

Stats.levelVariance 
levelVariance 
 For each group with equal keys at the level specified by `level`,
 returns the variance of the values in the group. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Multi-level statistics</category>

### [Stats.max](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#max)

Stats.max 
max 
 For each numerical column, returns the maximal values as a series.
 The function skips over missing and `NaN` values. When there are no values,
 the result is `NaN`.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.max](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#max)

Stats.max 
max 
 Returns the maximum of the values in a series. The result is an float value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.maxBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#maxBy)

Stats.maxBy 
maxBy 
 Returns the key and value of the greatest element in the series. The result
 is an optional value. When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.mean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#mean)

Stats.mean 
mean 
 For each numerical column, returns the mean of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are
 no available values, the result is NaN.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.mean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#mean)

Stats.mean 
mean 
 Returns the mean of the values in a series. The function skips over missing values
 and `NaN` values. When there are no available values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.median](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#median)

Stats.median 
median 
 For each numerical column, returns the median of the values in the column.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.median](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#median)

Stats.median 
median 
 Returns the median of the elements of the series.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.min](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#min)

Stats.min 
min 
 For each numerical column, returns the minimal values as a series.
 The function skips over missing and `NaN` values. When there are no values,
 the result is `NaN`.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.min](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#min)

Stats.min 
min 
 Returns the minimum of the values in a series. The result is an float value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.minBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#minBy)

Stats.minBy 
minBy 
 Returns the key and value of the least element in the series. The result
 is an optional value. When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.movingCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingCount)

Stats.movingCount 
movingCount 
 Returns a series that contains counts over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is 0.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingKurt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingKurt)

Stats.movingKurt 
movingKurt 
 Returns a series that contains kurtosis over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingMax](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingMax)

Stats.movingMax 
movingMax 
 Returns a series that contains maximum over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements are calculated using smaller windows spanning over `1 .. size-1`
 values. If the entire window contains missing values, the result is missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingMean](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingMean)

Stats.movingMean 
movingMean 
 Returns a series that contains means over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingMedian](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingMedian)

Stats.movingMedian 
movingMedian 
 Returns a series that contains median over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingMin)

Stats.movingMin 
movingMin 
 Returns a series that contains minimum over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements are calculated using smaller windows spanning over `1 .. size-1`
 values. If the entire window contains missing values, the result is missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingSkew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingSkew)

Stats.movingSkew 
movingSkew 
 Returns a series that contains skewness over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingStdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingStdDev)

Stats.movingStdDev 
movingStdDev 
 Returns a series that contains standard deviations over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingSum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingSum)

Stats.movingSum 
movingSum 
 Returns a series that contains sums over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is 0.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is notconvertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.movingVariance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#movingVariance)

Stats.movingVariance 
movingVariance 
 Returns a series that contains variance over a moving window of the specified size.
 The first `size-1` elements of the returned series are always missing; if the
 entire window contains missing values, the result is also missing.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the specified series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Moving windows</category>

### [Stats.numSum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#numSum)

Stats.numSum 
numSum 
 Sum that operates only any appropriate numeric type. When there are no available
 values, the result is zero of the approriate numeric type.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.quantile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#quantile)

Stats.quantile 
quantile 
 Returns the series of quantiles of the series. Excel version of quantile,
 equivalent to QuantileDefinition.R7 from Math.Net
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.skew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#skew)

Stats.skew 
skew 
 For each numerical column, returns the skewness of the values in a series.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 3 values,
 the result is NaN.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.skew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#skew)

Stats.skew 
skew 
 Returns the skewness of the values in a series. The function skips over missing
 values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 3 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.stdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#stdDev)

Stats.stdDev 
stdDev 
 For each numerical column, returns the standard deviation of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values,
 the result is NaN.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.stdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#stdDev)

Stats.stdDev 
stdDev 
 Returns the standard deviation of the values in a series. The function skips over
 missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

Stats.sum 
sum 
 For each numerical column, returns the sum of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are no
 available values, the result is 0.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.sum](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#sum)

Stats.sum 
sum 
 Returns the sum of the values in a series. The function skips over missing values
 and `NaN` values. When there are no available values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.tryMax](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#tryMax)

Stats.tryMax 
tryMax 
 Returns the maximum of the values in a series. The result is an option value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.tryMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#tryMin)

Stats.tryMin 
tryMin 
 Returns the minimum of the values in a series. The result is an option value.
 When the series contains no values, the result is `None`.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [Stats.uniqueCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#uniqueCount)

Stats.uniqueCount 
uniqueCount 
 For each column, returns the number of unique values.

### [Stats.uniqueCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#uniqueCount)

Stats.uniqueCount 
uniqueCount 
 Returns the number of unique values in a series.

### [Stats.variance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#variance)

Stats.variance 
variance 
 For each numerical column, returns the variance of the values in the column.
 The function skips over missing values and `NaN` values. When there are less
 than 2 values, the result is NaN.

 <category>Frame statistics</category>

### [Stats.variance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-stats.html#variance)

Stats.variance 
variance 
 Returns the variance of the values in a series. The function skips over missing values
 and `NaN` values. When there are less than 2 values, the result is NaN.
 Throws a `FormatException` or an `InvalidCastException` if the value type of the series
 is not convertible to floating point number.

 <category>Series statistics</category>

### [TryValue<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html)

TryValue<'T> 

 Represents a value or an exception. This type is used by functions such as
 `Series.tryMap` and `Frame.tryMap` to capture the result of a lambda function,
 which may be either a value or an exception. The type is a discriminated union,
 so it can be processed using F# pattern matching, or using `Value`, `HasValue`
 and `Exception` properties
  
TryValue<'T>.HasValue 
HasValue 
TryValue<'T>.Exception 
Exception 
TryValue<'T>.Value 
Value 
TryValue<'T>.IsSuccess 
IsSuccess 
TryValue<'T>.IsError 
IsError 
TryValue<'T>.Success 
Success 
TryValue<'T>.Error 
Error

### [TryValue<'T>.HasValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#HasValue)

TryValue<'T>.HasValue 
HasValue 
 Returns `true` when the `TryValue<T>` object represents a
 successfully calculated value

### [TryValue<'T>.Exception](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#Exception)

TryValue<'T>.Exception 
Exception 
 Returns the exception captured by this value. When `HasValue = true`,
 accessing the property throws `InvalidOperationException`.

### [TryValue<'T>.Value](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#Value)

TryValue<'T>.Value 
Value 
 Returns the value of `TryValue<T>` when the value is present;
 otherwise, throws an exception that was captured

### [TryValue<'T>.IsSuccess](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#IsSuccess)

TryValue<'T>.IsSuccess 
IsSuccess

### [TryValue<'T>.IsError](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#IsError)

TryValue<'T>.IsError 
IsError

### [TryValue<'T>.Success](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#Success)

TryValue<'T>.Success 
Success

### [TryValue<'T>.Error](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryvalue-1.html#Error)

TryValue<'T>.Error 
Error

### [UnionBehavior](/Deedle/reference/deedle-unionbehavior.html)

UnionBehavior 

 This enumeration specifies the behavior of `Union` operation on series when there are
 overlapping keys in two series that are being unioned. The options include preferring values
 from the left/right series or throwing an exception when both values are available.
  
UnionBehavior.PreferLeft 
PreferLeft 
UnionBehavior.PreferRight 
PreferRight 
UnionBehavior.Exclusive 
Exclusive

### [UnionBehavior.PreferLeft](/Deedle/reference/deedle-unionbehavior.html#PreferLeft)

UnionBehavior.PreferLeft 
PreferLeft 
 When there are values available in both series that are being unioned, prefer the left value.

### [UnionBehavior.PreferRight](/Deedle/reference/deedle-unionbehavior.html#PreferRight)

UnionBehavior.PreferRight 
PreferRight 
 When there are values available in both series that are being unioned, prefer the right value.

### [UnionBehavior.Exclusive](/Deedle/reference/deedle-unionbehavior.html#Exclusive)

UnionBehavior.Exclusive 
Exclusive 
 When there are values available in both series that are being unioned, raise an exception.

### [VectorCallSite<'R>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectorcallsite-1.html)

VectorCallSite<'R> 

 Represents a generic function <code>∀.'T.(IVector<'T> -> 'R)</code>. The function can be
 generically invoked on an argument of type <code>IVector</code> using <code>IVector.Invoke</code>.
  
VectorCallSite<'R>.Invoke 
Invoke

### [VectorCallSite<'R>.Invoke](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectorcallsite-1.html#Invoke)

VectorCallSite<'R>.Invoke 
Invoke

### ['T opt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-opt-1.html)

'T opt 

 A type alias for the <code>OptionalValue<T></code> type. The type alias can be used
 to make F# type definitions that use optional values directly more succinct.
  
'T opt.HasValue 
HasValue 
'T opt.Value 
Value 
'T opt.ValueOrDefault 
ValueOrDefault 
'T opt.Missing 
Missing

### ['T opt.HasValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-opt-1.html#HasValue)

'T opt.HasValue 
HasValue 
 Gets a value indicating whether the current `OptionalValue<T>` has a value

### ['T opt.Value](/Deedle/reference/deedle-opt-1.html#Value)

'T opt.Value 
Value 
<pre> Returns the value stored in the current `OptionalValue<T>`.
 Exceptions:
   `InvalidOperationException` - Thrown when `HasValue` is `false`.</pre>

### ['T opt.ValueOrDefault](/Deedle/reference/deedle-opt-1.html#ValueOrDefault)

'T opt.ValueOrDefault 
ValueOrDefault 
 Returns the value stored in the current `OptionalValue<T>` or
 the default value of the type `T` when a value is not present.

### ['T opt.Missing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-opt-1.html#Missing)

'T opt.Missing 
Missing 
 Returns a new instance of `OptionalValue<T>` that does not contain a value.

### ['T tryval](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryval-1.html)

'T tryval 

 A type alias for the <code>TryValue<T></code> type. The type alias can be used
 to make F# type declarations that explcitly handle exceptions more succinct.
  
'T tryval.HasValue 
HasValue 
'T tryval.Exception 
Exception 
'T tryval.Value 
Value 
'T tryval.IsSuccess 
IsSuccess 
'T tryval.IsError 
IsError

### ['T tryval.HasValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryval-1.html#HasValue)

'T tryval.HasValue 
HasValue 
 Returns `true` when the `TryValue<T>` object represents a
 successfully calculated value

### ['T tryval.Exception](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryval-1.html#Exception)

'T tryval.Exception 
Exception 
 Returns the exception captured by this value. When `HasValue = true`,
 accessing the property throws `InvalidOperationException`.

### ['T tryval.Value](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryval-1.html#Value)

'T tryval.Value 
Value 
 Returns the value of `TryValue<T>` when the value is present;
 otherwise, throws an exception that was captured

### ['T tryval.IsSuccess](/Deedle/reference/deedle-tryval-1.html#IsSuccess)

'T tryval.IsSuccess

### [Excel.GetFsiSeriesAndFrames](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excel.html#GetFsiSeriesAndFrames)

GetFsiSeriesAndFrames 
 Inspect the FSharp interactive session and find all Series and Frames

### [Excel.asFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excel.html#asFrame)

Excel.asFrame 
asFrame

### [Excel.DeedleToExcel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excel.html#DeedleToExcel)

Excel.DeedleToExcel 
DeedleToExcel 
 Copy all Series and Frames in the FSharp interactive session to
 Excel. A sheet is created for each Series and each Frame.
 As series and frames are added to the session they will be added to
 the current Excel workbook as new sheets.
