### [Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html)

Frame 

 Arrow-specific functions on Deedle <code>Frame</code> values.
 Open <code>Deedle.Arrow</code> and then call these as <code>Frame.readArrow</code>,
 <code>Frame.writeArrow</code>, <code>Frame.toRecordBatch</code>, etc.
  
Frame.toRecordBatch 
toRecordBatch 
Frame.ofRecordBatch 
ofRecordBatch 
Frame.readArrow 
readArrow 
Frame.writeArrow 
writeArrow 
Frame.readArrowStream 
readArrowStream 
Frame.writeArrowStream 
writeArrowStream 
Frame.readFeather 
readFeather 
Frame.writeFeather 
writeFeather 
Frame.writeArrowWithIndex 
writeArrowWithIndex 
Frame.readArrowWithIndex 
readArrowWithIndex 
Frame.writeFeatherWithIndex 
writeFeatherWithIndex 
Frame.readFeatherWithIndex 
readFeatherWithIndex

### [Frame.toRecordBatch](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#toRecordBatch)

Frame.toRecordBatch 
toRecordBatch 

 Convert a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to an Apache Arrow <code>RecordBatch</code>.
 Column keys are converted to strings via <code>ToString()</code>.
 Row keys are <em>not</em> included; use <code>Frame.writeArrow</code> / <code>Frame.readArrow</code>
 for full round-trip serialisation.

### [Frame.ofRecordBatch](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#ofRecordBatch)

Frame.ofRecordBatch 
ofRecordBatch 

 Convert an Apache Arrow <code>RecordBatch</code> to a Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 Row keys are 0-based integers. Arrow null values become Deedle missing values.

### [Frame.readArrow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#readArrow)

Frame.readArrow 
readArrow 

 Read an Arrow IPC file (<code>.arrow</code> or Feather v2 <code>.feather</code>) into a
 Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 Files with multiple record batches are concatenated into a single frame.

### [Frame.writeArrow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#writeArrow)

Frame.writeArrow 
writeArrow 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to an Arrow IPC file (the standard <code>.arrow</code> format,
 also compatible with Feather v2 <code>.feather</code> files).

### [Frame.readArrowStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#readArrowStream)

Frame.readArrowStream 
readArrowStream 

 Read an Arrow IPC stream into a Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 All record batches in the stream are concatenated into a single frame.

### [Frame.writeArrowStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#writeArrowStream)

Frame.writeArrowStream 
writeArrowStream 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to an Arrow IPC stream (suitable for network transport
 or streaming pipelines). The stream is left open after writing.

### [Frame.readFeather](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#readFeather)

Frame.readFeather 
readFeather 

 Read a Feather v2 file (<code>.feather</code>) into a Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 Feather v2 is the Arrow IPC file format — this is an alias for <code>Frame.readArrow</code>.

### [Frame.writeFeather](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#writeFeather)

Frame.writeFeather 
writeFeather 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to a Feather v2 file (<code>.feather</code>).
 Feather v2 is the Arrow IPC file format — this is an alias for <code>Frame.writeArrow</code>.

### [Frame.writeArrowWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#writeArrowWithIndex)

Frame.writeArrowWithIndex 
writeArrowWithIndex 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to an Arrow IPC file, storing row keys in a special
 <code>__index__</code> column so they can be restored on read.
 Row keys are serialised via <code>ToString()</code>.

### [Frame.readArrowWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#readArrowWithIndex)

Frame.readArrowWithIndex 
readArrowWithIndex 

 Read an Arrow IPC file that was written with <code>Frame.writeArrowWithIndex</code>,
 restoring the original string row keys.
 If no <code>__index__</code> column is present, returns a frame with 0-based integer
 row keys converted to strings.

### [Frame.writeFeatherWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#writeFeatherWithIndex)

Frame.writeFeatherWithIndex 
writeFeatherWithIndex 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to a Feather v2 file, storing row keys in a special
 <code>__index__</code> column so they can be restored on read.

### [Frame.readFeatherWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-frame.html#readFeatherWithIndex)

Frame.readFeatherWithIndex 
readFeatherWithIndex 

 Read a Feather v2 file that was written with <code>Frame.writeFeatherWithIndex</code>,
 restoring the original string row keys.

### [Series](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-series.html)

Series 

 Arrow-specific functions on Deedle <code>Series</code> values.
 Open <code>Deedle.Arrow</code> and then call these as <code>Series.toArrowArray</code>,
 <code>Series.ofArrowArray</code>, etc.
  
Series.toArrowArray 
toArrowArray 
Series.ofArrowArray 
ofArrowArray

### [Series.toArrowArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-series.html#toArrowArray)

Series.toArrowArray 
toArrowArray 

 Convert a Deedle <code>Series</code> to an Apache Arrow <code>IArrowArray</code>.
 The series element type determines the Arrow array type (same mapping as
 <code>Frame.toRecordBatch</code>). Missing values are encoded as Arrow validity-bitmap nulls.

### [Series.ofArrowArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-series.html#ofArrowArray)

Series.ofArrowArray 
ofArrowArray 

 Convert an Apache Arrow <code>IArrowArray</code> to a Deedle <code>Series<int,obj></code>.
 Row keys are 0-based integers. Arrow null values become Deedle missing values.
 The values are boxed as <code>obj</code>; use <code>Series.map</code> to obtain a typed series.

### [ArrowFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html)

ArrowFrame 

 Static factory methods for reading Arrow / Feather files, accessible from C# as
 <code>ArrowFrame.ReadArrow(path)</code> etc.
  
ArrowFrame.ReadArrow 
ReadArrow 
ArrowFrame.ReadArrowStream 
ReadArrowStream 
ArrowFrame.ReadArrowWithIndex 
ReadArrowWithIndex 
ArrowFrame.ReadFeather 
ReadFeather 
ArrowFrame.ReadFeatherWithIndex 
ReadFeatherWithIndex

### [ArrowFrame.ReadArrow](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html#ReadArrow)

ArrowFrame.ReadArrow 
ReadArrow 
Read an Arrow IPC file into a <code>Frame<int,string></code>.

### [ArrowFrame.ReadArrowStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html#ReadArrowStream)

ArrowFrame.ReadArrowStream 
ReadArrowStream 
Read an Arrow IPC stream into a <code>Frame<int,string></code>.

### [ArrowFrame.ReadArrowWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html#ReadArrowWithIndex)

ArrowFrame.ReadArrowWithIndex 
ReadArrowWithIndex 

 Read an Arrow IPC file written with <code>WriteArrowWithIndex</code>,
 restoring string row keys.

### [ArrowFrame.ReadFeather](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html#ReadFeather)

ArrowFrame.ReadFeather 
ReadFeather 
Read a Feather v2 file into a <code>Frame<int,string></code>.

### [ArrowFrame.ReadFeatherWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-arrowframe.html#ReadFeatherWithIndex)

ArrowFrame.ReadFeatherWithIndex 
ReadFeatherWithIndex 

 Read a Feather v2 file written with <code>WriteFeatherWithIndex</code>,
 restoring string row keys.

### [FrameArrowExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-arrow-framearrowextensions.html)

 an <code>__index__</code> column for round-tripping.

Frame.readExcel 
readExcel 

 Read the first worksheet of an Excel file as a <code>Frame<int, string></code>.

### [Frame.readExcelSheet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-frame.html#readExcelSheet)

Frame.readExcelSheet 
readExcelSheet 
 Read a specific worksheet by name from an Excel file.

### [Frame.readExcelSheetByIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-frame.html#readExcelSheetByIndex)

Frame.readExcelSheetByIndex 
readExcelSheetByIndex 
 Read a specific worksheet by zero-based index from an Excel file.

### [Frame.sheetNames](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-frame.html#sheetNames)

Frame.sheetNames 
sheetNames 
 Return the names of all worksheets in an Excel file.

### [ExcelFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-excelframe.html)

ExcelFrame 

 Static factory methods for reading Excel files, accessible from C# as
 <code>ExcelFrame.ReadExcel(path)</code> etc.

ExcelFrame.SheetNames

### [ExcelFrame.ReadExcel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-excelframe.html#ReadExcel)

ExcelFrame.ReadExcel 
ReadExcel 

 Read the first worksheet of an Excel file (.xls or .xlsx) as a
 <code>Frame<int, string></code>. The first row is treated as column headers.

### [ExcelFrame.ReadExcelSheet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-excelframe.html#ReadExcelSheet)

ExcelFrame.ReadExcelSheet 
ReadExcelSheet 
 Read a specific worksheet by name from an Excel file (.xls or .xlsx).
 The first row is treated as column headers.

### [ExcelFrame.ReadExcelSheetByIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-excelframe.html#ReadExcelSheetByIndex)

ExcelFrame.ReadExcelSheetByIndex 
ReadExcelSheetByIndex 
 Read a specific worksheet by zero-based index from an Excel file (.xls or .xlsx).
 The first row is treated as column headers.

### [ExcelFrame.SheetNames](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelreader-excelframe.html#SheetNames)

ExcelFrame.SheetNames 
SheetNames 
 Return the names of all worksheets in an Excel file.

### [Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html)

Frame 

 F# module extensions for writing Excel files.
 Open <code>Deedle.ExcelWriter</code> and then call these as <code>Frame.writeExcel</code>,
 <code>Frame.writeExcelSheet</code>, etc.
  
Frame.writeExcel 
writeExcel 
Frame.writeExcelSheet 
writeExcelSheet 
Frame.writeExcelSheets 
writeExcelSheets 
Frame.writeExcelStream 
writeExcelStream 
Frame.writeExcelSheetStream 
writeExcelSheetStream

### [Frame.writeExcel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html#writeExcel)

Frame.writeExcel 
writeExcel 
 Write a frame to the first sheet of an Excel (.xlsx) file.
 Column keys become the header row. Any existing file is overwritten.

### [Frame.writeExcelSheet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html#writeExcelSheet)

Frame.writeExcelSheet 
writeExcelSheet 
 Write a frame to a named sheet of an Excel (.xlsx) file.
 Any existing file is overwritten.

### [Frame.writeExcelSheets](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html#writeExcelSheets)

Frame.writeExcelSheets 
writeExcelSheets 
 Write multiple frames to separate sheets of a single Excel (.xlsx) file.
 Provide a sequence of (sheetName, frame) pairs. Any existing file is overwritten.

### [Frame.writeExcelStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html#writeExcelStream)

Frame.writeExcelStream 
writeExcelStream 
 Write a frame to an Excel (.xlsx) stream (first sheet, default name).

### [Frame.writeExcelSheetStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-excelwriter-frame.html#writeExcelSheetStream)

Frame.writeExcelSheetStream 
writeExcelSheetStream 
 Write a frame to a named sheet of an Excel (.xlsx) stream.

 Column keys become the header row. Any existing file is overwritten.

### [AsyncSeriesConstruction<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-asyncseriesconstruction-1.html)

AsyncSeriesConstruction<'K> 
 Asynchronous version of `SeriesConstruction<'K>`. Returns a workflow that evaluates
 the index, together with a construction to apply (asynchronously) on vectors 
AsyncSeriesConstruction<'K>.Item1 
Item1 
AsyncSeriesConstruction<'K>.Item2 
Item2

### [BoundaryBehavior](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-boundarybehavior.html)

BoundaryBehavior 
 Specifies the boundary behavior for the `IIndexBuilder.GetRange` operation
 (whether the boundary elements should be included or not) 
BoundaryBehavior.IsExclusive 
IsExclusive 
BoundaryBehavior.IsInclusive 
IsInclusive 
BoundaryBehavior.Inclusive 
Inclusive 
BoundaryBehavior.Exclusive

### [BoundaryBehavior.Exclusive](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-boundarybehavior.html#Exclusive)

BoundaryBehavior.Exclusive 
Exclusive

### [IIndex<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html)

IIndex<'K> 
 An interface that represents index mapping keys of some generic type `T` to locations
 of address `Address`. The `IIndex<K>` contains minimal set of operations that have to
 be supported by an index. This type should be only used directly when
 extending the DataFrame library and adding a new way of storing or loading data.
 Values of this type are constructed using the associated `IIndexBuilder` type. 
IIndex<'K>.AddressAt 
AddressAt 
IIndex<'K>.KeyAt 
KeyAt 
IIndex<'K>.Locate 
Locate 
IIndex<'K>.Lookup 
Lookup 
IIndex<'K>.IsOrdered 
IsOrdered 
IIndex<'K>.Mappings 
Mappings 
IIndex<'K>.Builder 
Builder 
IIndex<'K>.Comparer 
Comparer 
IIndex<'K>.Keys 
Keys 
IIndex<'K>.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 
IIndex<'K>.KeyRange 
KeyRange 
IIndex<'K>.AddressingScheme 
AddressingScheme 
IIndex<'K>.KeySequence 
KeySequence 
IIndex<'K>.AddressOperations 
AddressOperations 
IIndex<'K>.KeyCount 
KeyCount

### [IIndex<'K>.AddressAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#AddressAt)

IIndex<'K>.AddressAt 
AddressAt 
 Return an address that represents the specified offset

### [IIndex<'K>.KeyAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#KeyAt)

IIndex<'K>.KeyAt 
KeyAt 
 Performs reverse lookup - and returns key for a specified address

### [IIndex<'K>.Locate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Locate)

IIndex<'K>.Locate 
Locate

### [IIndex<'K>.Lookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Lookup)

IIndex<'K>.Lookup 
Lookup 
 Find the address associated with the specified key, or with the nearest
 key as specified by the `lookup` argument. The `condition` function is called
 when searching for keys to ask the caller whether the address should be returned
 (or whether to continue searching). This is used when searching for previous element
 in a series (where we need to check if a value at the address is available)

### [IIndex<'K>.IsOrdered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#IsOrdered)

IIndex<'K>.IsOrdered 
IsOrdered 
 Returns `true` if the index is ordered and `false` otherwise

### [IIndex<'K>.Mappings](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Mappings)

IIndex<'K>.Mappings 
Mappings 
 Returns all key-address mappings in the index

### [IIndex<'K>.Builder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Builder)

IIndex<'K>.Builder 
Builder 
 Returns an index builder that can be used for constructing new indices of the
 same kind as the current index (e.g. a lazy index returns a lazy index builder)

### [IIndex<'K>.Comparer](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Comparer)

IIndex<'K>.Comparer 
Comparer 
 Returns a comparer associated with the values used by the current index.

### [IIndex<'K>.Keys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#Keys)

IIndex<'K>.Keys 
Keys 
 Returns a (fully evaluated) collection with all keys in the index

### [IIndex<'K>.IsEmpty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#IsEmpty)

IIndex<'K>.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 
 Returns whether the specified index is empty. This is equivalent to
 testing if `Keys` are empty, but it does not have to evaluate delayed index.

### [IIndex<'K>.KeyRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#KeyRange)

IIndex<'K>.KeyRange 
KeyRange 
 Returns the minimal and maximal key associated with the index.
 (the operation may fail for unordered indices)

### [IIndex<'K>.AddressingScheme](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#AddressingScheme)

IIndex<'K>.AddressingScheme 
AddressingScheme 
 Returns the addressing scheme of the index. When creating a series or a frame
 this is compared for equality with the addressing scheme of the vector(s).

### [IIndex<'K>.KeySequence](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#KeySequence)

IIndex<'K>.KeySequence 
KeySequence 
 Returns a lazy sequence that iterates over all keys in the index

### [IIndex<'K>.AddressOperations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#AddressOperations)

IIndex<'K>.AddressOperations 
AddressOperations 
 Returns the address operations associated with this index. The addresses of the
 index are not necesarilly continuous integers from 0. This provides some operations
 that can be used for implementing generic operations over any kind of indices.

### [IIndex<'K>.KeyCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindex-1.html#KeyCount)

IIndex<'K>.KeyCount 
KeyCount 
 Returns the number of keys in the index

### [IIndexBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html)

IIndexBuilder 

 A builder represents various ways of constructing index, either from keys or from
 other indices. The operations that build a new index from an existing index also
 build `VectorConstruction` which specifies how to transform vectors aligned with the
 previous index to match the new index. The methods generally take `VectorConstruction`
 as an input, apply necessary transformations to it and return a new `VectorConstruction`.
  
IIndexBuilder.Aggregate 
Aggregate 
IIndexBuilder.AsyncMaterialize 
AsyncMaterialize 
IIndexBuilder.Create 
Create 
IIndexBuilder.Create 
Create 
IIndexBuilder.DropItem 
DropItem 
IIndexBuilder.GetAddressRange 
GetAddressRange 
IIndexBuilder.GetRange 
GetRange 
IIndexBuilder.GroupBy 
GroupBy 
IIndexBuilder.Intersect 
Intersect 
IIndexBuilder.LookupLevel 
LookupLevel 
IIndexBuilder.Merge 
Merge 
IIndexBuilder.OrderIndex 
OrderIndex 
IIndexBuilder.Project 
Project 
IIndexBuilder.Recreate 
Recreate 
IIndexBuilder.Reindex 
Reindex 
IIndexBuilder.Resample 
Resample 
IIndexBuilder.Search 
Search 
IIndexBuilder.Shift 
Shift 
IIndexBuilder.Union 
Union 
IIndexBuilder.WithIndex 
WithIndex

### [IIndexBuilder.Aggregate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Aggregate)

IIndexBuilder.Aggregate 
Aggregate 

 Aggregate an ordered index into floating windows or chunks.

### [IIndexBuilder.AsyncMaterialize](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#AsyncMaterialize)

IIndexBuilder.AsyncMaterialize 
AsyncMaterialize 
 Given an index and vector construction, return a new index asynchronously
 to allow composing evaluation of lazy series. The command to be applied to
 vectors can be applied asynchronously using `vectorBuilder.AsyncBuild`

### [IIndexBuilder.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Create)

IIndexBuilder.Create 
Create 
 Create a new index using the specified keys. This overload takes data as ReadOnlyCollection
 and so it is more efficient if the caller already has the keys in an allocated collection.
 Optionally, the caller can specify if the index keys are ordered or not. When the value
 is not set, the construction should check and infer this from the data.

### [IIndexBuilder.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Create)

IIndexBuilder.Create 
Create 
 Create a new index using the specified keys. Optionally, the caller can specify
 if the index keys are ordered or not. When the value is not set, the construction
 should check and infer this from the data.

### [IIndexBuilder.DropItem](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#DropItem)

IIndexBuilder.DropItem 
DropItem 
 Drop an item associated with the specified key from the index.

### [IIndexBuilder.GetAddressRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#GetAddressRange)

IIndexBuilder.GetAddressRange 
GetAddressRange 
 Create a new index that represents sub-range of an existing index.
 The range is specified as a pair of addresses, which means that it can be
 used by operations such as "series.Take(5)" (which do not rely on keys)

### [IIndexBuilder.GetRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#GetRange)

IIndexBuilder.GetRange 
GetRange 
 Create a new index that represents sub-range of an existing index. The range is specified
 as a pair of options (when `None`, the original left/right boundary should be used)
 that contain boundary behavior and the boundary key.

### [IIndexBuilder.GroupBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#GroupBy)

IIndexBuilder.GroupBy 
GroupBy 
 Group a (possibly unordered) index according to a provided sequence of labels.
 The operation results in a sequence of unique labels along with corresponding
 series construction objects which can be applied to arbitrary vectors/columns.

### [IIndexBuilder.Intersect](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Intersect)

IIndexBuilder.Intersect 
Intersect 
 Creates an interesection of two indices and builds corresponding vector transformations
 for both vectors that match the left and the right index.

### [IIndexBuilder.LookupLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#LookupLevel)

IIndexBuilder.LookupLevel 
LookupLevel 
 Get items associated with the specified key from the index. This method takes
 `ICustomLookup<K>` which provides an implementation of `ICustomKey<K>`. This
 is used for custom equality testing (for example, when getting a level of a hierarchical index)

### [IIndexBuilder.Merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Merge)

IIndexBuilder.Merge 
Merge 
 Append two indices and builds corresponding vector transformations
 for both vectors that match the left and the right index. If the indices
 are ordered, the ordering should be preserved (the keys should be aligned).
 The specified `VectorListTransform` defines how to deal with the case when
 a key is defined in both indices (i.e. which value should be in the new vector).

### [IIndexBuilder.OrderIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#OrderIndex)

IIndexBuilder.OrderIndex 
OrderIndex 
 Order (possibly unordered) index and return transformation that reorders vector

### [IIndexBuilder.Project](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Project)

IIndexBuilder.Project 
Project 
 When we perform some projection on the vector (`Select` or `Convert`), then we may also
 need to perform some transformation on the index (because it may turn delayed index
 into an evaluated index). If the vector operation does that, then `Project` should do the
 same (e.g. evaluate) on the index.

### [IIndexBuilder.Recreate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Recreate)

IIndexBuilder.Recreate 
Recreate 
 When we create a new vector (`IVectorBuilder.Create`), then we may get a materialized
 vector and we may need to perform the same transformation on the index. This is similar
 to `Project`, but used in different scenarios.

### [IIndexBuilder.Reindex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Reindex)

IIndexBuilder.Reindex 
Reindex 
 Given an old index and a new index, build a vector transformation that reorders
 elements from a vector associated with the old index so that they match the new
 index. When finding element location in the new index, the provided `Lookup` strategy
 is used. This is used, for example, when doing left/right join (to align the new data
 with another index) or when selecting multiple keys (`Series.lookupAll`).

 The proivded `condition` is used when searching for a value in the old index
 (when lookup is not exact). It is called to check that the address contains an
 appropriate value (e.g. when we need to skip over missing values).

### [IIndexBuilder.Resample](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Resample)

IIndexBuilder.Resample 
Resample 
 Aggregate data into non-overlapping chunks by aligning them to the
 specified keys. The second parameter specifies the direction. If it is
 `Direction.Forward` than the key is the first element of a chunk; for
 `Direction.Backward`, the key is the last element (note that this does not
 hold at the boundaries where values before/after the key may also be included)

### [IIndexBuilder.Search](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Search)

IIndexBuilder.Search 
Search 
 Get a series construction that restricts the range of the input to only
 locations where the specified vector contains the specified value.
 (used to filter frame rows according to a column value)

### [IIndexBuilder.Shift](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Shift)

IIndexBuilder.Shift 
Shift 
<pre> Shift the values in the series by a specified offset, in a specified direction.
 The resulting series should be shorter by abs(offset); key for which there is no
 value should be dropped. For example:

     (original)  (shift 1) (shift -1)
     a b c       _ b c     a b _
     1 2 3         1 2     1 2</pre>

### [IIndexBuilder.Union](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#Union)

IIndexBuilder.Union 
Union 
 Creates a union of two indices and builds corresponding vector transformations
 for both vectors that match the left and the right index.

### [IIndexBuilder.WithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-iindexbuilder.html#WithIndex)

IIndexBuilder.WithIndex 
WithIndex 
 Create a new index by picking a new key value for each key in the original index
 (used e.g. when we have a frame and want to use specified column as a new index).

### [SeriesConstruction<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-seriesconstruction-1.html)

SeriesConstruction<'K> 
 Represents a pair of index and vector construction
 (many of the index operations take/return an index together with a construction
 command that builds a vector matching with the index, so this type alias
 makes this more obvious) 
SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item1 
Item1 
SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item2 
Item2

### [SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item1](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-seriesconstruction-1.html#Item1)

SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item1 
Item1

### [SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item2](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-seriesconstruction-1.html#Item2)

SeriesConstruction<'K>.Item2 
Item2

### [LinearAddressOperations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearaddressoperations.html)

LinearAddressOperations 
 Implements address operations for linear addressing 
LinearAddressOperations.LinearAddressOperations 
LinearAddressOperations

### [LinearAddressOperations.LinearAddressOperations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearaddressoperations.html#LinearAddressOperations)

LinearAddressOperations.LinearAddressOperations 
LinearAddressOperations

### [LinearIndex<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearindex-1.html)

LinearIndex<'K> 
 An index that maps keys `K` to offsets `Address`. The keys cannot be duplicated.
 The construction checks if the keys are ordered (using the provided or the default
 comparer for `K`) and disallows certain operations on unordered indices.

### [LinearIndexBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearindexbuilder.html)

LinearIndexBuilder 
 Index builder object that is associated with `LinearIndex<K>` type. The builder
 provides operations for manipulating linear indices (and the associated vectors). 
LinearIndexBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
LinearIndexBuilder.Instance 
Instance

### [LinearIndexBuilder.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearindexbuilder.html#``.ctor``)

LinearIndexBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [LinearIndexBuilder.Instance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearindexbuilder.html#Instance)

LinearIndexBuilder.Instance 
Instance 
 Provides a global access to an instance of LinearIndexBuilder

### [LinearRangeIndex<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-linear-linearrangeindex-1.html)

LinearRangeIndex<'K> 
 A virtual index that represents a subrange of a specified index. This is useful for
 windowing operations where we do not want to allocate a new index for each window.
 This index can be cheaply constructed and it implements many of the standard functions
 without actually allocating the index (e.g. KeyCount, KeyAt, IsEmpty). For more
 complex index manipulations (including lookup), an actual index is constructed lazily.

### [VirtualIndexBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualindexbuilder.html)

VirtualIndexBuilder 
 Implements `IIndexBuilder` interface for BigDeedle. This directly implements operations that can
 be implemented on virtual vectors (mostly merging, slicing) and for other operations, it calls
 ordinary `LinearIndexBuilder`. The resulting `VectorConstruction` corresponds to the addressing
 scheme of the returned index (i.e. if we return virtual, we expect to build virtual vector; if
 we materialize, the vector builder also has to materialize). 
VirtualIndexBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
VirtualIndexBuilder.Instance 
Instance

### [VirtualIndexBuilder.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualindexbuilder.html#``.ctor``)

VirtualIndexBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [VirtualIndexBuilder.Instance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualindexbuilder.html#Instance)

VirtualIndexBuilder.Instance 
Instance 
 Returns an instance of the virtual index builder

### [VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualorderedindex-1.html)

VirtualOrderedIndex<'K> 
 Represents an ordered index based on data provided by a virtual source.
 The index can be used by BigDeedle virtual frames and series, without accessing
 all data from the data source.

 The index only evaluates the full key collection when needed. Most of the actual
 work is delegated to the `IVirtualVectorSource<'K>` value passed in the constructor. 
VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.Source 
Source

### [VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualorderedindex-1.html#``.ctor``)

VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.Source](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualorderedindex-1.html#Source)

VirtualOrderedIndex<'K>.Source 
Source 
 Returns the underlying source associated with the index

### [VirtualOrdinalIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualordinalindex.html)

VirtualOrdinalIndex 
 Represents an ordinal index based on addressing provided by a virtual source.
 The index can be used by BigDeedle virtual frames and series, without accessing
 all data from the data source. 
VirtualOrdinalIndex.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
VirtualOrdinalIndex.Ranges 
Ranges 
VirtualOrdinalIndex.Source 
Source

### [VirtualOrdinalIndex.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualordinalindex.html#``.ctor``)

VirtualOrdinalIndex.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [VirtualOrdinalIndex.Ranges](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualordinalindex.html#Ranges)

VirtualOrdinalIndex.Ranges 
Ranges 
 Returns the ranges of data mapped in this index

### [VirtualOrdinalIndex.Source](/Deedle/reference/deedle-indices-virtual-virtualordinalindex.html#Source)

VirtualOrdinalIndex.Source 
Source 
 Returns the source that is used to identify the index.

### [AddressingExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-addressingextensions.html)

AddressingExtensions 
 
AddressingExtensions.AddressRangeExtensions 
AddressRangeExtensions

### [AddressRangeExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-addressingextensions-addressrangeextensions.html)

AddressRangeExtensions 
 
AddressRangeExtensions.AsAbsolute 
AsAbsolute

### [AddressRangeExtensions.AsAbsolute](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-addressingextensions-addressrangeextensions.html#AsAbsolute)

AddressRangeExtensions.AsAbsolute 
AsAbsolute 
 When the address represents an absolute offset, this can be used to turn 'Start'
 and 'End' restrictions into the usual 'Fixed' restriction. The result is a choice
 with either new absolute range or custom (sequence of addresses)

### [Array](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-array.html)

Array 
 This module contains additional functions for working with arrays.
 `Deedle.Internals` is opened, it extends the standard `Array` module. 
Array.dropRange 
dropRange 
Array.binarySearchNearestGreater 
binarySearchNearestGreater 
Array.binarySearchNearestSmaller 
binarySearchNearestSmaller 
Array.choosei 
choosei

### [Array.dropRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-array.html#dropRange)

Array.dropRange 
dropRange 
 Drop a specified range from a given array. The operation is inclusive on
 both sides. Given [ 1; 2; 3; 4 ] and indices (1, 2), the result is [ 1; 4 ]

### [Array.binarySearchNearestGreater](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-array.html#binarySearchNearestGreater)

Array.binarySearchNearestGreater 
binarySearchNearestGreater 
 Returns the index of 'key' or the index of immediately following value.
 If the specified key is greater than all keys in the array, None is returned.

 When 'inclusive' is false, the function returns the index of strictry greater value.
 Note that the function expects that the array contains distinct values
 (which is fine because LinearIndex does not support duplicate keys)

### [Array.binarySearchNearestSmaller](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-array.html#binarySearchNearestSmaller)

Array.binarySearchNearestSmaller 
binarySearchNearestSmaller 
 Returns the index of 'key' or the index of immediately preceeding value.
 If the specified key is smaller than all keys in the array, None is returned.

 When 'inclusive' is false, the function returns the index of strictry smaller value.
 Note that the function expects that the array contains distinct values
 (which is fine because LinearIndex does not support duplicate keys)

### [Array.choosei](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-array.html#choosei)

Array.choosei 
choosei 
 Returns a new array containing only the elements for which the specified function returns `Some`.
 The predicate is called with the index in the source array and the element.

### [BinomialHeap](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html)

BinomialHeap 

 Module with functions for working with binomial heaps
  
BinomialHeap.empty 
empty 
BinomialHeap.emptyCustom 
emptyCustom 
BinomialHeap.isEmpty 
isEmpty 
BinomialHeap.insertTree 
insertTree 
BinomialHeap.insert 
insert 
BinomialHeap.mergeTrees 
mergeTrees 
BinomialHeap.merge 
merge 
BinomialHeap.findMin 
findMin 
BinomialHeap.removeMin 
removeMin 
BinomialHeap.deleteMin 
deleteMin 
BinomialHeap.(|LT|GT|EQ|) 
(|LT|GT|EQ|)

### [BinomialHeap.empty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#empty)

BinomialHeap.empty 
empty 
 Creates an empty heap that uses the default .NET comparer

### [BinomialHeap.emptyCustom](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#emptyCustom)

BinomialHeap.emptyCustom 
emptyCustom 
 Creates an empty heap using the specified comparer

### [BinomialHeap.isEmpty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#isEmpty)

BinomialHeap.isEmpty 
isEmpty 
 Returns true when the specified heap is emtpy

### [BinomialHeap.insertTree](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#insertTree)

BinomialHeap.insertTree 
insertTree 
 Inser the specified tree into the list of trees forming binomial heap.
 If the rank of the inserted tree is higher than the rank of the head,
 then join the trees and add the resulting tree.

### [BinomialHeap.insert](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#insert)

BinomialHeap.insert 
insert 
 Insert the specified element to the heap

### [BinomialHeap.mergeTrees](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#mergeTrees)

BinomialHeap.mergeTrees 
mergeTrees 
 Merge two lists of ranked trees, producing a new list.

### [BinomialHeap.merge](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#merge)

BinomialHeap.merge 
merge 
 Merge two binomial heaps (using the comparer of the first one)

### [BinomialHeap.findMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#findMin)

BinomialHeap.findMin 
findMin 
 Find minimal value from the specified binomial heap

### [BinomialHeap.removeMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#removeMin)

BinomialHeap.removeMin 
removeMin 
 Remove minimal value from the specified binomial heap
 (and return the value, together with a new heap)

### [BinomialHeap.deleteMin](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#deleteMin)

BinomialHeap.deleteMin 
deleteMin 
 Remove minimal value from the specified binomial heap
 (and return the new heap)

### [BinomialHeap.(|LT|GT|EQ|)](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap.html#(|LT|GT|EQ|))

BinomialHeap.(|LT|GT|EQ|) 
(|LT|GT|EQ|) 
 Active pattern that makes it easier to deal with results from IComparer

### [Convert](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-convert.html)

Convert 
 
Convert.convertType 
convertType 
Convert.canConvertType 
canConvertType

### [Convert.convertType](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-convert.html#convertType)

Convert.convertType 
convertType 
 Helper function that converts value to a specified type. The conversion
 is done using the specified conversion kind, which specifies the level
 of flexibility (Exact - cast, Safe - according to 'sourcesByTarget',
 Flexible - anything that System.Convert allows)

### [Convert.canConvertType](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-convert.html#canConvertType)

Convert.canConvertType 
canConvertType 
 A function that returns `true` when `convertType<'T>` is expected
 to succeed on the specified value (this is approximation - it may
 return 'true' even if the conversion fails, but not the other way round)

### [DynamicExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-dynamicextensions.html)

DynamicExtensions 

 Module that implements various helpers for supporting C# dynamic type.
 (this takes care of some of the complexity around building `DynamicMetaObject`
 and it is used by `SeriesBuilder` and `Frame`)
 
DynamicExtensions.GetterWrapper<'T> 
GetterWrapper<'T> 
DynamicExtensions.SetterWrapper<'T> 
SetterWrapper<'T> 
DynamicExtensions.WrappedExpression 
WrappedExpression 
DynamicExtensions.createPropertyMetaObject 
createPropertyMetaObject 
DynamicExtensions.createGetterAndSetterFromFunc 
createGetterAndSetterFromFunc 
DynamicExtensions.Wrap 
Wrap 
DynamicExtensions.Wrap 
Wrap 
DynamicExtensions.AsExpression 
AsExpression

### [DynamicExtensions.createPropertyMetaObject](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-dynamicextensions.html#createPropertyMetaObject)

DynamicExtensions.createPropertyMetaObject 
createPropertyMetaObject 
 This can be used when getter/setter are generic (in some way) - the caller
 is responsible for generating the right expression tree (this cannot easily
 be done using quotations)

### [DynamicExtensions.createGetterAndSetterFromFunc](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-dynamicextensions.html#createGetterAndSetterFromFunc)

DynamicExtensions.createGetterAndSetterFromFunc 
createGetterAndSetterFromFunc 
 This can be used when the setter is a simple non-generic function that
 takes the name as string & argument as object (and returns nothing)

### [DynamicExtensions.Wrap](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-dynamicextensions.html#Wrap)

DynamicExtensions.Wrap 
Wrap

### [DynamicExtensions.Wrap](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-dynamicextensions.html#Wrap)

DynamicExtensions.Wrap

### [Formatting](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html)

Formatting 

 Contains helper functions and configuration constants for pretty printing
 
Formatting.RowStartItemCount

Formatting.RowEndItemCount

Formatting.ColumnEndItemCount

StartInlineItemCount 
Formatting.EndInlineItemCount 
EndInlineItemCount 
Formatting.MaxCellWidth 
MaxCellWidth 
Formatting.formatKey 
formatKey 
Formatting.formatTable 
formatTable

### [Formatting.RowStartItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#RowStartItemCount)

Formatting.RowStartItemCount 
RowStartItemCount 
 Maximal number of rows to be printed at the beginning of a series/frame

### [Formatting.RowEndItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#RowEndItemCount)

Formatting.RowEndItemCount 
RowEndItemCount 
 Maximal number of rows to be printed at the end of a series/frame

### [Formatting.ColumnStartItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#ColumnStartItemCount)

Formatting.ColumnStartItemCount 
ColumnStartItemCount 
 Maximal number of columns to be printed at the beginning of a series/frame

### [Formatting.ColumnEndItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#ColumnEndItemCount)

Formatting.ColumnEndItemCount 
ColumnEndItemCount 
 Maximal number of columns to be printed at the end of a series/frame

### [Formatting.StartInlineItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#StartInlineItemCount)

Formatting.StartInlineItemCount 
StartInlineItemCount 
 Maximal number of items to be printed at the beginning of an inline formatted series/frame

### [Formatting.EndInlineItemCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#EndInlineItemCount)

Formatting.EndInlineItemCount 
EndInlineItemCount 
 Maximal number of items to be printed at the end of an inline formatted series/frame

### [Formatting.MaxCellWidth](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#MaxCellWidth)

Formatting.MaxCellWidth 
MaxCellWidth 
 Maximum width (in characters) of a single cell value in FSI/ToString output.
 Values longer than this are truncated with "..." to keep columns readable.

### [Formatting.formatKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#formatKey)

Formatting.formatKey 
formatKey 
 Format a key value for display. DateTime keys with no time component (i.e. time = 00:00:00)
 are formatted as date-only using the current culture's short date format, matching the
 behaviour of SaveCsv. DateTime keys with a time component are formatted as
 "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss" using the invariant culture to ensure consistent output
 across locales (e.g. avoiding AM/PM suffixes on Windows). All other values use ToString().

### [Formatting.formatTable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-formatting.html#formatTable)

Formatting.formatTable 
formatTable

### [List](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-list.html)

List 
 This module contains additional functions for working with lists. 
List.tryChooseBy 
tryChooseBy

### [List.tryChooseBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-list.html#tryChooseBy)

List.tryChooseBy 
tryChooseBy 
 Returns an option value that is Some when the specified function 'f'
 succeeds for all values from the input list. Otherwise returns None.

### [MissingValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html)

MissingValues 
 Utility functions for identifying missing values. The `isNA` function
 can be used to test whether a value represents a missing value - this includes
 the `null` value, `Nullable<T>` value with `HasValue = false` and
 `Single.NaN` as well as `Double.NaN`.

 The functions in this module are not intended to be called directly. 
MissingValues.isNA 
isNA 
MissingValues.flattenNA 
flattenNA 
MissingValues.containsNA 
containsNA 
MissingValues.containsMissingOrNA 
containsMissingOrNA 
MissingValues.createNAArray 
createNAArray 
MissingValues.createMissingOrNAArray 
createMissingOrNAArray

### [MissingValues.isNA](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#isNA)

MissingValues.isNA 
isNA

### [MissingValues.flattenNA](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#flattenNA)

MissingValues.flattenNA 
flattenNA

### [MissingValues.containsNA](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#containsNA)

MissingValues.containsNA 
containsNA

### [MissingValues.containsMissingOrNA](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#containsMissingOrNA)

MissingValues.containsMissingOrNA 
containsMissingOrNA

### [MissingValues.createNAArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#createNAArray)

MissingValues.createNAArray 
createNAArray

### [MissingValues.createMissingOrNAArray](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-missingvalues.html#createMissingOrNAArray)

### [ReadOnlyCollection](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-readonlycollection.html)

ReadOnlyCollection 
 Provides helper functions for working with `ReadOnlyCollection<T>` similar to those
 in the `Array` module. Most importantly, F# 3.0 does not know that array implements
 `IList<T>`. 
ReadOnlyCollection.ofArray 
ofArray 
ReadOnlyCollection.ofSeq

ofArray 
 Converts an array to ReadOnlyCollection.

ReadOnlyCollection.ofSeq 
ofSeq 
 Converts a lazy sequence to fully evaluated ReadOnlyCollection

### [ReadOnlyCollection.map](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-readonlycollection.html#map)

ReadOnlyCollection.map 
map 
 Transform all elements of ReadOnlyCollection using the specified function

ReadOnlyCollection.foldOptional 
foldOptional 
 Fold elements of the ReadOnlyCollection, skipping over missing values

### [Seq](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html)

Seq 
 This module contains additional functions for working with sequences.
 `Deedle.Internals` is opened, it extends the standard `Seq` module. 
Seq.uniqueBy 
uniqueBy 
Seq.lastAndLength 
lastAndLength 
Seq.unreduce 
unreduce 
Seq.choosel 
choosel

mapl

rangeStep 
Seq.range 
range

Seq.structuralHash 
structuralHash

headOrNone 
Seq.lastFew 
lastFew 
Seq.getEnumerator 
getEnumerator

### [Seq.uniqueBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#uniqueBy)

Seq.uniqueBy 
uniqueBy 
 If the projection returns the same value for all elements, then it
 returns the value. Otherwise it throws an exception.

### [Seq.lastAndLength](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#lastAndLength)

Seq.lastAndLength 
lastAndLength 
 Returns the last element and the length of a sequence
 (using just a single iteration over the sequence)

### [Seq.unreduce](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#unreduce)

Seq.unreduce 
unreduce 
 Generate infinite sequence using the specified function.
 The initial state is returned as the first element.

### [Seq.choosel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#choosel)

Seq.choosel 
choosel 
 Same as `Seq.choose` but passes 64 bit index (l stands for long) to the function

### [Seq.mapl](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#mapl)

Seq.mapl 
mapl 
 Same as `Seq.map` but passes 64 bit index (l stands for long) to the function

### [Seq.skipAtMost](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#skipAtMost)

Seq.skipAtMost 
skipAtMost 
 Skip at most the specified number of elements. This is like
 `Seq.skip`, but it does not throw when the sequence is shorter.

### [Seq.rangeStep](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#rangeStep)

Seq.rangeStep 
rangeStep 
 A helper function that generates a sequence for the specified range of
 int or int64 values. This is notably faster than using `lo .. step .. hi`.

### [Seq.range](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#range)

Seq.range 
range 
 A helper function that generates a sequence for the specified range of
 int or int64 values. This is notably faster than using `lo .. hi`.

### [Seq.structuralEquals](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#structuralEquals)

Seq.structuralEquals 
structuralEquals 
 Comapre two sequences using the `Equals` method. Returns true
 when all their elements are equal and they have the same size.

### [Seq.structuralHash](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#structuralHash)

Seq.structuralHash 
structuralHash 
 Calculate hash code of a sequence, based on the values

### [Seq.headOrNone](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#headOrNone)

Seq.headOrNone 
headOrNone 
 If the input is non empty, returns `Some(head)` where `head` is
 the first value. Otherwise, returns `None`.

### [Seq.lastFew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#lastFew)

Seq.lastFew 
lastFew 
 Returns the specified number of elements from the end of the sequence
 Note that this needs to store the specified number of elements in memory
 and it needs to iterate over the entire sequence.

### [Seq.getEnumerator](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#getEnumerator)

Seq.getEnumerator 
getEnumerator 
 Calls the `GetEnumerator` method. Simple function to guide type inference.

### [Seq.startAndEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#startAndEnd)

Seq.startAndEnd 
startAndEnd 
 Given a sequence, returns `startCount` number of elements at the beginning
 of the sequence (wrapped in `Choice1Of3`) followed by one `Choice2Of2()` value
 and then followed by `endCount` number of elements at the end of the sequence
 wrapped in `Choice3Of3`. If the input is shorter than `startCount + endCount`,
 then all values are returned and wrapped in `Choice1Of3`.

### [Seq.windowedWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#windowedWhile)

Seq.windowedWhile 
windowedWhile 
 Generate floating windows from the input sequence. New floating window is
 started for each element. To find the end of the window, the function calls
 the provided argument `f` with the first and the last elements of the window
 as arguments. A window ends when `f` returns `false`.

### [Seq.chunkedWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#chunkedWhile)

Seq.chunkedWhile 
chunkedWhile 
 Generate non-verlapping chunks from the input sequence. A chunk is started
 at the beginning and then immediately after the end of the previous chunk.
 To find the end of the chunk, the function calls the provided argument `f`
 with the first and the last elements of the chunk as arguments. A chunk
 ends when `f` returns `false`.

### [Seq.windowedWithBounds](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#windowedWithBounds)

Seq.windowedWithBounds 
windowedWithBounds 
<pre> A version of `Seq.windowed` that allows specifying more complex boundary
 behaviour. The `boundary` argument can specify one of the following options:

  * `Boundary.Skip` - only full windows are returned (like `Seq.windowed`)
  * `Boundary.AtBeginning` - incomplete windows (smaller than the required
    size) are returned at the beginning.
  * `Boundary.AtEnding` - incomplete windows are returned at the end.

 The result is a sequence of `DataSegnebt<T>` values, which makes it
 easy to distinguish between complete and incomplete windows.</pre>

### [Seq.chunkedWithBounds](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#chunkedWithBounds)

Seq.chunkedWithBounds 
chunkedWithBounds 
 Similar to `Seq.windowedWithBounds`, but generates non-overlapping chunks
 rather than floating windows. See that function for detailed documentation.
 The function may iterate over the sequence repeatedly.

### [Seq.windowRangesWithBounds](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#windowRangesWithBounds)

Seq.windowRangesWithBounds 
windowRangesWithBounds 
<pre> Generates addresses of chunks in a collection of size 'length'. For example, consider
 a collection with 7 elements (and indices 0 .. 6) and the requirement to create chunks
 of length 4:

    0 1 2 3 4 5 6

    s
    s s
    s s s
    w w w w
      w w w w
        w w w w
          w w w w
            e e e
              e e
                e

 The windows 's' are returned when `boundary = Boundary.AtBeginning` and the windows
 'e' are returned when `boundary = Boundary.AtEnding`. The middle is returned always.
 The windows are specified by *inclusive* indices, so, e.g. the first window is returned
 as a pair (0, 0).</pre>

### [Seq.chunkRangesWithBounds](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#chunkRangesWithBounds)

Seq.chunkRangesWithBounds 
chunkRangesWithBounds 
<pre> Generates addresses of windows in a collection of size 'length'. For example, consider
 a collection with 7 elements (and indices 0 .. 6) and the requirement to create windows
 of length 3:

    0 1 2 3 4 5 6

 When the `AtEnding` flag is set for `boundary`:

    c c c
          c c c
                d

 The two chunks marked as 'c' are returned always. The incomplete chunk at the end is
 returned unless the `Skip` flag is set for `boundary`. When the `AtBeginning` flag is
 set, the incomplete chunk is (when not `Skip`) returned at the beginning:

    d
      c c c
            c c c

 The chunks are specified by *inclusive* indices, so, e.g. the first chunk in
 the second example above is returned as a pair (0, 0).</pre>

### [Seq.windowRangesWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#windowRangesWhile)

Seq.windowRangesWhile 
windowRangesWhile 
 Generate floating windows from the input sequence. New floating window is
 started for each element. To find the end of the window, the function calls
 the provided argument `f` with the first and the last elements of the window
 as arguments. A window ends when `f` returns `false`.
 The function returns the windows as pairs of their indices.

### [Seq.windowRangesWhileFromEnd](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#windowRangesWhileFromEnd)

Seq.windowRangesWhileFromEnd 
windowRangesWhileFromEnd 
 Generate overlapping floating windows from the input sequence. A window ends at
 each element of the input. To find the start of the window, the function walks
 backward from the current element and calls the provided argument `f` with the
 candidate start element and the current (end) element. The window starts at the
 earliest element for which `f` returns `true`.
 The function returns the windows as pairs of their (start, end) indices.

### [Seq.chunkRangesWhile](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#chunkRangesWhile)

Seq.chunkRangesWhile 
chunkRangesWhile 
 Generate non-verlapping chunks from the input sequence. A chunk is started
 at the beginning and then immediately after the end of the previous chunk.
 To find the end of the chunk, the function calls the provided argument `f`
 with the first and the last elements of the chunk as arguments. A chunk
 ends when `f` returns `false`.
 The function returns the chunks as pairs of their indices.

### [Seq.isSorted](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#isSorted)

Seq.isSorted 
isSorted 
 Returns true if the specified sequence is sorted.

### [Seq.tryFirstAndLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#tryFirstAndLast)

Seq.tryFirstAndLast 
tryFirstAndLast 
 Returns the first and the last element from a sequence or 'None' if the sequence is empty

### [Seq.alignOrdered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignOrdered)

Seq.alignOrdered 
alignOrdered 
 Align two ordered sequences of keys (using the specified comparer)
 The resulting relocations are returned as two-element list for symmetry with other functions
 Throws ComparisonFailedException when the comparer fails.

 When `intersectionOnly = true`, the function only adds keys & relocations
 for keys that appear in both sequences (otherwise, it performs union)

### [Seq.alignAllOrderedMany](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignAllOrderedMany)

Seq.alignAllOrderedMany 
alignAllOrderedMany 
 Align N ordered sequences of keys (using the specified comparer)
 This is the same as `alignOrdered` but for larger number of key sequences.
 Throws ComparisonFailedException when the comparer fails.
 (This performs union on the specified sequences)

### [Seq.alignAllOrderedFew](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignAllOrderedFew)

Seq.alignAllOrderedFew 
alignAllOrderedFew 
 Align N ordered sequences of keys (using the specified comparer)
 This is the same as `alignOrdered` but for larger number of key sequences.
 Throws ComparisonFailedException when the comparer fails.
 (This performs union on the specified sequences)

### [Seq.alignAllOrdered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignAllOrdered)

Seq.alignAllOrdered 
alignAllOrdered 
 Calls either `alignAllOrderedMany` or `alignAllOrderedFew` depending on the number
 of sequences to be aligned. Performance measurements suggest that 150 is the limit
 when the implementation using binomial heap is faster.

### [Seq.alignUnorderedUnion](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignUnorderedUnion)

Seq.alignUnorderedUnion 
alignUnorderedUnion 
 Align two unordered sequences of keys (performs union of the keys)
 The resulting relocations are returned as two-element list for symmetry with other functions

### [Seq.alignUnorderedIntersection](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignUnorderedIntersection)

Seq.alignUnorderedIntersection 
alignUnorderedIntersection 
 Align two unordered sequences of keys (performs intersection of the keys)
 The resulting relocations are returned as two-element list for symmetry with other functions

### [Seq.alignUnordered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignUnordered)

Seq.alignUnordered 
alignUnordered 
 Align two unordered sequences of keys. Calls either
 `alignUnorderedUnion` or `alignUnorderedIntersection`, based
 on the `intersectionOnly` parameter.

### [Seq.alignAllUnordered](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-seq.html#alignAllUnordered)

Seq.alignAllUnordered 
alignAllUnordered 
 Align N unordered sequences of keys (performs union of the keys)

### [BinomialHeap<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-binomialheap-1.html)

BinomialHeap<'T> 

 Binomial heap stores a list of trees together with custom comparer

### [ComparisonFailedException](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-comparisonfailedexception.html)

ComparisonFailedException 

 An internal exception that is used to handle the case when comparison fails
 (even though the type implements IComparable and everything...)

### [Deque<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html)

Deque<'T> 

 Mutable double ended queue that holds elements in a circular array.
 The data structure provides O(1) RemoveFirst and RemoveLast. Add is
 O(1) when the deque has enough internal capacity, otherwise it extends
 the array 2x (so amortized cost is O(1) too).
  
Deque<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Deque<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Deque<'T>.Add 
Add 
Deque<'T>.RemoveFirst 
RemoveFirst 
Deque<'T>.RemoveLast 
RemoveLast 
Deque<'T>.Item 
Item 
Deque<'T>.Last 
Last 
Deque<'T>.IsFull 
IsFull 
Deque<'T>.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 
Deque<'T>.Count 
Count 
Deque<'T>.Capacity 
Capacity 
Deque<'T>.First 
First

### [Deque<'T>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#``.ctor``)

Deque<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
 Creates a new Deque with the default capacity

### [Deque<'T>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#``.ctor``)

Deque<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Deque<'T>.Add](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#Add)

Deque<'T>.Add 
Add 
 Adds the provided item to the back of the Deque.

### [Deque<'T>.RemoveFirst](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#RemoveFirst)

Deque<'T>.RemoveFirst 
RemoveFirst 
 Removes an item from the front of the Deque and returns it.

### [Deque<'T>.RemoveLast](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#RemoveLast)

Deque<'T>.RemoveLast 
RemoveLast 
 Removes an item from the back of the Deque and returns it.

### [Deque<'T>.Item](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#Item)

Deque<'T>.Item 
Item 
 Gets the value at the specified index of the Deque

### [Deque<'T>.Last](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#Last)

Deque<'T>.Last 
Last 
 Gets the element at the end

### [Deque<'T>.IsFull](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#IsFull)

Deque<'T>.IsFull 
IsFull 
 Gets whether or not the Deque is filled to capacity.

### [Deque<'T>.IsEmpty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#IsEmpty)

Deque<'T>.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 
 Gets whether or not the Deque is empty.

### [Deque<'T>.Count](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#Count)

Deque<'T>.Count 
Count 
 Gets the number of elements contained in the Deque.

### [Deque<'T>.Capacity](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#Capacity)

Deque<'T>.Capacity 
Capacity 
 Gets the total number of elements the internal array can hold without resizing.

### [Deque<'T>.First](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-deque-1.html#First)

Deque<'T>.First 
First 
 Gets the element at the front

### [IFrameFormattable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-iframeformattable.html)

IFrameFormattable 

 An interface implemented by frames that support nice formatting for .NET interactive notebooks
 This seems to be needed as you cannot register formatters for <code>Frame<_,_></code> in .NET interactive
 (The <code>Deedle.DotNetInteractive</code> packages uses this interface for registering custom formatters.)

### [IFsiFormattable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-ifsiformattable.html)

IFsiFormattable

 (The `FSharp.DataFrame.fsx` file registers an FSI printer using this interface.)

### [ISeriesFormattable](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-iseriesformattable.html)

ISeriesFormattable 

 An interface implemented by series that support nice formatting for .NET interactive notebooks
 This seems to be needed as you cannot register formatters for <code>Series<_,_></code> in .NET interactive
 (The `Deedle.DotNetInteractive` packages uses this interface for registering custom formatters.)
 
ISeriesFormattable.GetKeyCount 
GetKeyCount 
ISeriesFormattable.GetKeyLevels

### [ISeriesFormattable.GetKeyCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-iseriesformattable.html#GetKeyCount)

### [ISeriesFormattable.GetKeyLevels](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-iseriesformattable.html#GetKeyLevels)

ISeriesFormattable.GetKeyLevels 
GetKeyLevels

ISeriesFormattable.GetValueCount 
GetValueCount

ISeriesFormattable.InteractiveFormat 
InteractiveFormat

### [RankedTree<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-rankedtree-1.html)

RankedTree<'T> 

 Tree where nodes contain values and zero or more child trees.
 For each node, we store rank - the number of children
  
RankedTree<'T>.Node 
Node

### [RankedTree<'T>.Node](/Deedle/reference/deedle-internal-rankedtree-1.html#Node)

RankedTree<'T>.Node 
Node

### [CustomKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-customkey.html)

CustomKey 
 Helper type that can be used to get `ICustomKey` for any object (including objects
 that actually implement the interface and tuples) 
CustomKey.Get 
Get 
CustomKey.GetForTuple 
GetForTuple

### [CustomKey.Get](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-customkey.html#Get)

CustomKey.Get 
Get 
 Returns `ICustomKey` instance for the specified key. If the specified key implements
 `ICustomKey`, then it is just returned; if it is a tuple, we use special key for tuples
 otherwise, it must be a primitive key so we just return it

### [CustomKey.GetForTuple](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-customkey.html#GetForTuple)

CustomKey.GetForTuple 
GetForTuple

### [ICustomKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-icustomkey.html)

ICustomKey 
 Represents a special hierarchical key. This is mainly used in pretty printing (where we want to
 get parts of the keys based on levels. `CustomKey.Get` provides a way of getting `ICustomKey`. 
ICustomKey.GetLevel 
GetLevel 
ICustomKey.GetLevels 
GetLevels 
ICustomKey.Levels 
Levels

### [ICustomKey.GetLevel](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-icustomkey.html#GetLevel)

ICustomKey.GetLevel 
GetLevel 
 Gets a value at the specified level (the levels are indexed from 1).
 This is used for pretty printing only. If `Levels=1` then this method is not
 called and the pretty printer invokes `ToString` on the whole object instead.

### [ICustomKey.GetLevels](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-icustomkey.html#GetLevels)

ICustomKey.GetLevels 
GetLevels 
 Gets values of the key at all levels

### [ICustomKey.Levels](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-icustomkey.html#Levels)

ICustomKey.Levels 
Levels 
 Returns the number of levels of a hierarchical key. For example, a tuple (1, 42) has 2 levels.
 This is used for pretty printing only.

### [SimpleLookup<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-keys-simplelookup-1.html)

SimpleLookup<'T> 
 Implements a simple lookup that matches any multi-level key against a specified array of
 optional objects (that represent missing/set parts of a key) 
SimpleLookup<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [PCA.eigenValues](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-pca.html#eigenValues)

PCA.eigenValues 
eigenValues 

 The eigen values of the PCA transformation.

### [PCA.eigenVectors](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-pca.html#eigenVectors)

PCA.eigenVectors 
eigenVectors 

 The eigen vectors of the PCA transformation.

### [PCA.normalizeColumns](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-pca.html#normalizeColumns)

PCA.normalizeColumns 
normalizeColumns 

 Normalizes the columns in the dataframe using a z-score.
 That is (X - mean) / (std. dev)

### [PCA.pca](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-pca.html#pca)

PCA.pca 
pca 

 Computes the principal components from the data frame.
 The principal components are listed from PC1 .. PCn
 Where PC1 explains most of the variance.

### [t<'a>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-pca-t-1.html)

### [Finance.ewmCorr](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCorr)

Finance.ewmCorr 
ewmCorr 
 Exponentially weighted moving correlation frame

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmCorrMatrix](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCorrMatrix)

Finance.ewmCorrMatrix 
ewmCorrMatrix 
 Exponentially weighted moving correlation matrix

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmCov](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCov)

Finance.ewmCov 
ewmCov 
 Exponentially weighted moving covariance frame

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmCovMatrix](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCovMatrix)

Finance.ewmCovMatrix 
ewmCovMatrix 
 Exponentially weighted moving covariance matrix

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmCrossCov](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCrossCov)

Finance.ewmCrossCov 
ewmCrossCov 
 Exponentially weighted moving covariance between two series (mean-corrected).
 Uses the same EWMA update rule as <c>ewmCovMatrix</c>: the EWM mean is tracked for
 each series and the cross-product of deviations from those means is accumulated.
 The two series are aligned on their shared keys; rows where either value is missing
 are dropped before computation. Initialised with the full-sample covariance.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmCrossVol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmCrossVol)

Finance.ewmCrossVol 
ewmCrossVol 
 Exponentially weighted moving cross-volatility between two series.
 Defined as the signed square root of <c>ewmCrossCov</c>:
 <c>sign(cov) * sqrt(|cov|)</c>, so that squaring recovers the magnitude of the
 covariance and the sign indicates the direction of co-movement.
 The two series are aligned on their shared keys; rows where either value is
 missing are dropped before computation.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVar](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVar)

Finance.ewmVar 
ewmVar 
 Exponentially weighted moving variance on frame

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVar](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVar)

Finance.ewmVar 
ewmVar 
 Exponentially weighted moving variance on series

### [Finance.ewmVol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVol)

Finance.ewmVol 
ewmVol 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility on frame.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVol](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVol)

Finance.ewmVol 
ewmVol 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility on series.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVolRMS](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVolRMS)

Finance.ewmVolRMS 
ewmVolRMS 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility using root mean square (no mean correction)
 applied to each column of a frame.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVolRMS](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVolRMS)

Finance.ewmVolRMS 
ewmVolRMS 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility using root mean square (no mean correction).
 Computes vol as sqrt(EWM(x²)), which equals ewmMean for strictly positive sequences.
 Appropriate for returns series that are already mean-centred (e.g. zero-mean returns).

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVolStdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVolStdDev)

Finance.ewmVolStdDev 
ewmVolStdDev 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility using standard deviation (mean-corrected)
 applied to each column of a frame.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [Finance.ewmVolStdDev](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-finance.html#ewmVolStdDev)

Finance.ewmVolStdDev 
ewmVolStdDev 
 Exponentially weighted moving volatility using standard deviation (mean-corrected).
 Tracks the EWM mean and computes volatility as the sqrt of the EWM variance of
 deviations from that mean.

 <category>Exponentially Weighted Moving</category>

### [MatrixExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-mathnetnumerics-matrixextensions.html)

MatrixExtensions
