### [Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-frame.html)

Frame 
 Static helpers that extend the <c>Frame</c> concept with ML.NET conversions.
 Open <c>Deedle.MicrosoftML</c> to bring these into scope alongside the
 standard Deedle <c>Frame</c> module.

 <category>ML.NET IDataView integration</category> 
Frame.ofDataView 
ofDataView 
Frame.toDataView 
toDataView

### [Frame.ofDataView](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-frame.html#ofDataView)

Frame.ofDataView 
ofDataView 

 Convert an ML.NET <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/idataview">IDataView</a> to a Deedle Frame with integer (0-based) row keys.
  

 Column types are mapped as:
 <ul><li><code>Double</code> → <code>float</code></li><li><code>Single</code> → <code>float32</code></li><li><code>Int32</code> → <code>int</code></li><li><code>Int64</code> → <code>int64</code></li><li><code>Boolean</code> → <code>bool</code></li><li><code>Text</code> → <code>string</code></li><li><code>Vector(Single)</code> → <code>float32 array</code> (one array per row)</li><li><code>Vector(Double)</code> → <code>float array</code> (one array per row)</li><li>Other composite types → <code>null</code></li></ul>

### [Frame.toDataView](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-frame.html#toDataView)

Frame.toDataView 
toDataView 

 Convert a Deedle Frame to an ML.NET <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/idataview">IDataView</a>.
  

 Column types are inferred from the stored values:
 <ul><li><code>float</code> columns → <code>Double</code></li><li><code>string</code> columns → <code>Text</code> (<code>ReadOnlyMemory<char></code>)</li><li><code>bool</code> columns → <code>Boolean</code></li><li><code>int32</code> / <code>int64</code> / <code>float32</code> → their ML.NET equivalents</li><li><code>float32 array</code> columns → fixed-length <code>VectorDataViewType(Single)</code></li></ul>
 Missing values become <code>NaN</code> for numeric columns and empty string for text.

### [FrameDataView](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framedataview.html)

FrameDataView 
 IDataView that wraps materialised Deedle column arrays.
 Constructed via <c>Frame.toDataView</c>. 
FrameDataView.Schema 
Schema 
FrameDataView.RowCount 
RowCount

### [FrameDataView.Schema](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framedataview.html#Schema)

FrameDataView.Schema 
Schema 
 The ML.NET schema describing all columns.

### [FrameDataView.RowCount](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framedataview.html#RowCount)

FrameDataView.RowCount 
RowCount 
 The number of rows.

### [FrameMLExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framemlextensions.html)

FrameMLExtensions 
 Extension methods on <c>Frame<'R, string></c> for ML.NET integration.

 <category>ML.NET IDataView integration</category> 
FrameMLExtensions.FitTransform 
FitTransform 
FrameMLExtensions.ToDataView 
ToDataView 
FrameMLExtensions.Transform 
Transform

### [FrameMLExtensions.FitTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framemlextensions.html#FitTransform)

FrameMLExtensions.FitTransform 
FitTransform 

 Fit an ML.NET estimator on this Frame and transform it, returning
 the result as a new <code>Frame<int, string></code>.
 Equivalent to <code>Pipeline.fitTransform estimator frame</code>.

### [FrameMLExtensions.ToDataView](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framemlextensions.html#ToDataView)

FrameMLExtensions.ToDataView 
ToDataView 

 Convert this Deedle Frame to an ML.NET <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/idataview">IDataView</a>.
 Equivalent to <code>Frame.toDataView frame</code>.

### [FrameMLExtensions.Transform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-framemlextensions.html#Transform)

FrameMLExtensions.Transform 
Transform 

 Apply an already-fitted ML.NET transformer to this Frame,
 returning the transformed result as a new <code>Frame<int, string></code>.
 Equivalent to <code>Pipeline.applyTransformer transformer frame</code>.

### [Pipeline](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-pipeline.html)

Pipeline 
 Static helpers that extend the <c>Frame</c> concept with ML.NET pipeline operations.
 Open <c>Deedle.MicrosoftML</c> to use these alongside the standard Deedle API.

 <category>ML.NET pipeline integration</category> 
Pipeline.applyTransformer 
applyTransformer 
Pipeline.fitEstimator 
fitEstimator 
Pipeline.fitTransform 
fitTransform 
Pipeline.fitTransformOn 
fitTransformOn

### [Pipeline.applyTransformer](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-pipeline.html#applyTransformer)

Pipeline.applyTransformer 
applyTransformer 

 Apply an already-fitted ML.NET <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/itransformer">ITransformer</a> to a Deedle Frame,
 returning the transformed data as a new <code>Frame<int, string></code>.

### [Pipeline.fitEstimator](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-pipeline.html#fitEstimator)

Pipeline.fitEstimator 
fitEstimator 

 Fit an ML.NET estimator on a Deedle Frame and return the fitted model
 without transforming.

### [Pipeline.fitTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-pipeline.html#fitTransform)

Pipeline.fitTransform 
fitTransform 

 Fit an ML.NET estimator on a Deedle Frame and immediately apply the
 resulting transform, returning the output as a new <code>Frame<int, string></code>.

### [Pipeline.fitTransformOn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-microsoftml-pipeline.html#fitTransformOn)

Pipeline.fitTransformOn 
fitTransformOn 

 Fit an estimator on a training frame, then apply it to a separate
 prediction/test frame and return the result.

### [Frame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html)

Frame 

 Parquet-specific functions on Deedle <code>Frame</code> values.
 Open <code>Deedle.Parquet</code> and then call these as <code>Frame.readParquet</code>,
 <code>Frame.writeParquet</code>, etc.
  
Frame.readParquet 
readParquet 
Frame.writeParquet 
writeParquet 
Frame.readParquetStream 
readParquetStream 
Frame.writeParquetStream 
writeParquetStream 
Frame.writeParquetWithIndex 
writeParquetWithIndex 
Frame.readParquetWithIndex 
readParquetWithIndex

### [Frame.readParquet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#readParquet)

Frame.readParquet 
readParquet 

 Read a Parquet file into a Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 Row keys are 0-based integers. Parquet null values become Deedle missing values.
 Files with multiple row groups are concatenated into a single frame.

### [Frame.writeParquet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#writeParquet)

Frame.writeParquet 
writeParquet 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to a Parquet file.

### [Frame.readParquetStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#readParquetStream)

Frame.readParquetStream 
readParquetStream 

 Read a Parquet stream into a Deedle <code>Frame<int,string></code>.
 The stream must be seekable. All row groups are concatenated into a single frame.

### [Frame.writeParquetStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#writeParquetStream)

Frame.writeParquetStream 
writeParquetStream 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to a Parquet stream.
 The stream must be seekable. The stream is left open after writing.

### [Frame.writeParquetWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#writeParquetWithIndex)

Frame.writeParquetWithIndex 
writeParquetWithIndex 

 Write a Deedle <code>Frame</code> to a Parquet file, storing row keys in a special
 <code>__index__</code> column so they can be restored on read.
 Row keys are serialised via <code>ToString()</code>.

### [Frame.readParquetWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frame.html#readParquetWithIndex)

Frame.readParquetWithIndex 
readParquetWithIndex 

 Read a Parquet file that was written with <code>Frame.writeParquetWithIndex</code>,
 restoring the original string row keys.
 If no <code>__index__</code> column is present, returns a frame with 0-based integer
 row keys converted to strings.

### [FrameParquetExtensions](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frameparquetextensions.html)

FrameParquetExtensions 

 C# extension methods on <code>Frame<'R, string></code> for Parquet I/O.
  
FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquet 
WriteParquet 
FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetStream 
WriteParquetStream 
FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetWithIndex 
WriteParquetWithIndex

### [FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frameparquetextensions.html#WriteParquet)

FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquet 
WriteParquet 
Write this frame to a Parquet file.

### [FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frameparquetextensions.html#WriteParquetStream)

FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetStream 
WriteParquetStream 
Write this frame to a Parquet stream.

### [FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-frameparquetextensions.html#WriteParquetWithIndex)

FrameParquetExtensions.WriteParquetWithIndex 
WriteParquetWithIndex 

 Write this frame to a Parquet file, preserving row keys in
 an <code>__index__</code> column for round-tripping.

### [ParquetFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-parquetframe.html)

ParquetFrame 

 Static factory methods for reading Parquet files, accessible from C# as
 <code>ParquetFrame.ReadParquet(path)</code> etc.
  
ParquetFrame.ReadParquet 
ReadParquet 
ParquetFrame.ReadParquetStream 
ReadParquetStream 
ParquetFrame.ReadParquetWithIndex 
ReadParquetWithIndex

### [ParquetFrame.ReadParquet](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-parquetframe.html#ReadParquet)

ParquetFrame.ReadParquet 
ReadParquet 
Read a Parquet file into a <code>Frame<int,string></code>.

### [ParquetFrame.ReadParquetStream](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-parquetframe.html#ReadParquetStream)

ParquetFrame.ReadParquetStream 
ReadParquetStream 
Read a Parquet stream into a <code>Frame<int,string></code>.

### [ParquetFrame.ReadParquetWithIndex](/Deedle/reference/deedle-parquet-parquetframe.html#ReadParquetWithIndex)

ParquetFrame.ReadParquetWithIndex 
ReadParquetWithIndex 

 Read a Parquet file written with <code>WriteParquetWithIndex</code>,
 restoring string row keys.

### [Ranges](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html)

Ranges 
<pre> Provides F# functions for working with the `Ranges<'T>` type. Note that
 most of the functions are also exposed as members. The terminology in the
 functions below is:

  - **offset** refers to an absolute `int64` offset of a key in the range
  - **key** refers to a key value of type `'T`

 Say, you have daily range `[ (2015-01-01, 2015-01-10); (2015-02-01, 2015-02-10) ]`.
 Then, the keys are the dates and the offsets are 0 .. 9 for the first part and
 10 .. 19 for the second part.</pre> 
Ranges.inlineCreate 
inlineCreate 
Ranges.create 
create 
Ranges.combine 
combine 
Ranges.invalid 
invalid 
Ranges.keyAtOffset 
keyAtOffset 
Ranges.offsetOfKey 
offsetOfKey 
Ranges.restrictRanges 
restrictRanges 
Ranges.keyRange 
keyRange 
Ranges.keys 
keys 
Ranges.length 
length 
Ranges.lookup 
lookup

### [Ranges.inlineCreate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#inlineCreate)

Ranges.inlineCreate 
inlineCreate 
 Create a range from a sequence of low-high pairs
 (range key operations used standard arithmetic)

### [Ranges.create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#create)

Ranges.create 
create 
 Create a range from a sequence of low-high pairs
 and an explicitly specified range key operations

### [Ranges.combine](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#combine)

Ranges.combine 
combine 
 Combine ranges - the arguments don't have to be sorted, but must not overlap
 (they can be aligned, in which case ranges are merged)

### [Ranges.invalid](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#invalid)

Ranges.invalid 
invalid 
 Represents invalid offset

### [Ranges.keyAtOffset](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#keyAtOffset)

Ranges.keyAtOffset 
keyAtOffset 
<pre> Returns the key at a given offset. For example, given
 ranges [10..12; 30..32], the function defines a mapping:

   0->10, 1->11, 2->12, 3->30, 4->31, 5->32

 When the offset is wrong, throws `IndexOutOfRangeException`</pre>

### [Ranges.offsetOfKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#offsetOfKey)

Ranges.offsetOfKey 
offsetOfKey 
<pre> Returns the offset of a given key. For example, given
 ranges [10..12; 30..32], the function defines a mapping:

   10->0, 11->1, 12->2, 30->3, 31->4, 32->5

 When the key is wrong, returns `Ranges.invalid`</pre>

### [Ranges.restrictRanges](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#restrictRanges)

Ranges.restrictRanges 
restrictRanges 
 Restricts the ranges according to the specified range restriction

### [Ranges.keyRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#keyRange)

Ranges.keyRange 
keyRange 
 Returns the smallest & greatest overall key

### [Ranges.keys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#keys)

Ranges.keys 
keys 
 Returns a lazy sequence containing all keys

### [Ranges.length](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#length)

Ranges.length 
length 
 Returns the length of the ranges

### [Ranges.lookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges.html#lookup)

Ranges.lookup 
lookup 
 Implements a lookup using the specified semantics & check function.
 For `Exact` match, this is the same as `offsetOfKey`. In other cases,
 we first find the place where the key *would* be and then scan in one
 or the other direction until 'check' returns 'true' or we find the end.

 Returns the key together with its offset in the range.

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey> 
<pre> A set of operations on keys that you need to implement in order to use the
 `Ranges<'TKey>` type. The `'TKey` type is typically the key of a BigDeedle
 series. It can represent different things, such as:

  - `int64` - if you have ordinally indexed series
  - `Date` (of some sort) - if you have daily time series
  - `DateTimeOffset` - if you have time series with DTO keys

 The operations need to implement the *right* thing based on the logic of the
 keys. So for example if you have one data point every hour, `IncrementBy` should
 add the appropriate number of hours. Or if you have keys as business days, the
 `IncrementBy` operation should add a number of business days (that is, the
 operations may be simple numerical addition, but may contain more logic).</pre> 
IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Compare 
Compare 
IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Distance 
Distance 
IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.IncrementBy 
IncrementBy 
IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Range 
Range 
IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.ValidateKey 
ValidateKey

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Compare](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html#Compare)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Compare 
Compare 
 Compare two keys. Return +1 if first is larger, -1 if second is larger, 0 otherwise

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Distance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html#Distance)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Distance 
Distance 
 Return distance between two keys - return 0 if the keys are the same
 (the second key is always larger than the first)

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.IncrementBy](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html#IncrementBy)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.IncrementBy 
IncrementBy 
 Get the n-th next key after the specified key (n is always non-negative)

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Range](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html#Range)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.Range 
Range 
 Generate keys within the specific range (inclusively). Here, the second
 key _can_ be smaller (in which case the range should be from larger to smaller)

### [IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.ValidateKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-irangekeyoperations-1.html#ValidateKey)

IRangeKeyOperations<'TKey>.ValidateKey 
ValidateKey 
 Find the first valid key around the specified `'TKey` value. This is used
 when not all `'TKey` values can appear as keys of the series. If lookup is
 `Exact`, it should just check validity; for other lookups, this should either
 find first smaller or first larger valid key (but not skip any keys).

 This is only called with `lookup = Lookup.Exact`, unless you are also using
 `Ranges.lookup` function inside `IVirtualVectorSource<'T>.LookupValue`.

### [Ranges<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html)

Ranges<'T> 
 Represents a sub-range of an ordinal index. The range can consist of
 multiple blocks, i.e. [ 0..9; 20..29 ]. The pairs represent indices
 of first and last element (inclusively) and we also keep size so that
 we do not have to recalculate it.

 For more information, see also the documentation for the `Ranges` module. 
Ranges<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
Ranges<'T>.KeyAtOffset 
KeyAtOffset 
Ranges<'T>.Lookup 
Lookup 
Ranges<'T>.MergeWith 
MergeWith 
Ranges<'T>.OffsetOfKey 
OffsetOfKey 
Ranges<'T>.Restrict 
Restrict 
Ranges<'T>.Operations 
Operations 
Ranges<'T>.Keys 
Keys 
Ranges<'T>.FirstKey 
FirstKey 
Ranges<'T>.Ranges 
Ranges 
Ranges<'T>.LastKey 
LastKey 
Ranges<'T>.Length 
Length

### [Ranges<'T>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#``.ctor``)

Ranges<'T>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Ranges<'T>.KeyAtOffset](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#KeyAtOffset)

Ranges<'T>.KeyAtOffset 
KeyAtOffset 
 Returns the key at the specified offset

### [Ranges<'T>.Lookup](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Lookup)

Ranges<'T>.Lookup 
Lookup 
 Searches for a key in the range (see `Ranges.lookup` for more info)

### [Ranges<'T>.MergeWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#MergeWith)

Ranges<'T>.MergeWith 
MergeWith 
 Merge the current range with other specified ranges

### [Ranges<'T>.OffsetOfKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#OffsetOfKey)

Ranges<'T>.OffsetOfKey 
OffsetOfKey 
 Returns the absolute offset of the key in the ranges

### [Ranges<'T>.Restrict](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Restrict)

Ranges<'T>.Restrict 
Restrict 
 Restricts the ranges according to the specified range restriction

### [Ranges<'T>.Operations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Operations)

Ranges<'T>.Operations 
Operations

### [Ranges<'T>.Keys](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Keys)

Ranges<'T>.Keys 
Keys 
 Returns a lazy sequence containing all keys

### [Ranges<'T>.FirstKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#FirstKey)

Ranges<'T>.FirstKey 
FirstKey 
 Returns the first key in the range

### [Ranges<'T>.Ranges](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Ranges)

Ranges<'T>.Ranges 
Ranges

### [Ranges<'T>.LastKey](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#LastKey)

Ranges<'T>.LastKey 
LastKey 
 Returns the last key in the range

### [Ranges<'T>.Length](/Deedle/reference/deedle-ranges-ranges-1.html#Length)

Ranges<'T>.Length 
Length 
 Returns the length of the ranges

### [IBinaryTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ibinarytransform.html)

IBinaryTransform 
 Represent a transformation that is applied when combining two vectors (because
 we are combining untyped `IVector` values, the transformation is also untyped) 
IBinaryTransform.GetFunction 
GetFunction 
IBinaryTransform.IsMissingUnit 
IsMissingUnit

### [IBinaryTransform.GetFunction](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ibinarytransform.html#GetFunction)

IBinaryTransform.GetFunction 
GetFunction 
 Returns a function that combines two values stored in vectors into a new vector value.
 Although generic, this function will only be called with the `T` set to the
 type of vector that is being built. Since `VectorConstruction` is not generic,
 the type cannot be statically propagated.

### [IBinaryTransform.IsMissingUnit](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ibinarytransform.html#IsMissingUnit)

IBinaryTransform.IsMissingUnit 
IsMissingUnit 
 Assuming `*` is the result of `GetFunction`, this property returns true when
 for all `x` it is the case that `Missing * x = x = x * Missing`. This enables
 certain optimizations (as we do not have to call `*` when one argument is N/A)

### [INaryTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-inarytransform.html)

INaryTransform 
 Represent a tranformation that is applied when combining N vectors
 (This follows exactly the same pattern as `IBinaryTransform`) 
INaryTransform.GetFunction 
GetFunction

### [INaryTransform.GetFunction](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-inarytransform.html#GetFunction)

INaryTransform.GetFunction 
GetFunction 
 Returns a function that combines N values stored in vectors into a new vector value

### [IRowReaderTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-irowreadertransform.html)

IRowReaderTransform 
 When an `INaryTransform` implements this interface, it is a special well-known
 transformation that creates a _row reader_ vector to be used in `frame.Rows`.
 (See the implementation in the `Build` operation in `ArrayVector.fs`) 
IRowReaderTransform.ColumnAddressAt 
ColumnAddressAt

### [IRowReaderTransform.ColumnAddressAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-irowreadertransform.html#ColumnAddressAt)

IRowReaderTransform.ColumnAddressAt 
ColumnAddressAt

### [IVectorBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ivectorbuilder.html)

IVectorBuilder 
 Represents an object that can construct vector values by processing
 the "mini-DSL" representation `VectorConstruction`. 
IVectorBuilder.AsyncBuild 
AsyncBuild 
IVectorBuilder.Build 
Build 
IVectorBuilder.Create 
Create 
IVectorBuilder.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing

### [IVectorBuilder.AsyncBuild](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ivectorbuilder.html#AsyncBuild)

IVectorBuilder.AsyncBuild 
AsyncBuild 
 Asynchronous version of `Build` operation. This is mainly used for
 `AsyncMaterialize` and it does not handle fully general vector constructions (yet)

### [IVectorBuilder.Build](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ivectorbuilder.html#Build)

IVectorBuilder.Build 
Build 
 Apply a vector construction to a given vector. The second parameter
 is an array of arguments ("variables") that may be referenced from the
 `VectorConstruction` using the `Return 0` construct.

### [IVectorBuilder.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ivectorbuilder.html#Create)

IVectorBuilder.Create 
Create 
 Create a vector from an array containing values. The values may
 still represent missing values and the vector should handle this.
 For example `Double.NaN` or `null` should be turned into a missing
 value in the returned vector.

### [IVectorBuilder.CreateMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-ivectorbuilder.html#CreateMissing)

IVectorBuilder.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing 
 Create a vector from an array containing values that may be missing.
 Even if a value is passed, it may be a missing value such as `Double.NaN`
 or `null`. The vector builder should hanlde this.

### [KnownLocation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-knownlocation.html)

KnownLocation 
 An `IVectorLocation` created from a known address and offset
 (typically used in LinearIndex/ArrayVector where both are the same) 
KnownLocation.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [KnownLocation.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-knownlocation.html#``.ctor``)

KnownLocation.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [Vector](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vector.html)

Vector 
 Type that provides access to creating vectors (represented as arrays) 
Vector.Create 
Create 
Vector.Create 
Create 
Vector.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing 
Vector.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing

### [Vector.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vector.html#Create)

Vector.Create 
Create 
 Creates a vector that stores the specified data in an array.
 Values such as `null` and `Double.NaN` are turned into missing values.

### [Vector.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vector.html#Create)

Vector.Create 
Create 
 Creates a vector that stores the specified data in an array.
 Values such as `null` and `Double.NaN` are turned into missing values.

### [Vector.CreateMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vector.html#CreateMissing)

Vector.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing 
 Creates a vector that stores the specified data in an array.
 Values such as `null` and `Double.NaN` are turned into missing values.

### [Vector.CreateMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vector.html#CreateMissing)

Vector.CreateMissing 
CreateMissing 
 Creates a vector that stores the specified data in an array.
 Values such as `null` and `Double.NaN` are turned into missing values.

### [VectorConstruction](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html)

VectorConstruction 
 A "mini-DSL" that describes construction of a vector. Vector can be constructed
 from various range operations (relocate, drop, slicing, appending), by combination
 of two vectors or by taking a vector from a list of variables.

 Notably, vectors can only be constructed from other vectors of the same type
 (the `Combine` operation requires this - even though that one could be made more general).
 This is an intentional choice to make the representation simpler.

 Logically, when we apply some index operation, we should get back a polymorphic vector
 construction (`\forall T. VectorConstruction<T>`) that can be applied to variuous
 different vector types. That would mean adding some more types, so we just model vector
 construction as an untyped operation and the typing is resquired by the `Build` method
 of the vector builder. 
VectorConstruction.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty 
VectorConstruction.IsFillMissing 
IsFillMissing 
VectorConstruction.IsCombine 
IsCombine 
VectorConstruction.IsRelocate 
IsRelocate 
VectorConstruction.IsAsyncCustomCommand 
IsAsyncCustomCommand 
VectorConstruction.IsGetRange 
IsGetRange 
VectorConstruction.IsDropRange 
IsDropRange 
VectorConstruction.IsReturn 
IsReturn 
VectorConstruction.IsAppend 
IsAppend 
VectorConstruction.IsCustomCommand 
IsCustomCommand 
VectorConstruction.Return 
Return 
VectorConstruction.Empty 
Empty 
VectorConstruction.Relocate 
Relocate 
VectorConstruction.DropRange 
DropRange 
VectorConstruction.GetRange 
GetRange 
VectorConstruction.Append 
Append 
VectorConstruction.Combine 
Combine 
VectorConstruction.FillMissing 
FillMissing 
VectorConstruction.CustomCommand 
CustomCommand 
VectorConstruction.AsyncCustomCommand 
AsyncCustomCommand

### [VectorConstruction.IsEmpty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsEmpty)

VectorConstruction.IsEmpty 
IsEmpty

### [VectorConstruction.IsFillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsFillMissing)

VectorConstruction.IsFillMissing 
IsFillMissing

### [VectorConstruction.IsCombine](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsCombine)

VectorConstruction.IsCombine 
IsCombine

### [VectorConstruction.IsRelocate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsRelocate)

VectorConstruction.IsRelocate 
IsRelocate

### [VectorConstruction.IsAsyncCustomCommand](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsAsyncCustomCommand)

VectorConstruction.IsAsyncCustomCommand 
IsAsyncCustomCommand

### [VectorConstruction.IsGetRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsGetRange)

VectorConstruction.IsGetRange 
IsGetRange

### [VectorConstruction.IsDropRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsDropRange)

VectorConstruction.IsDropRange 
IsDropRange

### [VectorConstruction.IsReturn](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsReturn)

VectorConstruction.IsReturn 
IsReturn

### [VectorConstruction.IsAppend](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsAppend)

VectorConstruction.IsAppend 
IsAppend

### [VectorConstruction.IsCustomCommand](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#IsCustomCommand)

VectorConstruction.IsCustomCommand 
IsCustomCommand

### [VectorConstruction.Return](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#Return)

VectorConstruction.Return 
Return 
 When constructing vectors, we get an array of vectors to be used as "variables"
 - this element represent getting one of the variables.

### [VectorConstruction.Empty](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#Empty)

VectorConstruction.Empty 
Empty 
 Creates an empty vector of the requested type and size
 The returned vector is filled with missing values.

### [VectorConstruction.Relocate](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#Relocate)

VectorConstruction.Relocate 
Relocate 
 Reorders elements of the vector. Carries a new required vector length and a list
 of relocations (each pair of addresses specifies that an element at a new address
 should be filled with an element from an old address). The addresses may be out of
 range!

### [VectorConstruction.DropRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#DropRange)

VectorConstruction.DropRange 
DropRange 
 Drop the specified range of addresses from the vector
 and return a new vector that excludes the range

### [VectorConstruction.GetRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#GetRange)

VectorConstruction.GetRange 
GetRange 
 Get the specified range of addresses from the vector and return it as a new vector

### [VectorConstruction.Append](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#Append)

VectorConstruction.Append 
Append 
 Append two vectors after each other

### [VectorConstruction.Combine](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#Combine)

VectorConstruction.Combine 
Combine 
 Combine N aligned vectors. The `IVectorValueListTransform` object
 specifies how to merge values (in case there is a value at a given address
 in more than one of the vectors).

### [VectorConstruction.FillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#FillMissing)

VectorConstruction.FillMissing 
FillMissing 
 Create a vector that has missing values filled using the specified direction
 (forward means that n-th value will contain (n-i)-th value where (n-i) is the
 first index that contains a value).

### [VectorConstruction.CustomCommand](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#CustomCommand)

VectorConstruction.CustomCommand 
CustomCommand 
 Apply a custom command to a vector - this can be used by special indices (e.g. index
 for a lazy vector) to provide a custom operations to be used. The first parameter
 is a list of sub-vectors to be combined (if as in e.g. `Append`) and the
 second argument is a function that will be called with evaluated vectors and is
 supposed to create the new vector.

### [VectorConstruction.AsyncCustomCommand](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorconstruction.html#AsyncCustomCommand)

VectorConstruction.AsyncCustomCommand 
AsyncCustomCommand 
 Same as `CustomCommand` with the difference that the resulting vector is returned
 asynchronously (this is useful for lazy loading and it is used by `AsyncBuild`).

### [VectorData<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html)

VectorData<'T> 
 Provides a way to get the data of an arbitrary vector. This is a concrete type used
 by functions that operate on vectors (like `Series.sum`, etc.). The vector may choose
 to return the data as `ReadOnlyCollection` (with or without N/A values) which is more
 efficient to use or as a lazy sequence (slower, but more general). 
VectorData<'T>.IsSparseList 
IsSparseList 
VectorData<'T>.IsDenseList 
IsDenseList 
VectorData<'T>.IsSequence 
IsSequence 
VectorData<'T>.DenseList 
DenseList 
VectorData<'T>.SparseList 
SparseList 
VectorData<'T>.Sequence 
Sequence

### [VectorData<'T>.IsSparseList](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html#IsSparseList)

VectorData<'T>.IsSparseList 
IsSparseList

### [VectorData<'T>.IsDenseList](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html#IsDenseList)

VectorData<'T>.IsDenseList 
IsDenseList

### [VectorData<'T>.IsSequence](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html#IsSequence)

VectorData<'T>.IsSequence 
IsSequence

### [VectorData<'T>.DenseList](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html#DenseList)

VectorData<'T>.DenseList 
DenseList

### [VectorData<'T>.SparseList](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectordata-1.html#SparseList)

### [VectorFillMissing](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorfillmissing.html)

VectorFillMissing 
 Specifies how to fill missing values in a vector (when using the
 `VectorConstruction.FillMissing` command). This can only fill missing
 values using strategy that does not require access to index keys -
 either using constant or by propagating values. 
VectorFillMissing.IsConstant 
IsConstant 
VectorFillMissing.IsDirection 
IsDirection

Direction 
VectorFillMissing.Constant

### [VectorHole](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorhole.html)

VectorHole 
 Representes a "variable" in the mini-DSL below

### [VectorListTransform](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-vectorlisttransform.html)

VectorListTransform 
 A transformation on vector(s) can specified as binary or as N-ary. A binary transformation
 can be applied to N elements using `List.reduce`, but allows optimizations. 
VectorListTransform.IsBinary

VectorListTransform.IsNary 
IsNary

VectorListTransform.Nary

### [ArrayVector<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-arrayvector-arrayvector-1.html)

ArrayVector<'T> 
 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 Vector that stores data in an array. The data is stored using the
 `ArrayVectorData<'T>` type (discriminated union)

### [ArrayVectorBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-arrayvector-arrayvectorbuilder.html)

ArrayVectorBuilder 
 Implements a builder object (`IVectorBuilder`) for creating
 vectors of type `ArrayVector<'T>`. This includes operations such as
 appending, relocating values, creating vectors from arrays etc.
 The vector builder automatically switches between the two possible
 representations of the vector - when a missing value is present, it
 uses `ArrayVectorData.VectorOptional`, otherwise it uses
 `ArrayVectorData.VectorNonOptional`. 
ArrayVectorBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
ArrayVectorBuilder.Instance 
Instance

### [ArrayVectorBuilder.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-arrayvector-arrayvectorbuilder.html#``.ctor``)

ArrayVectorBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [ArrayVectorBuilder.Instance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-arrayvector-arrayvectorbuilder.html#Instance)

ArrayVectorBuilder.Instance 
Instance 
 Provides a global access to an instance of the `ArrayVectorBuilder`

### [VirtualVectorSource](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource.html)

VirtualVectorSource 
 Module that implements various helper operations over `IVirtualVectorSource` type 
VirtualVectorSource.IBoxedVectorSource<'T> 
IBoxedVectorSource<'T> 
VirtualVectorSource.ICombinedVectorSource<'T> 
ICombinedVectorSource<'T> 
VirtualVectorSource.IMappedVectorSource<'T, 'R> 
IMappedVectorSource<'T, 'R> 
VirtualVectorSource.boxSource 
boxSource

combine

map 
VirtualVectorSource.createRowReader 
createRowReader

### [VirtualVectorSource.boxSource](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource.html#boxSource)

VirtualVectorSource.boxSource 
boxSource 
 Creates a new vector source that boxes all values
 The result also implements IBoxedVectorSource

### [VirtualVectorSource.combine](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource.html#combine)

VirtualVectorSource.combine 
combine 
 Creates a new vector that combines (zips) vectors using a given function
 The result also implements ICombinedVectorSource

### [VirtualVectorSource.map](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource.html#map)

VirtualVectorSource.map 
map 
 Creates a new vector that transforms source values using a given function
 The result also implements IMappedVectorSource

### [VirtualVectorSource.createRowReader](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource.html#createRowReader)

VirtualVectorSource.createRowReader 
createRowReader 
 Creates a "row reader vector" - that is a vector that provides source for the series
 returned by frame.Rows. This is combined from a number of sources, but we do not
 fully materialize anything to create the vector. The first parameter is a ctor
 for `VirtualVector` which is defined below in the file.

### [IBoxedVectorSource<'T>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorsource-iboxedvectorsource-1.html)

IBoxedVectorSource<'T> 
 Marks IVirtualVectorSource that has been created by boxing another source 
IBoxedVectorSource<'T>.Source 
Source

ICombinedVectorSource<'T> 
 Marks IVirtualVectorSource that has been created by combining another sources 
ICombinedVectorSource<'T>.Sources 
Sources

IMappedVectorSource<'T, 'R> 
 Marks IVirtualVectorSource that has been created by mapping from source

### [DelayedLocation](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-delayedlocation.html)

DelayedLocation 
 Represents a vector location that calculates the offset using
 address operations as needed (typically, we want to avoid this
 because it might be slow) 
DelayedLocation.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [DelayedLocation.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-delayedlocation.html#``.ctor``)

DelayedLocation.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [IVirtualVectorSource](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html)

IVirtualVectorSource 
 Non-generic part of the `IVirtualVectorSource<'V>` interface, which
 provides some basic information about the virtualized data source 
IVirtualVectorSource.Invoke 
Invoke 
IVirtualVectorSource.AddressingSchemeID 
AddressingSchemeID 
IVirtualVectorSource.ElementType 
ElementType 
IVirtualVectorSource.AddressOperations 
AddressOperations 
IVirtualVectorSource.Length 
Length

### [IVirtualVectorSource.Invoke](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html#Invoke)

IVirtualVectorSource.Invoke 
Invoke 
 Invoke a generic operation (wrapped in an interface)
 that takes a generic version of this type

### [IVirtualVectorSource.AddressingSchemeID](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html#AddressingSchemeID)

IVirtualVectorSource.AddressingSchemeID 
AddressingSchemeID 
 Identifies the addressing scheme associated with this source. This should be shared
 by all sources for which the same addresses are valid (e.g. all columns in the same table)

### [IVirtualVectorSource.ElementType](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html#ElementType)

IVirtualVectorSource.ElementType 
ElementType 
 Returns the type of elements - essentially typeof<'V> for IVirtualVectorSource<'V>

### [IVirtualVectorSource.AddressOperations](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html#AddressOperations)

IVirtualVectorSource.AddressOperations 
AddressOperations 
 Returns the addressing implementation associated with this vector source

### [IVirtualVectorSource.Length](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource.html#Length)

IVirtualVectorSource.Length 
Length 
 Returns the length of the source - by design, Big Deedle always
 needs to know the length of the source (e.g. for binary search)

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V> 
 Represents a data source for Big Deedle. The interface is used both as a representation
 of data source for `VirtualVector` (this file) and `VirtualIndex` (another file). The
 index uses `Length` and `ValueAt` to perform binary search when looking for a key; the
 vector simply provides an access to values using `ValueAt`. 
IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.GetSubVector 
GetSubVector 
IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupRange 
LookupRange 
IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupValue 
LookupValue 
IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.MergeWith 
MergeWith 
IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.ValueAt 
ValueAt

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.GetSubVector](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html#GetSubVector)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.GetSubVector 
GetSubVector 
 Returns a virtual source for the specified range (used when performing splicing on the
 frame/series, both using address or using keys - which are obtained using Lookup)

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupRange](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html#LookupRange)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupRange 
LookupRange 
 Find a range (continuous or a sequence of indices) such that all values in the range are
 the specified value. This is used, for example, when filtering frame based on column
 value (say column "PClass" has a value "1").

 If the data source has some "clever" representation of the range, it can return
 `Custom of IVectorRange` - which is then passed to `GetSubVector`.

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupValue](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html#LookupValue)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.LookupValue 
LookupValue 
 Find the address associated with the specified value. This is used by the
 index and it has the same signature as `IIndex<'K>.Lookup` (see `Index.fs`).

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.MergeWith](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html#MergeWith)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.MergeWith 
MergeWith 
 Merge the current source with a list of other sources
 (used by functions such as `Frame.merge` and `Frame.mergeAll`)

### [IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.ValueAt](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsource-1.html#ValueAt)

IVirtualVectorSource<'V>.ValueAt 
ValueAt 
 Returns the value at the specifid address. We assume that the address is in range
 [0L, Length-1L] and that each location has a value (or has a missing value, but is valid)

### [IVirtualVectorSourceOperation<'R>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsourceoperation-1.html)

IVirtualVectorSourceOperation<'R> 
 A helper type used by non-generic `IVirtualVectorSource` to invoke generic
 operations that require generic `IVirtualVectorSource<'T>` as an argument. 
IVirtualVectorSourceOperation<'R>.Invoke 
Invoke

### [IVirtualVectorSourceOperation<'R>.Invoke](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-ivirtualvectorsourceoperation-1.html#Invoke)

IVirtualVectorSourceOperation<'R>.Invoke 
Invoke 
 Generic method that is invoked by the non-generic interface with the correct `'T`

### [RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-rangesaddressoperations-1.html)

RangesAddressOperations<'TKey> 

 In BigDeedle, we often use `Ranges<'T>` to represent the address range obtained as a result
 of slicing and merging frames & series. This implements `IAddressOperations` for `Ranges<'T>`.
  
RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.Ranges 
Ranges

### [RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-rangesaddressoperations-1.html#``.ctor``)

RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.Ranges](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-rangesaddressoperations-1.html#Ranges)

RangesAddressOperations<'TKey>.Ranges 
Ranges 
 Provides access to the underlying ranges

### [VirtualAddressingScheme](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualaddressingscheme.html)

VirtualAddressingScheme 
 Represents an addressing scheme associated to virtual vectors. The addresses
 may be partitioned differently (for different data sources), so this carries
 an "id" of the data source (to make sure we don't try to mix mismatching data
 sources) 
VirtualAddressingScheme.VirtualAddressingScheme 
VirtualAddressingScheme

### [VirtualAddressingScheme.VirtualAddressingScheme](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualaddressingscheme.html#VirtualAddressingScheme)

VirtualAddressingScheme.VirtualAddressingScheme 
VirtualAddressingScheme

### [VirtualVector<'V>](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvector-1.html)

VirtualVector<'V> 
 Creates an `IVector<'T>` implementation that provides operations for accessing
 data in `IVirtualVectorSource`. This mostly just calls `ValueAt` to read data. 
VirtualVector<'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
VirtualVector<'V>.Source 
Source

### [VirtualVector<'V>.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvector-1.html#``.ctor``)

VirtualVector<'V>.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [VirtualVector<'V>.Source](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvector-1.html#Source)

VirtualVector<'V>.Source 
Source 
 Returns the wrapped source of the vector

### [VirtualVectorBuilder](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorbuilder.html)

VirtualVectorBuilder 
 Implements a builder object (`IVectorBuilder`) for creating vectors of type `VirtualVector<'T>`.
 This can do a few things without evaluating vectors (merging, slicing). For other operations
 the builder needs to materialize the vector and call `ArrayVectorBuilder`. 
VirtualVectorBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor`` 
VirtualVectorBuilder.Instance 
Instance

### [VirtualVectorBuilder.``.ctor``](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorbuilder.html#``.ctor``)

VirtualVectorBuilder.``.ctor`` 
``.ctor``

### [VirtualVectorBuilder.Instance](/Deedle/reference/deedle-vectors-virtual-virtualvectorbuilder.html#Instance)

VirtualVectorBuilder.Instance 
Instance 
 Returns an instance of VirtualVectorBuilder

### [IndexUtilsModule](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-indexutilsmodule.html)

IndexUtilsModule 
 
IndexUtilsModule.binarySearch 
binarySearch

### [IndexUtilsModule.binarySearch](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-indexutilsmodule.html#binarySearch)

IndexUtilsModule.binarySearch 
binarySearch 
<pre> Binary search in range [ 0L .. count ]. The function is generic in ^T and
 is 'inline' so that the comparison on ^T is optimized.

  - `count` specifies the upper bound for the binary search
  - `valueAt` is a function that returns value ^T at the specified location
  - `value` is the ^T value that we are looking for
  - `lookup` is the lookup semantics as used in Deedle
  - `check` is a function that tests whether we want a given location
    (if no, we scan - this can be used to find the first available value in a series)</pre>

### [IndexUtils](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-indexutils.html)

IndexUtils 
 Helpers that can be used when implementing Lookup 
IndexUtils.BinarySearch 
BinarySearch

### [IndexUtils.BinarySearch](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-indexutils.html#BinarySearch)

IndexUtils.BinarySearch 
BinarySearch 
 See the comment for `IndexUtilsModule.binarySearch`

### [Virtual](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtual.html)

Virtual 
 Provides static methods for creating virtual series and virtual frames.
 Those provide necessary wrapping around `IVirtualVectorSource` values 
Virtual.CreateFrame 
CreateFrame 
Virtual.CreateOrdinalFrame 
CreateOrdinalFrame 
Virtual.CreateOrdinalSeries 
CreateOrdinalSeries 
Virtual.CreateSeries 
CreateSeries

### [Virtual.CreateFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtual.html#CreateFrame)

Virtual.CreateFrame 
CreateFrame 
 Create a frame with ordinal index, containing the specified sources as columns.
 The index source should support lookup (which is used for series lookup, slicing etc.)
 The value source does not need to implement lookup - mainly `ValueAt`, merging and getting sub-source

### [Virtual.CreateOrdinalFrame](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtual.html#CreateOrdinalFrame)

Virtual.CreateOrdinalFrame 
CreateOrdinalFrame 
 Create a frame with ordinal index, containing the specified sources as columns.

### [Virtual.CreateOrdinalSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtual.html#CreateOrdinalSeries)

Virtual.CreateOrdinalSeries 
CreateOrdinalSeries 
 Creates a virtual series with ordinal index. The parameter is `IVirtualVectorSource`
 that specifies how to access values in the series (and is also used to determine the size
 of the series index)

### [Virtual.CreateSeries](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtual.html#CreateSeries)

Virtual.CreateSeries 
CreateSeries 
 Create a virtual series with an index and values specified by two `IVirtualVectorSource` values.
 The index source should support lookup (which is used for series lookup, slicing etc.)
 The value source does not need to implement lookup - mainly `ValueAt`, merging and getting sub-source

### [VirtualVectorHelper](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtualvectorhelper.html)

VirtualVectorHelper 

 Helper that is invoked via Reflection to create generic virtual vectors.
 
VirtualVectorHelper.Create 
Create

### [VirtualVectorHelper.Create](/Deedle/reference/deedle-virtual-virtualvectorhelper.html#Create)

VirtualVectorHelper.Create 
Create

### [HttpMethod](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html)

HttpMethod 
 The method to use in an HTTP request 
HttpMethod.Options 
Options 
HttpMethod.Get 
Get 
HttpMethod.Head 
Head 
HttpMethod.Post 
Post 
HttpMethod.Put 
Put 
HttpMethod.Delete 
Delete 
HttpMethod.Trace 
Trace 
HttpMethod.Connect 
Connect 
HttpMethod.PropFind 
PropFind 
HttpMethod.PropPatch 
PropPatch 
HttpMethod.MkCol 
MkCol 
HttpMethod.Copy 
Copy 
HttpMethod.Move 
Move 
HttpMethod.Lock 
Lock 
HttpMethod.Unlock 
Unlock 
HttpMethod.Patch 
Patch

### [HttpMethod.Options](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Options)

HttpMethod.Options 
Options 
 Request information about the communication options available on the request/response chain identified by the URI

### [HttpMethod.Get](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Get)

HttpMethod.Get 
Get 
 Retrieve whatever information (in the form of an entity) is identified by the URI

### [HttpMethod.Head](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Head)

HttpMethod.Head 
Head 
 Identical to GET except that the server MUST NOT return a message-body in the response

### [HttpMethod.Post](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Post)

HttpMethod.Post 
Post 
 Requests that the server accepts the entity enclosed in the request as a
 new subordinate of the resource identified by the Request-URI in the Request-Line

### [HttpMethod.Put](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Put)

HttpMethod.Put 
Put 
 Requests that the enclosed entity be stored under the supplied Request-URI

### [HttpMethod.Delete](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Delete)

HttpMethod.Delete 
Delete 
 Requests that the origin server deletes the resource identified by the Request-URI

### [HttpMethod.Trace](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Trace)

HttpMethod.Trace 
Trace 
 Used to invoke a remote, application-layer loop- back of the request message

### [HttpMethod.Connect](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Connect)

HttpMethod.Connect 
Connect 
 Reserved for use with a proxy that can dynamically switch to being a tunnel

### [HttpMethod.PropFind](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#PropFind)

HttpMethod.PropFind 
PropFind 
 Retrieves properties defined on the resource identified by the request URI

### [HttpMethod.PropPatch](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#PropPatch)

HttpMethod.PropPatch 
PropPatch 
 Processes instructions specified in the request body to set and/or remove properties defined on the resource identified by the request URI

### [HttpMethod.MkCol](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#MkCol)

HttpMethod.MkCol 
MkCol 
 Creates a new collection resource at the location specified by the Request URI

### [HttpMethod.Copy](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Copy)

HttpMethod.Copy 
Copy 
 Creates a duplicate of the source resource, identified by the Request-URI, in the destination resource, identified by the URI in the Destination header

### [HttpMethod.Move](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Move)

HttpMethod.Move 
Move 
 Logical equivalent of a copy, followed by consistency maintenance processing, followed by a delete of the source where all three actions are performed atomically

### [HttpMethod.Lock](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Lock)

HttpMethod.Lock 
Lock 
 Used to take out a lock of any access type on the resource identified by the request URI.

### [HttpMethod.Unlock](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Unlock)

HttpMethod.Unlock 
Unlock 
 Removes the lock identified by the lock token from the request URI, and all other resources included in the lock

### [HttpMethod.Patch](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpmethod.html#Patch)

HttpMethod.Patch 
Patch 
 Requests that the origin server applies partial modifications contained in the entity enclosed in the request to the resource identified by the request URI

### [HttpRequestHeaders](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html)

 Headers that can be sent in an HTTP request

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Accept](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Accept)

HttpRequestHeaders.Accept 
Accept 
 Content-Types that are acceptable for the response

### [HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptCharset](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#AcceptCharset)

HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptCharset 
AcceptCharset 
 Character sets that are acceptable

### [HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptDatetime](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#AcceptDatetime)

HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptDatetime 
AcceptDatetime 
 Acceptable version in time

### [HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#AcceptEncoding)

HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptEncoding 
AcceptEncoding 
 List of acceptable encodings. See HTTP compression.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptLanguage](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#AcceptLanguage)

HttpRequestHeaders.AcceptLanguage 
AcceptLanguage 
 List of acceptable human languages for response

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Allow](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Allow)

HttpRequestHeaders.Allow 
Allow 
 The Allow header, which specifies the set of HTTP methods supported.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Authorization](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Authorization)

HttpRequestHeaders.Authorization 
Authorization 
 Authentication credentials for HTTP authentication

### [HttpRequestHeaders.BasicAuth](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#BasicAuth)

HttpRequestHeaders.BasicAuth 
BasicAuth 
 Authentication header using Basic Auth encoding

### [HttpRequestHeaders.CacheControl](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#CacheControl)

HttpRequestHeaders.CacheControl 
CacheControl 
 Used to specify directives that MUST be obeyed by all caching mechanisms along the request/response chain

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Connection](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Connection)

HttpRequestHeaders.Connection 
Connection 
 What type of connection the user-agent would prefer

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentDisposition](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentDisposition)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentDisposition 
ContentDisposition 
 Describes the placement of the content. Valid dispositions are: inline, attachment, form-data

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentEncoding)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentEncoding 
ContentEncoding 
 The type of encoding used on the data

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentLanguage](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentLanguage)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentLanguage 
ContentLanguage 
 The language the content is in

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentLocation](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentLocation)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentLocation 
ContentLocation 
 An alternate location for the returned data

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentMD5](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentMD5)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentMD5 
ContentMD5 
 A Base64-encoded binary MD5 sum of the content of the request body

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentRange](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentRange)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentRange 
ContentRange 
 Where in a full body message this partial message belongs

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentType](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentType)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentType 
ContentType 
 The MIME type of the body of the request (used with POST and PUT requests)

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ContentTypeWithEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ContentTypeWithEncoding)

HttpRequestHeaders.ContentTypeWithEncoding 
ContentTypeWithEncoding 
 The MIME type of the body of the request (used with POST and PUT requests) with an explicit encoding

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Date](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Date)

HttpRequestHeaders.Date 
Date 
 The date and time that the message was sent

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Expect](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Expect)

HttpRequestHeaders.Expect 
Expect 
 Indicates that particular server behaviors are required by the client

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Expires](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Expires)

HttpRequestHeaders.Expires 
Expires 
 Gives the date/time after which the response is considered stale

### [HttpRequestHeaders.From](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#From)

HttpRequestHeaders.From 
From 
 The email address of the user making the request

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Host](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Host)

HttpRequestHeaders.Host 
Host 
 The domain name of the server (for virtual hosting), and the TCP port number on which the server is listening.
 The port number may be omitted if the port is the standard port for the service requested.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.IfMatch](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#IfMatch)

HttpRequestHeaders.IfMatch 
IfMatch 
 Only perform the action if the client supplied entity matches the same entity on the server.
 This is mainly for methods like PUT to only update a resource if it has not been modified since the user last updated it. If-Match: "737060cd8c284d8af7ad3082f209582d" Permanent

### [HttpRequestHeaders.IfModifiedSince](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#IfModifiedSince)

HttpRequestHeaders.IfModifiedSince 
IfModifiedSince 
 Allows a 304 Not Modified to be returned if content is unchanged

### [HttpRequestHeaders.IfNoneMatch](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#IfNoneMatch)

HttpRequestHeaders.IfNoneMatch 
IfNoneMatch 
 Allows a 304 Not Modified to be returned if content is unchanged

### [HttpRequestHeaders.IfRange](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#IfRange)

HttpRequestHeaders.IfRange 
IfRange 
 If the entity is unchanged, send me the part(s) that I am missing; otherwise, send me the entire new entity

### [HttpRequestHeaders.IfUnmodifiedSince](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#IfUnmodifiedSince)

HttpRequestHeaders.IfUnmodifiedSince 
IfUnmodifiedSince 
 Only send the response if the entity has not been modified since a specific time

### [HttpRequestHeaders.KeepAlive](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#KeepAlive)

HttpRequestHeaders.KeepAlive 
KeepAlive 
 Specifies a parameter used into order to maintain a persistent connection

### [HttpRequestHeaders.LastModified](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#LastModified)

HttpRequestHeaders.LastModified 
LastModified 
 Specifies the date and time at which the accompanying body data was last modified

### [HttpRequestHeaders.MaxForwards](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#MaxForwards)

HttpRequestHeaders.MaxForwards 
MaxForwards 
 Limit the number of times the message can be forwarded through proxies or gateways

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Origin](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Origin)

HttpRequestHeaders.Origin 
Origin 
 Initiates a request for cross-origin resource sharing (asks server for an 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' response header)

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Pragma](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Pragma)

HttpRequestHeaders.Pragma 
Pragma 
 Implementation-specific headers that may have various effects anywhere along the request-response chain.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Prefer](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Prefer)

HttpRequestHeaders.Prefer 
Prefer 
 Optional instructions to the server to control request processing. See RFC https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7240 for more details

### [HttpRequestHeaders.ProxyAuthorization](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#ProxyAuthorization)

HttpRequestHeaders.ProxyAuthorization 
ProxyAuthorization 
 Authorization credentials for connecting to a proxy.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Range](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Range)

HttpRequestHeaders.Range 
Range 
 Request only part of an entity. Bytes are numbered from 0

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Referer](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Referer)

HttpRequestHeaders.Referer 
Referer 
 This is the address of the previous web page from which a link to the currently requested page was followed. (The word "referrer" is misspelled in the RFC as well as in most implementations.)

### [HttpRequestHeaders.TE](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#TE)

HttpRequestHeaders.TE 
TE 
 The transfer encodings the user agent is willing to accept: the same values as for the response header
 Transfer-Encoding can be used, plus the "trailers" value (related to the "chunked" transfer method) to
 notify the server it expects to receive additional headers (the trailers) after the last, zero-sized, chunk.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Trailer](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Trailer)

HttpRequestHeaders.Trailer 
Trailer 
 The Trailer general field value indicates that the given set of header fields is present in the trailer of a message encoded with chunked transfer-coding

### [HttpRequestHeaders.TransferEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#TransferEncoding)

HttpRequestHeaders.TransferEncoding 
TransferEncoding 
 The TransferEncoding header indicates the form of encoding used to safely transfer the entity to the user.  The valid directives are one of: chunked, compress, deflate, gzip, or identity.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Translate](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Translate)

HttpRequestHeaders.Translate 
Translate 
 Microsoft extension to the HTTP specification used in conjunction with WebDAV functionality.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Upgrade](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Upgrade)

HttpRequestHeaders.Upgrade 
Upgrade 
 Specifies additional communications protocols that the client supports.

### [HttpRequestHeaders.UserAgent](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#UserAgent)

HttpRequestHeaders.UserAgent 
UserAgent 
 The user agent string of the user agent

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Via](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Via)

HttpRequestHeaders.Via 
Via 
 Informs the server of proxies through which the request was sent

### [HttpRequestHeaders.Warning](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#Warning)

HttpRequestHeaders.Warning 
Warning 
 A general warning about possible problems with the entity body

### [HttpRequestHeaders.XHTTPMethodOverride](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httprequestheaders.html#XHTTPMethodOverride)

HttpRequestHeaders.XHTTPMethodOverride 
XHTTPMethodOverride 
 Override HTTP method.

### [HttpResponseHeaders](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html)

HttpResponseHeaders 
 Headers that can be received in an HTTP response 
HttpResponseHeaders.AccessControlAllowOrigin

StrictTransportSecurity

HttpResponseHeaders.WWWAuthenticate 
WWWAuthenticate

### [HttpResponseHeaders.AccessControlAllowOrigin](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#AccessControlAllowOrigin)

HttpResponseHeaders.AccessControlAllowOrigin 
AccessControlAllowOrigin 
 Specifying which web sites can participate in cross-origin resource sharing

### [HttpResponseHeaders.AcceptRanges](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#AcceptRanges)

HttpResponseHeaders.AcceptRanges 
AcceptRanges 
 What partial content range types this server supports

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Age](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Age)

HttpResponseHeaders.Age 
Age 
 The age the object has been in a proxy cache in seconds

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Allow](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Allow)

HttpResponseHeaders.Allow 
Allow 
 Valid actions for a specified resource. To be used for a 405 Method not allowed

### [HttpResponseHeaders.CacheControl](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#CacheControl)

HttpResponseHeaders.CacheControl 
CacheControl 
 Tells all caching mechanisms from server to client whether they may cache this object. It is measured in seconds

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Connection](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Connection)

HttpResponseHeaders.Connection 
Connection 
 Options that are desired for the connection

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentEncoding)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentEncoding 
ContentEncoding 
 The type of encoding used on the data. See HTTP compression.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLanguage](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentLanguage)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLanguage 
ContentLanguage 
 The language the content is in

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLength](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentLength)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLength 
ContentLength 
 The length of the response body in octets (8-bit bytes)

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLocation](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentLocation)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentLocation 
ContentLocation 
 An alternate location for the returned data

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentMD5](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentMD5)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentMD5 
ContentMD5 
 A Base64-encoded binary MD5 sum of the content of the response

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentDisposition](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentDisposition)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentDisposition 
ContentDisposition 
 An opportunity to raise a "File Download" dialogue box for a known MIME type with binary format or suggest a filename for dynamic content. Quotes are necessary with special characters.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentRange](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentRange)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentRange 
ContentRange 
 Where in a full body message this partial message belongs

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ContentType](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ContentType)

HttpResponseHeaders.ContentType 
ContentType 
 The MIME type of this content

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Date](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Date)

HttpResponseHeaders.Date 
Date 
 The date and time that the message was sent (in "HTTP-date" format as defined by RFC 2616)

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ETag](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ETag)

HttpResponseHeaders.ETag 
ETag 
 An identifier for a specific version of a resource, often a message digest

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Expires](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Expires)

HttpResponseHeaders.Expires 
Expires 
 Gives the date/time after which the response is considered stale

### [HttpResponseHeaders.LastModified](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#LastModified)

HttpResponseHeaders.LastModified 
LastModified 
 The last modified date for the requested object

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Link](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Link)

HttpResponseHeaders.Link 
Link 
 Used to express a typed relationship with another resource, where the relation type is defined by RFC 5988

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Location](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Location)

HttpResponseHeaders.Location 
Location 
 Used in redirection, or when a new resource has been created.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.P3P](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#P3P)

HttpResponseHeaders.P3P 
P3P 
 This header is supposed to set P3P policy

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Pragma](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Pragma)

HttpResponseHeaders.Pragma 
Pragma 
 Implementation-specific headers that may have various effects anywhere along the request-response chain.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.ProxyAuthenticate](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#ProxyAuthenticate)

HttpResponseHeaders.ProxyAuthenticate 
ProxyAuthenticate 
 Request authentication to access the proxy.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Refresh](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Refresh)

HttpResponseHeaders.Refresh 
Refresh 
 Used in redirection, or when a new resource has been created. This refresh redirects after 5 seconds.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.RetryAfter](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#RetryAfter)

HttpResponseHeaders.RetryAfter 
RetryAfter 
 If an entity is temporarily unavailable, this instructs the client to try again later. Value could be a specified period of time (in seconds) or a HTTP-date.[28]

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Server](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Server)

HttpResponseHeaders.Server 
Server 
 A name for the server

### [HttpResponseHeaders.SetCookie](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#SetCookie)

HttpResponseHeaders.SetCookie 
SetCookie 
 An HTTP cookie

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Status](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Status)

HttpResponseHeaders.Status 
Status 
 The HTTP status of the response

### [HttpResponseHeaders.StrictTransportSecurity](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#StrictTransportSecurity)

HttpResponseHeaders.StrictTransportSecurity 
StrictTransportSecurity 
 A HSTS Policy informing the HTTP client how long to cache the HTTPS only policy and whether this applies to subdomains.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Trailer](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Trailer)

HttpResponseHeaders.Trailer 
Trailer 
 The Trailer general field value indicates that the given set of header fields is present in the trailer of a message encoded with chunked transfer-coding.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.TransferEncoding](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#TransferEncoding)

HttpResponseHeaders.TransferEncoding 
TransferEncoding 
 The form of encoding used to safely transfer the entity to the user. Currently defined methods are: chunked, compress, deflate, gzip, identity.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Vary](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Vary)

HttpResponseHeaders.Vary 
Vary 
 Tells downstream proxies how to match future request headers to decide whether the cached response can be used rather than requesting a fresh one from the origin server.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Via](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Via)

HttpResponseHeaders.Via 
Via 
 Informs the client of proxies through which the response was sent.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.Warning](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#Warning)

HttpResponseHeaders.Warning 
Warning 
 A general warning about possible problems with the entity body.

### [HttpResponseHeaders.WWWAuthenticate](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpresponseheaders.html#WWWAuthenticate)

HttpResponseHeaders.WWWAuthenticate 
WWWAuthenticate 
 Indicates the authentication scheme that should be used to access the requested entity.

### [HttpStatusCodes](/Deedle/reference/fsharp-data-httpstatuscodes.html)

HttpStatusCodes 
 Status codes that can be received in an HTTP response 
HttpStatusCodes.Continue 
Continue 
HttpStatusCodes.SwitchingProtocols 
SwitchingProtocols 
HttpStatusCodes.Processing 
Processing 
HttpStatusCodes.EarlyHints 
EarlyHints 
HttpStatusCodes.OK 
OK 
HttpStatusCodes.Created 
Created 
HttpStatusCodes.Accepted 
Accepted 
HttpStatusCodes.NonAuthoritativeInformation 
NonAuthoritativeInformation 
HttpStatusCodes.NoContent 
NoContent 
HttpStatusCodes.ResetContent 
ResetContent 
HttpStatusCodes.PartialContent 
PartialContent 
HttpStatusCodes.MultiStatus 
MultiStatus 
HttpStatusCodes.AlreadyReported 
AlreadyReported 
HttpStatusCodes.IMUsed 
IMUsed 
HttpStatusCodes.MultipleChoices 
MultipleChoices 
HttpStatusCodes.MovedPermanently

Forbidden 
HttpStatusCodes.NotFound

HttpStatusCodes.MethodNotAllowed

NotAcceptable

ProxyAuthenticationRequired

HTTPVersionNotSupported 
HttpStatusCodes.VariantAlsoNegotiates
